stem_zh: 1. External attack on or failure of critical technology An external attack or failure of critical technology would likely result in system unavailability for customers and/or staff.Staff would not be able to access key operational and financial systems which would likely bring all operations to a halt.Similarly,attacks on or failure of customer-based systems could result in lost bookings or an inability for customers to print out vital travel/flight documentation. External attacks or systems failures would present potentially severe operational disruption to the business, resulting in damage to our reputaton and loss of customer confidence. In the most severe case,an attack on in-flight control systems or air traffic control systems could have fatal consequences for customers and crew. Malicious external attacks are likely to result in the loss of critical business and/or personal customer data. There is likely to be an adverse reputational impact if customer data is lost or stolen.Legal and compensation costs could also be significant.

2. Significant safety/security event A significant safety or security breach could be catastrophic for Corjetz.Significant injury or in the worst-case scenario,loss of life due to an air accident or a security incident would have major consequences for the business. - There would inevitably be sustained adverse media coverage and we would likely lose customer confidence. - This would lead to a reduction in future revenue which would have an adverse impact on our share price,and we would also likely lose investor confidence. - It is likely that we would face some form of fine or penalty which is likely to be extremely costly. - Inevitably,we would face severe operational disruption in the aftermath of any safety or security incident which would impact on our revenues and profit. - A significant security breach could result in overallbusiness failure. 3.Climate change and eco-policies and regulations - Climate change,causing more adverse weather conditions,is likely to result in adverse customer experience. This will be due to disruption and delays and potentially could result in injury to customers. - There is also likely to be operational disruption due to airspace and runway closures. - In extreme cases,we may incur aircraft damage in extreme storm or difficult landing conditions.This could be extremely costly. In terms of eco-policies,we may face significant increases in aviation taxes/levies from local governments in countries where we fly. Legislation to cut aircraft global CO₂emissions may constrain growth and capacity,resulting in pressure on margins as we seek to find alternative ways to deliver our operations in an environmentally friendly way. - Political changes and priorities in different countries will make it challenging to keep up to date with the increasing global awareness of climate change.If we fail to keep up,then we may lose competitive advantage or face a customer backlash. 3 (a)To:Chief finance officer From:Senior business analyst Date:September 2023 Performance evaluation year end (y/e)30 June 20X3 Introduction The following report will ssess the provisional performance of Corjetz in the latest financial year.This will include an assessment of a range of both financial and non-financial performance measures. Appendix 1 y/e 30 June y/e 30 June Increase/(decrease) (%) Favourable(F)/ adverse(A) Headline financial results($millions) 20X3 20X2 Revenue 5,747 5,308 8 ·3 F Costs(excluding fuel) 4,088 3,722 9 ·8 A Fuel costs 1,274 1,063 19 ·8 A Profit before tax 385 523 -26 ·4 A Summary statistics 20X3 20X2 Seats flown(millions) 94 ·5 85 ·7 10 ·3 F Passengers(millions) 86 ·5 79 ·7 8 ·5 F Number of routes operated 978 912 7 ·2 F Revenue per passenger($) 66 ·4 66 ·6 -0 ·3 A Total cost per passenger($) 62 ·0 60 ·0 3 ·3 A KPls 20X3 20X2 Available seat kilometres(millions)(ASK) 104,328 96,280 8 ·4 F Revenue per available seat kilometre($)(RASK) 0 ·06 0 ·06 一 Load factor(%) 91-5 93 ·0 -1 ·50 A Return on capital employed(%) 9 ·3 13 ·0 -3 ·7 A Customer satisfaction(%) 77 ·0 78 ·0 -1 ·0 A Punctuality(%) 76 ·0 79 ·0 -3 ·0 A Staff turnover(%) 5 ·2 5 ·9 -0 ·7 F CO₂per passenger(grams) 82 ·0 88 ·0 -6 ·8 F Evaluation of 20X3 performance Headline financial results Revenues have increased by 8·3%which is positive considering the highly competitive environment in which we operate.If we look deeper,we can see that this has been driven by increased passenger numbers,which is also a positive indicator for thee business.Increasing passenger numbers each year is an important aspect of our business and a key determinant of success. However,revenue per passenger has slightly decreased (0·3%),meaning our seat prices are not moving up in line with costs. This is further evidenced by the cost per passenger which has risen by 3·3%.This is likely due to the increased competitive challenges we face and the fact that our customers are very price sensitive.Our strategic position and a key determinant of our competitive success is a low cost/low price operator and increasing prices is likely to result in a loss of customers and the loss of our competitive position.We have increased passenger numbers which is highly positive.But increasingly,competitors are likely to be forcing us to discount our prices further to maintain our position. Total costs have increased at a greater rate than our revenues in the year.Costs excluding fuel increased by nearly 10%,these are likely to be partly driven by higher staff costs from flying more routes (7·2%),which inevitably willresult in higher costs for the business in operating these flights both in the air and on the ground.We know that airport charges are rising,as airports are increasingly facing capacity challenges and this will continue to impact on our business in the future,as growth is likely to be limited by the airport capacity available to us. Fuel costs also increased by nearly 20%in the year.We have operated more routes in 20X3,which will inevitably impact on fuel costs.However,we will need to investigate this further as fuel is a considerable proportion of Corjetz's costs and therefore any further increase in fuel prices could impact significantly on profit.We must re-evaluate our fuel purchasing policy and our currency hedging activities to protect against fuel price fluctuations in the major countries where we fly and purchase fuel. As a result of the above,we have seen profit before tax fall by 26·4%.This is a significant reduction,and we will need to take a wider view of these results in the context of the whole industry.The airline industry is driven by many external factors, particularly current political agendas,economic cycles and consumer trends and we would need to look at the wider picture of the whole industry before drawing conclusions about our headline performance.However,shareholders are likely to be concerned with falling profits.A starting point will be to ensure that we thoroughly review our pricing strategy and our cost management and control activities throughout the whole business and review the profitability of each route which we offer. KPls and summary statistics Although we flew more seats in 20X3,not allof these were revenue generating,as passenger numbers did not increase at the same rate.Therefore,a significant number of the seats flown were empty(8 million in 20X3(94·5-86·5)compared to 6 million in 20X2(85·7-79·7).This has resulted in an adverse change in passenger load factor of 1·5%.This could be due to new routes not yet attracting customers at the same level as our established and most popular destinations.Therefore,we may need to evaluate new routes in more detail to establish their load factor and consider how we can improve passenger load through increased advertising or alternative pricing strategies. Return on capital employed has fallen from 13%to 9·3%.This has been in part due to our reduced profit for the year and may also have been caused by greater investment in capital employed.I do not have any information on this,but it could be assumed that if we are delivering 7·2%more routes,then we will have needed to employ more aircraft to achieve this. A concerning feature of performance in 20X3 is the reduction in customer satisfaction levels.Although not a significant percentage drop,it is sill concerning,as customer performance hitan all-time high last year and therefore it is disappointing that we have not been able to sustain this or even improve on it.This reduction in customer satisfaction could be driven by the reduction in punctuality which dropped 3%.This could be driven by the increasingly congested airports and the increasing demand for airspace,meaning that delays are becoming more frequent.However,customer satisfaction is a complex issue and may be caused by several factors,including in-flight service levels,accessibility and usability of our online booking systems and airport experience.Therefore,we must investigate all these areas to establish where we need to improve to increase our customer satisfaction levels. We appear to be performing well in terms of CO₂emissions,with emissions per passenger reducing 6·8%.This is likely to be increasingly driven by the fuel-efficient airplanes which we invest in and is a positive indicator of our commitment to our environmental and sustainability commitment. Another positive area of performance is staff turnover,which has reduced from 5·9%to 5·2%of staff in the year.Again, this supports our continued commitment to improve employee engagement through training and development as part of our environmental and sustainability commitment. Summary Performance in 20X3,both financial and non-financial,has been disappointing overall.AIthough we have flown more passengers,this has not transferred into more profit for the business,as costs have increased at a greater rate than revenues. This will inevitably disappoint our shareholders who will be concerned that costs are not being managed effectively. Although Corjetz flew more passengers across more routes,these are clearly not yet converting into profit.However,it may be too early to assess the viability of these additional routes,as it may take more time to become established and attract more passengers and therefore increase load factor. It is of concern that our customer satisfaction has reduced,and this is an area we must prioritise.Our customers are highly price sensitive and retaining loyalty and repeat business is key to our business model. There have been some positives in performance,notably that passenger numbers did increase which means that we are attracting increasing numbers of customers to our flights and destinations.Also,our CO₂emissions have significantly reduced per passenger which is a positive indicator of our work towards reducing our impact on the environment. (b)Briefing paper To:Chief finance officer From:Senior business analyst Date:September 20X3 Environmental and sustainability audit report content Overview An environmental and sustainability audit reportshould provide Corjetz'skey stakeholders with an assurance of the environmental and sustainability management policies,processes and activities which are undertaken by the organisation.Such a report should primarily focus on the four key principles outined in Corjetz's environmental and sustainability dashboard.However, it should also consider the overall commitment of leadership to environmental and sustainability activities and a review of thee organisation's commitment to stakeholder engagement regarding these priorities. Key content 1.Review of overall leadership/commitment to the sustainability The audit report should begin with an assessment of Corjetz's leadership with regard to its environmental and sustainability priorities.The audit report will assess the policies and procedures put in place by senior management to maintain the overall focus of sustainability practices throughout the business.The audit report should evaluate the overall level of commitment by assessing cultural focus on sustainability. The report should contain a review of evidence that there is a commitment to promoting a culture which is aware of environmental and sustainability issues.The dashboard itself provides evidence of leadership focus and commitment on sustainability and the environment.

2. Review of stakeholder engagement The audit report should identify and assess evidence of wider stakeholder involvement in environmental and sustainability activities.It should report on any relevant committees or meetings relating to the key priorities of environmental and sustainability issues (such as any reports and meeting held by the safety committee).It will also report on policies and interactions with external stakeholders such as aircraft suppliers and external contractors regarding their actions and responsibilities for maintaining environmental and sustainability standards.The report willcontain evidence of any actions taken and implemented from stakeholder engagement activities (such as training and development for staff in environmental awareness). 3.Audit assessment and findings of key priorities Climate responsibility The report should consider the overall attitudes of leadership and management to climate responsibility.The audit report should focus on the controls implemented to manage and minimise carbon emissions,energy usage,waste management and noise reduction.The report would include an assessment of the objectives and targets set by the business in these key activities and the achievement of these. Safety The audit report should include an assessment of the key health and safety management procedures and controls put in place.This will include an evaluation and assessment of the adequacy of training policies implemented and assess any potential link with training and levels of health and safety incidents in the year.Security protocols will be assessed against industry standards and reported.Health and safety controls should be of particular interest to auditors,and they should report on key incidents and potential reasons and recommendations for improvement. Customer and supplier relationships The audit report should contain an assessment of the systems and controls in place to manage customer relationships. Evidence should be presented of systems to deal with customer complaints and an evaluation of the effectiveness of these systems.Interviews with customers and customer feedback response analysis may provide some explanation of this evidence. Supplier selection and management policies need to be assessed and reported.Responsibilities and procedures for supplier selection and monitoring should be reported,as should results of any KPls set for key suppliers.Evidence of ethical supplier selection and management policy statements should be reviewed and reported.Evidence of actions taken to prevent bribery or fraud should be included. Employee relationships The audit report should contain evidence that the employee management process,from selection through to termination, is operating effectively and objectively.In particular,the report should focus on the level and adequacy of training for specific job roles.The outcome of employee engagement surveys should also be assessed and evaluated and any recommended actions from this feedback should have been actioned. Summary An annual environmental and sustainability audit report should focus on reassuring stakeholders that Corjetz's leadership is taking positive actions and is maintaining a positive culture which promotes achievement and development of its environmental and sustainability priorities.The focus should be on the key priorities but should also reassure stakeholders that the overall culture of the business is driving towards sustainability. Beago ' s KPls 13 September 2024 1 1.Introduction Beago is a transport network company(TNC)operating in the country of Bealand,which has a population of over 200 million people.Beago was established 12 years ago and has since grown to become the second largest TNC business in Bealand (based on revenue and customer numbers). 2.TNC industry information Overview A TNC is a business which provides on-demand transportation services to customers through a website or a mobile app-based platform.Unlike traditional taxicab hire businesses,TNCs do not maintain their own fleets of vehicles or employ drivers directly. Instead,individual drivers of cars work as independent contractors for TNCs. Customers request a ride through the TNC platform and are then connected to a contracted driver who is also connected to the platform.For the purpose of this pre-seen and the case study exam,the term TNC will be used to define businesses which offer the range of services outlined on pages 3 and 4. Bealand has awell-developed economy and nearly 80%of its population live and work in its urban areas.Its public transport facilities,including buses and trains,have come under increasing pressure in recent years due to a rapidly growing urban population,leading to overcrowding and unreliability.Bealand's government is therefore supportive of innovative ways to address the transport challenges facing the country's many cities.TNCs have proved to be popular in the cities as they offer a convenient and reasonably priced alternative to public transport.The government of Bealand sees TNCs as an alternative to people owning and using their own cars. Bealand market Zenbi is Beago's main competitor and was the first TNC business established in Bealand,14 years ago.Zenbi is currently the largest TNC operating in Bealand,and is also the world's second largest TNC,currently operating in over 50 countries. Beago was established two years after Zenbi and over the years has gradually obtained a significant proportion of Zenbi's market share.Beago only operates in Bealand, offering its services in 250 cities across the country.Currently, the local authority of each city sets its own criteria for allowing operating licences to TNCs and their drivers. Several TNC businesses have been attracted to the Bealand market in the last decade but most of these currently only operate within a limited number of cities.At the end of 20X3,together, Zenbi and Beago held 85%of the TNC market in Bealand. Customers registered with TNCs in Bealand have doubled in the last eight years.By the end of 20X3,25%of Bealand's population were active users of TNC services. September 2024 2 批注[1] Beago 是一家在Bealand 运营的运输网络公司 (T NC),Bealand 拥有2亿多人口。 Beago 成立于12年前， 现已发展成为Beeland 第二大 TNC 公司(基于收入和客户 数 量 ) 。 批注[2]: TNC 是一家通过网站或基于移动应用程序的平台， 它为客户提供即时运输服务。与传统的出租车租赁业务不 同 ，TNC 没有自己的车队，也不直接雇佣司机。相反，个 人司机作为TNC 的独立承包商工作。客户通过TNC 平台请 求搭车，然后与一名签约司机连接，该司机也与该平台连 接。为了这个预看材料和案例考试的目的，TNC 被用作来 定义这个行业，所提供的服务在第3页和第4页。 批注[3]: Bealand 经济发达，近80%的人口生活和工作在 城市地区。近年来，由于城市人口的快速增长，包括公共 汽车和火车在内的公共交通设施承受着越来越大的压力， 导致过度拥挤和不可靠。因此， Bealand 政府支持以创新 的方式解决该国许多城市面临的交通问题。事实证明， TNC 在城市中很受欢迎，因为它们提供了一种方便和价格 合理的替代公共交通的工具。 Bealand 政府将TNC 视为人 们拥有和使用自己汽车的另一种选择。 批注[4] Zenbi 是Beago 的主要竞争对手，是14年前在 Beago 成立的第一家TNC。Zenbi 是目前在Bealand 最大的 TNC, 也是世界第二大TNC, 目前在50多个国家开展业务 批注[5]: Beago 成立比Zenbi晚两年，多年来逐渐获得了 Zenbi 相当大比例的市场份额。 Beago 只 在Bealand 运营， 在全国250个城市提供服务。目前，每个城市的地方当局 都制定了自己的标准，以便向TNC 及其司机发放经营许可 证 。 批注[6]:在过去十年中， 一些TNC 企业被吸引到Bealand 市场，但其中大多数目前只在有限的几个城市开展业务。 截至20X3年底，Zenbi 和Beago 共占据Bealand 85%的 TNC 市场。 批注[7]:在Bealand 的TNC 注册的客户在过去八年中翻了 一番。到20X3年底，25%的Bealand 人是TNC 服务的活跃 用户。 批注[8]:然而，客户的竞争来自传统出租车和公共交通， 包括火车和公共汽车。批注[9]:左图： Bealand 国家TNC 市场份额 右图：Bealand 国家TNC 用户增长However,competition for customers also comes from traditional taxicabs and from public transport,including trains and buses. 批注[8]:然而，客户的竞争来自传统出租车和公共交通， 包括火车和公共汽车。 批注[9]:左图： Bealand 国家TNC 市场份额 右图：Bealand 国家TNC 用户增长 TNC services ServiceRide sharingDescription Service Ride sharing The term ride sharing refers to sharing a ride with other passengers.A shared ride allows multiple and unrelated customers with pickup and drop-off locations along the same or similar route to share a vehicle for that journey,usually at a discounted price. Both Zenbi and Beago were initially established to offer a ride sharing service,enabling drivers and customers to connect and share a ride.Ride sharing aims to reduce the number of single occupancy rides within cities, reducing congestion and impacting positively on the environment.The cost of the ride is also shared between each customer,making it a cost-effective transportation option.Customers must specifically request a shared ride on a TNC's platform to use this service. Ride hailingThe term ride hailing refers to when a customer requests a driver to take them to exactly where they need to go.This can be achieved by virtually 'hailing'a ride from a TNC business using their app or website.Using a traditional taxicab business,this would be achieved by hailing a taxicab from the street or making a phone call to book one. Ride hailing Micro mobilityRide hailing is the most popula servce offered by most TNCs,including Zenbi and Beago.These rides can be on-demand or scheduled in advance. Micro mobility refers to a network of shared bicycles,which can be rented by customers looking for transportation options which are more active,lower priced,more environmentally friendly,and often more efficient for shorterr trips during heavy traffic.Bicycles are located at fixed docking stations in key locations around a city.The docking station contains a terminal to release the bicycle. Micro mobility Customers use the TNC's platform to book and pay for the bicycle rental.On completion of the booking,customers are provided with a code to unlock the device from the docking station.The bicycle can then be returned to any docking station at the end of the hire period.Micro mobility options are often used by customers to complete the last part of their journey,such as a bicycle from a bus or train station to their place of work. Business services Business services are offered by TNCs to a range of businesses,to assist their employees to commute effectively and to travel to events and meetings.Also,businesses use TNCs to provide courtesy transport for their customers/clients.Business service solutions help client businesses to reduce costs,save time,and streamline their own ground transportation activities. TNC customers TNC customers use TNCs to travel from one point to another,such as commuting to and from work,exploring cities and to shop at local businesses. -To become a customer,an online account must be created on a TNC's website or mobile 'Customer app'. -The customer must enter their personal details (name,address and email).They must also input credit or debit card details,as all payments to TNCs are made electronically through the TNC platform. - Once registered,customers can choose from the range of services offered by the TNC. -To book a ride hail or a ride share,the customer must enter their required pick-up location (or use the global positioning system(GPS)on their phone)and a destination. -A price for the ride is quoted at the point of booking which the customer must confirm to accept the ride.A notification of the ride request s then automatically sent to the TNC's 'Driver app'. - Once a driver has confirmed acceptance of the ride,the Customer app notifies the customer of the driver profile,the vehicle model,and the vehicle registration number,which allow the customer to check driver details before getting into the vehicle. -The Customer app tracks the arrival of the driver on the in-app map,so that the customer can check progress of the driver's arrival.At the end of the ride,payment is automatically deducted from the customer's bank/credit card TNC drivers - These background checks are normally carried out by a third-party expert provider. - Once verified,a driver can accept a requested ride (by clicking 'Accept'in the Driver app). - Most TNCs require drivers to have a vehicle which meets specifications relating to the age and condition of the vehicle,which must be fully insured.However,drivers do not need to own their own vehicles.Many TNC drivers use leased vehicles to drive for TNCs and some TNCs offer leasing facilities to their drivers. - Drivers do not set their own prices.Each ride price is calculated by the TNC and is based on a standard rate fare plus a charge per kilometre.TNCs pay each driver between 75%and 85%of the ride price for each ride completed. In Bealand,drivers working for TNCs are recognised as independent contractors and not employees of the TNC.This allows drivers to choose their own working hours.This flexibility attracts a wide range of drivers,including students,retired people and part-time workers. Industry challenges TNC operations are subject to global economic conditions,including the resulting impact on spending by customers.Slow economic growth,recession or increases in fuel costs can decrease consumer spending power or confidence,which hinder TNC operations,resulting in lower revenues and earnings. Growth of TNC business depends on the ability to attract and retain qualified drivers who satisfy the required screening criteria and increase available drivers'operating hours.f TNCs do not September 2024 批注[30]:驾驶员还必须上传最近的照片，以便客户在被 接载时识别他们。在所有必要的背景调查得到验证之前， 驾驶员无权驾驶，包括犯罪记录、驾驶违规和医疗健康检 查。这些背景调查通常由第三方专家提供商执行。 批注[31]:一旦验证，驾驶员可以通过点击在驾驶员应用 中的“接受”来接受乘客请求的乘车。 批注[32]:大多数TNC 要求驾驶员拥有符合年龄和条件规 格的车辆，该车辆必须完全投保。但是，驾驶员不需要拥 有自己的车辆。许多TNC 驾驶员使用租赁车辆为TNC 开车 , 一 些TNC 为驾驶员提供租赁设施。 批注[33]:驾驶员不能自己设定价格。每次乘车的价格由 TNC 计算，基于标准费率加上每公里收费。 TNC 为每次完 成的乘车支付给每位驾驶员75%至85%的乘车价格。 批注[34]:在Bealand, 为TNC 工作的驾驶员被视为独立承 包商，而不是TNC 的雇员。这使得驾驶员可以选择自己的 工作时间。这种灵活性吸引了一大群驾驶员，包括学生、 退休人员和兼职工人。 批注[35]:叫车和拼车流程 注册客户登录到客户应用程序或网站并请求乘车。 →在应用或网站上给出乘车的价格报价，客户确认接受。 →乘车请求立即发送至司机的应用程序。 一位注册司机通 过司机应用程序接受乘车请求，并被分配该次乘车任务。 → 当司机接受乘车请求时，客户会通过客户应用程序收到 司机详情和预计到达接客地点的时间的通知。 →客户可以使用客户应用程序跟踪司机的进度，并且还可 以查看所要求乘车的路线分配。 → 司机到达指定的接送地点，并检查顾客姓名和目的地位 置。顾客核对车辆和司机详情。 →一旦顾客和司机的详细信息都已核实，司机就会接乘客 并开车前往所要求的目的地。 →乘车完成后，自动从客户的信用卡或借记卡中扣款。 →然后，顾客需要完成对司机评分分数的填写。 批注[36]:行业挑战 TNC 运营受到全球经济状况的影响，包括对消费者支出产 生的影响。经济增长缓慢、经济衰退或燃料成本上涨可能 会降低消费者的购买力或信心，阻碍TNC 运营，导致收入 和利润下降。 provide drivers with flexibility and opportunities to earn sufficient income,they will fail to attract new drivers and/or retain current drivers resulting in complaints and negative publicity.This will inevitably adversely affect customers and business clients.As urbanisation increases and car ownership decreases,maintaining and growing the necessary pool of drivers will be a challenge for TNCs. Similarly,success in the industry depends on the ability to attract new customers,retain existing customers and increase their usage.Customers have a wide variety of options for transportation, including using their own vehicles,rental cars,taxicabs,public transport and other TNC businesses.Customer preferences are likely to change over time so TNCs must continually look to new ways to appeal to new customers who have historically used other forms of transportation or other TNC platforms. Safety issues have been a major challenge for the TNC industry.Illegal,improper or inappropriate activities by customers and drivers have adversely affected the reputation of the whole industry. These activities have included assault,theft,unauthorised use of credit and debit cards or bank account details and the sharing of customer and driver account nformation.Such conduct has exposed TNCs to several high-profile lawsuits.The challenge for TNCs is to implement and continually update sufficient measures to safeguard against illegal/improper activities.Without these measures,growth and retention of customers and qualified drivers will be a continual challenge for TNCs. The environmental impact of transportation has become a major focus for governments across the world.The transportation industryis considered to be a significant contributor to air pollution, and TNC activity has been cited as a cause for rising congestion and air pollution in urban areas. Several cities in Bealand are introducing technologies to manage traffic flow and congestion, which could disrupt the services offered by TNCs.Also,clean air zones(CAZ)are being introduced in many towns and cities to improve air quality,with fees being imposed on all drivers driving in those designated zones. Latest developments -Food delivery.TNCs in some countries have recently started to offeraservice where customers can order food through a dedicated app from local and national restaurants and food outlets. Orders are placed via the TNC's platform,the restaurant confirms the order,prepares the food,and a TNC driver picks it up and delivers the food to the selected destination. - Driverless(autonomous)cars.Some global TNCs are currently testing the viability of driverless cars to deliver their services,but currently all such vehicles must operate with at least one supervisory person present in the vehicle.Driverless cars can navigate to a destination,avoiding obstacles without human intervention.To accomplish this,it uses an artificia intelligence system which senses its surroundings,processes visual data to avoid collisions.steers.brakes.and uses GPS to track current location and destination. -Electric vehicles(EV).Most TNCs recognise their potentially negative impact on the environment due to increasing mileage covered by drivers using fossilfuelled cars.Therefore, many TNCs are increasingly encouraging drivers to use EVs. 批注[37]: TNC 业务的增长取决于能否吸引和留住符合规 定筛选标准的合格司机，以及能否增加司机的工作时间。 如果TNC 不能为司机提供赚取足够收入的灵活性和机会， 就无法吸引新司机和/或留住现有司机，从而导致投诉和 负面宣传。这将不可避免地对客户和企业客户产生不利影 响。随着城市化进程的加快和汽车保有量的减少，保持和 增加必要的司机队伍将是TNC 面临的一项挑战。 批注[38]:同样，该行业的成功取决于吸引新客户、留住 现有客户和提高客户使用率的能力。客户有多种交通方式 可供选择，包括使用自己的车辆、租车、出租车、公共交 通和其他TNC 业务。客户的喜好可能会随着时间的推移而 改变，因此TNC 必须不断寻找新的方式来吸引那些过去一 直使用其他交通方式或其他TNC 平台的新客户。 批注[39]:安全问题一直是TNC 行业面临的一大挑战。客 户和司机的非法、不正当或不当活动对整个行业的声誉造 成了负面影响。这些活动包括攻击、盗窃、未经授权使用 信用卡和借记卡或银行账户信息以及共享客户和司机账户 信息。这些行为使TNC 面临多起引人注目的诉讼。 TNC 面 临的挑战是实施并不断更新足够的措施，以防范非法/不 当活动。没有这些措施，客户和合格司机的增长和保留对 TNC 来说将是一个持续的挑战。 批注[40]:交通对环境的影响已成为世界各国政府关注的 Technology Our business involves the collection,storage and processing of customer infrastructure and driver personal data.We also maintain confidential and sensitive information relating to our business.Therefore,our systems are vulnerable to security incidents and breaches.As the techniques used to obtain unauthorised access or sabotage systems change frequently,we may be unable to anticipate or prevent such attacks.We also rely on our third-party cloud service provider to ensure that it maintains sufficient investment in systems security and reliability. Competition The TNC market is highly competitive and characterised by rapid changes in technology.We expect competition to continue,both from current competitors and new entrants in the market.If we are unable to anticipate or successfully react to these competitive challenges,our competitive position could weaken. We also compete with public transport,taxicab companies,car rental businesses and micro mobility companies,some of which may have greater resources than we do. Licensing We depend on the ocal authority of each city in which we operate to grant us and industry and our drivers the operating licences to permit our operations.If we,or our regulations drivers,do not comply with license arrangements or other transport providers put pressure on local authorities to prevent us from receiving these licences to operate,this will severely restrict our business. Values Our Ethical Code of Conduct ensures that we mantain our focus on operating to the highest ethical and professional standards.Our focus is also on the safety of our customers,drivers and the communities we work within. 批注[79]:致力于安全：Committed to safety 批注[79]:致力于安全： We value the safety of our customers and drivers.To protect them we implement a range of safety features: - Annual background checks on every driver are carried out by third party experts. - Continuous criminal monitoring means drivers with disqualifying criminal convictions are removed from the platform immediately. - Drivers must complete a safety programme annually,which includes how to create a safe and comfortable ride,appropriate conversation topics and respecting personal boundaries. - If a customer or driver feels uncomfortable or needs emergency assistance at any point, they can instantly connect with our security team through an in-app help button,24/7. - Customers have access to a 'friends and family'notification system,which can notify designated customer contacts of the details of rides in progress. - Most of our drivers'vehicles have been equipped with cameras to record rides to protect both customers and drivers. Data privacy and the storage and usage of our customers'data is a top priority.All customer contact details,ride and payment information are considered classified data,and we fully comply with all Bealand legislation in terms of data privacy,storage and usage.We also expect our cloud service provider to operate to the same standards in terms of protecting and managing our customers'personal data. - At the end of each ride,drivers and customers must rate their ride.Any customer or driver who submits a rating below 80%satisfaction is prompted to provide more details. Committed to the environment A primary concern of government and the communites we serve is the level of harmful emissions in Bealand's towns and cities.We are aware that our business is a contributor to these emissions. Therefore,we are committed to reducing our impact,in several ways: - We encourage customers to select shared rides and micro mobility options wherever possible. - We continue to invest further in our micro mobility infrastructure across Bealand. 我们重视客户和司机的安全。为了保护他们，我们实施了 一系列安全措施： -第三方专家对每位司机进行年度背景调查。 一持续的犯罪监管意味着有过犯罪行为的不合格司机会立 即被平台移除。 -司机必须每年完成一项安全计划，其中包括如何创造安 全舒适的乘坐体验、合适的聊天话题和保持个人界限。 -如果客户或司机在任何时候感到不舒服或需要紧急援助， 他们可以通过应用程序内的帮助按钮全天候与我们的安全 团队联系。 -客户可以访问“朋友和家人”通知系统，该系统能向指 定的客户联系人通知正在进行的行程的详细信息。 -我们的大多数车辆都配备了摄像头来记录行程，以保护 客户和司机。 -数据隐私以及客户数据的存储和使用是首要任务。所有 客户的联系方式、乘车和支付信息都被视为机密，我们在 数据隐私、存储和使用方面完全遵守Bealand 的所有立法。 我们还希望我们的云服务提供商在保护和管理客户个人数 据方面遵循相同的标准。 -在每次行程结束时，司机和客户必须进行评分。任何满 意度低于80%的客户或司机都需要提供更多详细信息。 批注[80]:致力于环境保护： 政府和我们所服务的社区的主要关注点是在Bealand 的 城 市和城镇的有害气体排放量。我们意识到自身的业务产生 了这些排放。因此，我们通过多种方式减轻我们的影响： -我们鼓励客户尽可能选择拼车和共享单车方式。 -我们将继续在Bealand 国内投资共享单车基础设施。 September 2024 12 - The suggested answers presented below inevitably give much more detail than would be expected from most candidates under examination conditions,and include most of the obvious points evidenced from the case information. The answers are therefore intended to provide a structure of the approach required from candidates,and cover the range and depth of knowledge relating to each task which might be demonstrated by the most well prepared and able candidates.They are also intended to support revision and tuition for future examinations. 1 (a)Memo to the CEO The chair of the risk committee's email identified two key areas which cause serious concern,which will need to be effectively addressed if NCTech is going to maintain its good reputation and strong relationship with its client base.The board should be proactive in its response to the handling of the breach to avoid details being made public accidentally. The following actions would significantly reduce the potential damage to the business and reassure clients that NCTech takes its responsibility for data security very seriously.Taking each area in turn: Systems breach Unauthorised access to client data and data breaches are both identified as principal business risks in the NCTech risk register. However,it is apparent that the mitigation actions have not worked in this instance.The hacker was not immediately identified and blocked,otherwise data could not have been copied and extracted.Therefore,current IT security procedures will need to be reviewed as a matter of urgency,and more effective measures introduced. Another concern is that the system has been exposed to data breaches and hacking for a prolonged period,and it is not known if other similar incidents have occurred.The inherent system weakness,which has gone undetected for several months,has subjected all NCTech clients to a risk of data breach.It cannot be determined with any certainty if other client data has been hacked during this prolonged period.Should this system vulnerability be disclosed to clients,it would undoubtedly reduce their confidence in NCTech as a secure cloud services provider and could result in many clients taking their business elsewhere, further harming financial performance. It is vital that clients consider NCTech to have integrity by dealing with them in an open,honest and straightforward manner.If clients lose trust in NCTech,this could cause wider reputational harm which would only be advantageous to the competition, and it would be particularly easy for ‘pay as you go'clients to take their business elsewhere,eroding market share.Therefore, the board must issue a carefully worded communication to all clients detailing the nature of the breach and reassuring them that appropriate action has been taken to avoid a recurrence. Although there were no actual data lost,the copying of commercially confidential and sensitive client data could result in significant harm to the clients.They may decide to take legal action against NCTech for any consequential losses arising from the hacking incident.This is an unforeseen cost which has not been provided for. Breach of trust The deliberate concealment of the data violation from senior management constitutes a serious breach of trust.It is vital that all saf can be trusted to act correctly,adhere to prescribed procedures,and behave ethically atall times.The reason for this unacceptable behaviour needs to be investigated and understood,so that appropriate measures can be taken to avoid future similar recurrences. As this particular incident went unreported,it could be reasonably assumed that similar unreported incidents may have occurred in the past.However,it is unlikely that staff would alert the board to other breaches as this would reflect badly upon them. There could be a prevalent concealment culture within NCTech where internal controls are circumvented,exposing the business to significant reputational harm.The board needs to build a culture which encourages staff to report problems and incidents, rather than one which is seen to apportion blame,so that appropriate actions will be taken in the future. Actions to take A more detailed investigation into system security should be initiated by the board as a matter of priority,with the remit of recommending additional security measures which will assure the security of client data.Allffected clients should be contacted and advised of the extent of the breach on their stored data,together with details of actions taken by NCTech to remedy the situation.This may need to include offers of financial compensation to avoid any resultant legal action. The board should issue a statement about the security breach and the subsequent action taken by NCTech,thereby reducing its damaging impact on the business.By controlling the narrative in this way,it may be possible to deter clients from taking their business elsewhere. (b)Briefing paper to the NCTech board on IT security arrangements Introduction The four layers of IT security contained within the NCTech security manual comprise the overarching basis upon which current security arrangements have been devised.However,following the recent security breach,it would be sensible to undertake a full evaluation of their appropriateness so that further control measures could be implemented to avoid another breach.

1. Human layer The human layer controls are primarily based around the education and training of staff so that they remain fully compliant with NCTech standard operating procedures atall times.This should alert staff to any potential issues and will help to avoid further security breaches. This layer is also likely to include practices such as regularly changing passwords to help to ensure authorised access to the NCTech systems is tightly controlled and enable user activity to be actively monitored. On its own,the human layer is not a robust control because it relies on the iligent behaviour and attitudes ofall staff and system users.If operating procedures are not strictly complied with,there could be another IT security breach which may cause further significant harm to NCTech.Therefore,the human layer should be considered holistically as a component of IT security alongside the other three layers. Internal audit could be deployed to undertake spot checks to ensure that all security procedures are functioning within defined parameters.This would act as a deterrent for any staff currently willing to behave inappropriately,and enable procedures to be refined and further tightened,if necessary.

2. Perimeter layer The perimeter layer is not only the point where clients enter the NCTech system to access their data for legitimate business purposes,but also where unauthorised parties try to access data illegally.However,it is important that clients are not impeded by additional security measures,as this would make cloud services a less appealing option. The current arrangement is to ensure that any external device which requires access to the NCTech system is authorised in advance.This should mean that any unauthorised device cannot gain access to the laaS system,or the client data contained within it.Unfortunately,the recent data breach indicates that the current arrangements may not be a reliable control,as access through the perimeter layer took place without detection or expulsion. Security measures such as requiring every device to be issued with a unique key(or code)before gaining access to the laaS system should limit the chances of another laaS system perimeter breach.The data encryption software could be used to encode all data on the laaS system,which means that any data copied or removed will be of no value unless it can be de-coded,and this is not possible without using the data encryption software.NCTech must regularly upgrade all its perimeter security measures to ensure that they remain fit for purpose. 3.Application layer Many software applications are used by clients to process data accessible from the NCTech system.NCTech's application layer security measures ensure that individual client applications do not damage the integrity of the laaS system or corrupt other clients'data. The primary control is NCTech IT staff,who keep the laaS operating system functioning optimally at alltimes.They ensure that all client software applications are systematically checked for viruses before they can be used,with software restriction policies preventing any potentially unsafe software being used on the system. Although there have been no reported incidents where application layer controls may have failed,virus protection software and other application security measures must be regularly updated if they are to remain effective.

4. Data layer The data layer relates to the movement of client data into and out of the laaS system.Firewalls should limit a hacker gaining unauthorised access to NCTech client data,with regular data and system backups ensuring that any data illegally taken by hackers can be restored for immediate use by the authorised client user. Data stored remains the legal property of the relevant client at all times,with NCTech acting as custodian to safeguard it on behalf of the client.Therefore,it is essential that data held on the laaS system is secure and regular reviews of firewall security are undertaken. Conclusion Data security is fundamental to the NCTech business model.The recent data breach may have defeated some of the security measures in the four layers the laaS system.However,collectively they are likely to have made the breach more ifficult,and any unauthorised data unusable. However,if details of the recent data breach are made public,it could seriously damage the reputation of NCTech and its strong relationship with the client base.This could result in a loss of business and a negative effect on future business performance. It is therefore important that NCTech regularly reviews and updates the IT security manual. 2 (a)Briefing paper to the chair of the nominations committee As two directors are shortly due to retire,this presents the board with an opportunity to refresh its membership and embrace diversity as you have suggested.The rationale on board appointments should always be to constitute the best board which can deliver sustainable business success and satisfactory returns to shareholders. Diverse board membership has become increasingly popular because it can offer a number of clear business benefits,these include:

1. More effective decision making When a board has remained unchanged for several years,like NCTech,there is the risk of groupthink developing where ideas converge and go unchallenged.A more diverse board will comprise individuals who offer different opinions,so are able to analyse issues and tackle problems from a range of perspectives.Healthy debate can lead to better decisions when the board agrees to tackle similar and/or recurring issues in differing ways.

2. Reflective of the real world Ideally,the board should represent the markets it is targeting as it would help to optimally shape the company's long-term strategy and strengthen its position within its industry.When the NCTech board was established by the founding directors, it,unsurprisingly,was technologically biased,but over time other key functions of business activity were included. However,the current board structure may not be reflective of the wider market in terms of gender,ethnicity,backgrounds, etc,so it may not be able to consider issues from an external stakeholder's perspective.

3. Improved reputation A diverse board can signal to both internal and external stakeholders that it is aligned with their values,preferences and interests,and enhance the reputation of the firm: - making NCTech an equal opportunity employer;attracting high quality staff who are motivated to perform well and deliver greater success to the company;and 一 encouraging more customers to procure NCTech services particularly if the company positions itself as being socially responsible.This could increase revenue and market share.

4. Talent The board needs to ensure that they can access a wide talent pool so that the best calibre of directors is appointed.The problem if the nominations committee searches for new board members with limited pre-defined characteristics is that it will significantly reduce the number available,whereas reaching out to a more diverse group of potential candidates will increase the choice available,and this is more likely to result in better candidates appointed.

5. Stakeholder demands Stakeholders are becoming increasingly demanding of directors,in particular of non-executive directors(NEDs)who are often criticised for devoting insufficient time and effort to understanding the business,representing stakeholders demands,and scrutinising executive directors.Although the NCTech board is currently only looking to fill the positions of the two directors who are retiring,it might be opportune to extend this to include changing some of the NEDs and adding diversity. It should be appreciated that some institutional investors take board diversity into account when evaluating investment decisions,as there can be a positive correlation between business value and board diversity

6. Flexible operations A board comprising directors from a variety of backgrounds can have the effect of making NCTech more adaptable and responsive to the changing business environment.By setting this example at board level,it may cascade through the organisation and lead to innovation and creativity due to a broader and more diverse set of ideas.This in turn can lead to improved financial performance. (b)Email From:consultant@chelwood.com To:HRdirector@nctech.com Ref:Talent management programme Talent management is a strategic initiative which can help NCTech to realise the full potential of its staff.Starting with thee recruitment process,it includes a full range of measures and interventions to improve overallstaff performance.If done correctly,it can enhance competitive advantage and would become a major business strength for NCTech. A talent management programme views allmembers of staff as business-critical assets.Like any other asset,staff should be safeguarded and developed so that they can deliver good returns to shareholders. There are a number of advantages to NCTech of adopting the talent management programme,which include:

1. Performance enhancement The programme should be viewed as a strategic human resources management initiative,and like any strategy,it should have defined objectives and outcomes.Key performance indicators and associated metrics can indicate the business performance benefits which directly arise from the programme.NCTech has seen a deterioration in financial performance in the past year,so any intervention which could enhance business performance and improve financial returns must be given serious consideration.

2. Strategic aignment Talent management is a forward-looking programme which primarily focuses on the requirements of the business.This is because it is essential that the company can align its corporate objectives with talent management to deliver success. Planning will be a core element of the talent management programme to ensure that the company harnesses the inherent and relevant talent of staff to fit with business needs. The identification of business goals and understanding what is required to achieve them will help NCTech to create job profiles and specifications needed to deliver further success.These can then be used strategically to acquire new talent and develop existing talented staff.

3. Culture Investment in talent management is likely to be viewed positively by most employees,which could increase staff loyalty and their commitment to the company.Provided the programme is fair and accessible to all saf,it can become a powerful motivational tool,particularly if it is seen to help employees to attain advancement and personal success.Learning opportunities are an integral part of talent management and will quip staff with more skills and knowledge to help them develop their careers within NCTech. The talent management programme should offer rewards in recognition for the specific contributions of individual employees.This could further encourage a culture of effort and attainment among the workforce and contribute to inclusiveness and diversity.

4. Succession planning Workforce planning is a feature of talent management which is directly aligned to the future needs of the business.The programme can help NCTech to retain and develop the best talent and nurture the most capable workforce for the future needs of the business,such as replacing the two directors who are shortly to retire from the board. By shaping the future of NCTech,and determining specific staffing needs,it should make the recruitment of staff easier. This is because recruitment will be drawn from existing,known and available members of staff,which in turn will boost employee engagement and improve retention if the staff feel that they can progress their careers within NCTech.From a purely financial perspective,it is better to keep existing employees rather than hiring new ones as it will save on high recruitment costs. However,there are a number of disadvantages which need to be understood,so that they can be overcome and managed effectively if the talent management programme is to be successful. These include: 1.Resource intensity The time,internal resources and the costs required to operate an effective talent management programme can be very significant,so suficient funds will need to be found.This could be viewed as an additional financial burden for the business if the expected benefits are not immediately realisable.Instead,the programme should be regarded as an investment in people with deferred returns over the medium to long term.

2. Strategic distraction Some directors may view talent management as a bit of a fad,and an unnecessary business distraction.They may consider that the effort and resources required would be better applied to improve the existing services provided to clients or invest in new technologies.Without consensus at board level,the programme is unlikely to be successful and any positive effects on the NCTech business willnot be realised.

3. Conflict and lack of support The talent management programmes could cause conflict between the HR department,who will be championing it,and NCTech management if they are not fully consulted,and a consensus is not agreed.It could be a challenge to get support from every department,but this is necessary if the programme is to be a success. The orchestration of the talent management programme needs to be covered by a holistic plan encapsulating all areas of the business,and its implementation requires the consideration and support of all management.However,without a complete understanding of the programme and its business benefits,it can lead to a breakdown in relations and conflict between management and HR.

4. Limited talent pool Employees may be competent in their current roles,but even with a talent management programme,some staff may not be suited for advancement or even an extension of their current workload.This would be particularly relevant for NCTech's technical staff,as their skills may be too niche to grow into wider and more generic business roles. In such situations,it may be necessary to procure the services of recruitment consultants,which would result in additional costs and make the talent management programme less financially viable. 3 (a)Report to commercial director Introduction This report evaluates those stakeholders who may influence the strategic planning process,together with consideration of their conflicting demands on NCTech. NCTech stakeholders

1. Institutional shareholders Shareholders own NCTech and the primary purpose of the company is to maximise their wealth.However,as NCTech is listed on the Farland stock exchange,corporate governance formally regulates the relationship between the company and its shareholders.This is required because shareholders are able to sell their stake in the company at any time,so it would be inappropriate for any shareholder to exert too much influence over the company's strategic direction or key decisions. No individual shareholder holds more than 10%equity which will evidently limit the extent of the power and influence they are able to exert.However,collectively they could have a formidable impact on the company.Provided NCTech is able to improve share price performance,shareholders are not likely to make further demands.

2. Founding shareholders The original three founders,who stillremain as shareholders,are in a different position to the institutional shareholders as they are all directors on the board.This means that they have significantly more power and influence than other shareholders,but as they do not represent a majority on the board and the chair controls proceedings,they cannot act purely with self-interest.

3. Clients The client base provides the only source of revenue for NCTech,so keeping them satisfied must be a prime consideration. The core customer base would be classified as key players because not only are they powerful stakeholders,but they will also have a very keen interest in the operational performance of NCTech.Clients ultimately determine the strategic direction of the company,and it is vital that they remain happy with the quality and range of services offered by NCTech. Over time,the current laaS provision may not meet the commercial needs of the client base,so the marketing team must regularly consult with clients so that the board is aware of the business'direction of travel required over the medium to long term.Failure to do so could make the current service offerings obsolete and damage the business irreparably. However,the significant infrastructure investment required to deliver enhanced cloud services to clients may not be viewed as necessary or financially justifiable by some shareholders.This conflict between clients and shareholders could result in avoidable delays and potentially irreparable damage to NCTech's strategic position. 4.Employees As a service organisation,NCTech will be totally reliant on its workforce for the performance of the business.It is crucial that staff are fully engaged with the strategy which NCTech is trying to pursue,as they will ultimately be the ones delivering it.There are different types of employees who have varying degrees of power and influence over the firm, largely depending on how easy it would be to replace them,and they may have differing demands on the company.All employees may want higher salaries but only those who have significant power as they are difficult to replace and are key to the future of NCTech are able to exert sufficient pressure on the board. Some employees may demand more challenging work,structured training,and career development opportunities but these may not be aligned with the board's view of how NCTech needs to strategically progress.This will require a trade-off between retaining vital members of staff and delivering a costed strategy acceptable to other stakeholder groups. Conclusions and recommendations Understanding the relative importance of each stakeholder group,and their demands on NCTech,will be vital when devising a future strategy.Satisfying the needs and demands of significant stakeholders will ltimately help to determine the most achievable strategic direction for the firm.However,care must be taken to balance and address any conflicting stakeholder demands which could cause NCTech harm. (b)Slide 1 Capital elements 1.Financial

6. Social Accompanying notes Integrated reports differ from other forms of reporting as the focus is on business processes rather than the actual deliverables, using six capitals to demonstrate how value is created for all stakeholders,rather than just shareholders.

1. Financial capital indicates the funds available for use in core service provision,obtained through financing or generated through operations.The finance director has suggested that NCTech currently has limited access to the financial resources required for further investment,therefore additional financial capital would need to be sourced externally.Details of available finance should be included within the integrated report.

2. Manufactured capital comprises those physical assets deployed in core service provision.NCTech,being an IT company, has extensive technology infrastructure in support of its business model.The manufactured capital needs to be maintained and improved to ensure that the business remains relevant to its market and competitively advantageous.This should be detailed in the integrated report.

3. Human capital describes the skills,competences and experience of the NCTech workforce in delivering efficient and high-quality services to clients.This includes the motivation of staff necessary to innovate and improve business performance.Details of the talent management programme should be included in the integrated report as a component of human capital since it illustrates how staff can help deliver performance excellence.

4. Intellectual capital is those intangible assets which provide the basis for sustainable competitive advantage.At NCTech, this could include the organisation systems and proprietary or branded software used to deliver services to clients.It will also include the company's reputation with its client base,which is vital for ongoing business success.The integrated report should disclose details of customer satisfaction ratings as a measure of the changing value of NCTech's intellectual capital.

5. Natural capital describes inputs sourced from the natural environment required for service provisions,and any direct impact business operations may have on the natural environment.The technology at NCTech will require a continuous and reliable energy source,so NCTech should be transparent in disclosing the type of energy generation used,and this should be strengthened with an integrated report.

6. Social capital constitutes any relationships which have been established with stakeholder groups which aim to enhance individual and collective wellbeing ofall parties involved.Although NCTech does not have any stated social responsibility policies,with the primary aim to deliver high quality services to clients and good returns to shareholders,an integrated report could refocus management attention in this area.Ultimately,social capital can strengthen stakeholder engagement and the businesses reputation. Slide 2 Business benefits 1.Strategic and forward-looking 2.Materiality 3.Reliability and completeness 4.Consistent and comparable 5.Conciseness 6.Connectivity and relationships Accompanying notes If NCTech decided to adopt integrated reporting it could be beneficial in a several ways:

1. Strategic and forward-looking:It is set at the strategic level so encapsulates all aspects of business performance and is holistically reported.Although reflecting on past performance,it has a future orientation and therefore can assist management with decision making.

2. Materiality: Integrated reports focus on material issues,disclosing only those which may substantially impact on value creation.This enables the users of the information to appreciate key matters which are affecting NCTech,and not be distracted by less significant issues.

3. Reliability and completeness: The information detailed for each of the capital categories can be relied upon as being factual,complete and accurate.This objective approach removes the risk of incorrect judgement and bias being contained within the integrated report

4. Consistent and comparable: Each integrated report contains standard elements,in the form of the six capitals.This standard approach ensures consistency over time and enables NCTech's report to be compared with those of other companies.This will enable the board and senior management to gauge the relative strategic performance of NCTech and the success of any initiative.

5. Conciseness: The integrated report is kept relatively concise with data visualisation tools to assist user understanding. This should encourage more staff within NCTech to read the report and take an interest in the business,and for external stakeholders to understand how the business is performing for their benefit.

6. Connectivity and relationships: The integrated report aims to connect the business across all of its relationships which create value.At NCTech,the interconnected stakeholders include institutional shareholders,clients and the workforce. Each of the stakeholder groups contribute individually and collectively to value creation at NCTech,and they can use the integrated report to understand the extent and success of their contributions.
