The suggested answers presented below inevitably give much more detail than would be expected from most candidates under examination conditions,and include most of the obvious points evidenced from the case information. The answers are therefore intended to provide a structure of the approach required from candidates,and cover the range and depth of knowledge relating to each task which might be demonstrated by the most well prepared and able candidates.They are also intended to support revision and tuition for future examinations. 1 Report To:The board of NT From:External adviser Date:6 June 20X3 Subject:Culture,leadership style and successful change Introduction This report advises on both the changes needed to NT's culture to enable the diversification strategy to succeed,and the leadership style necessary to ensure the successful implementation of the organisational changes arising from the strategy. (a) Culture and change The culture of an organisation is intangible and difficult to describe but is often described as the way we do things round here'. The cultural web is a tool which can be used to analyse the culture of an organisation,however,and understand the different influences on it.It can provide a map of the existing NT culture and the future culture needed to make the diversification strategy succeed.The two maps can then be compared to determine how the culture needs to change. The cultural web identifies six elements which influence what is known as the cultural paradigm -the core beliefs and motivations of NT. Stories Currently NT's beginnings(with a focus on the hard work and perseverance of Kirk Nola and his desire to help children to play imaginatively),the winning of numerous industry awards,the positive impact on society which the organisation has had and endorsements from celebrities are all stories which are told.They reflect the organisation's core values of play,quality,loyalty and pride and have contributed to the high levels of staff and customer loyalty.Significantly,the story about the maverick operations director who made an unsuccessful attempt to widen the product portfolio provides a useful insight into how future change proposals will be treated by staff. The future To maintain staff goodwill,stories which build on those which currently exist will need to be assembled going forward.These could involve both celebrity endorsements and the winning of awards for any new products as well as the continuation of global educational and social projects.Any stories relating to successful change projects should be woven into this aspect of NT's cultural web to improve the organisation's capability to implement change. Symbols Currently Kirk Nola has a symbolic quality even though he has left the organisation,which underlines the importance of the company's history to the culture of the organisation. All the company buildings look as if they are constructed from Nola Bricks,indicating the key role of the base product to NT's culture.Both this and the quirky,colourful layout of the offices underpin the focus on play,another of the core values. All staff,including senior management,work in open plan offices and receive the same benefits package,indicating a lack of status symbols,and hence it may be inappropriate to introduce them,at least in the short to medium term. The future To facilitate the move to an alternative strategy,the importance of the symbolic power of the founder must be reduced so that staff are more able to engage with the new direction.Any changes would need to be made gradually,however.Buy-in will not be possible unless the timeframe is appropriate.The preservation of other symbols may be necessary to minimise resistance to change. Power structures Currently Historically,power has been held very tightly by Kirk Nola.He was fiercely protective of the brand and controlled it carefully. It is evident he was the key member of the board in terms of setting the strategic direction of the organisation,with the result that decision making has been very centralised.Comments on the intranet suggest that staff were fully supportive of this and trusted him completely to make decisions in both the organisation's and their best interests.Indeed,given the success of NT, the above market rates of pay offered,the range of benefits and the relaxed and fun work environment,it is unlikely that staff would challenge the status quo. The future Power now sits with ZC,via you as board of directors.You have a responsibility to ZC as part of corporate governance and a specific brief to fulfil ZC's objective in purchasing NT.Having the power to effect change may not be sufficient to overcome determined resistance among staff,however.Although staff are used to power sitting centrally,to immediately exert that power and make unpopular changes would not be in the best interests of future success.Taking time to implement the changes and adopting an appropriate leadership style to create buy-in to the change(which is covered in this report below)will go some way to overcoming the cultural resistance to change which clearly exists in NT. Organisational structures Currently NT is currently structured on a hierarchical functional basis,reflecting and supporting the centralised power structure and decision making. The future A divisional organisational structure,with semi-autonomous units based on products,is likely to be more appropriate to support the diversification strategy. Control systems Currently Ensuring quality of output is one of the core values and one of the bases for employee bonus payments.It is therefore of great significance to the organisation,as is customer satisfaction,another key performance indicator (KPI)monitored by NT and the other basis of employee bonus payments. In addition,a significant system of controls throughout the organisation is needed to support the values of focus and prudence and the risk averse nature of the decision making. The future The diversification brief requires a less risk averse approach to decision making and hence the control systems will need to be loosened to facilitate this.Controls and KPls will need to include financial metrics and there should be a focus on growth. Bonus schemes will need to be established to encourage a profit-focused outlook. Routines and rituals Currently The approach to recruitment,which ensures all employees are aligned to the company's values,particularly around thee importance of play,is a key ritual in the company.The annual Day of Play is also seen as an important event.These highlight the importance of the core value of play within NT and provide evidence that behaviour does not need to be overly formal. The future Recruitment may need to integrate a focus on entrepreneurism,for instance,to support the diversification agenda,and an ability to take well-considered risks to encourage growth.This will ensure those who are recruited will be the right fit for NT's new direction.New rituals may need to be introduced to celebrate success aligned to the new strategy,such as celebrations for new product launches or growth milestones. Summary Aspects of the existing paradigm should therefore be replaced to enable the diversification strategy to succeed,but this should be done with caution.The need for change is urgent but the board should be cognisant that immediate implementation of changes could increase staff resistance. (b)Leadership style and change There are two extremes of leadership style: Transformational or charismatic leadership is focused on building a vision for the organisation and energising and inspiring people to achieve it.It requires a high level of coordination,communication and cooperaton. Transactional leadership is based on systems and controls which can be used to drive change such as changes in structures and setting targets.A transactional leader would use rewards and punishments to motivate staff in order to achieve change. In essence,transformational leaders look at how to motivate people to do things,whereas transactional leaders look at how to get things done. Given the staff resistance to change as evidenced by the posts on the intranet,it is unlikely that a transformational approach will be successful as levels of cooperation willbe low.Staff are unlikely to buy into a new vision for NT or be influenced by the charisma and enthusiasm of a transformational leader given their loyalty to Kirk Nola and all that he represents.In addition,it takes time to build a new vision but the need for change is urgent-ZC wants the new strategy to be implemented immediately. Transactional leadership requires a hierarchical structure.This already exists at NT and staff are used to senior management having power (although,as noted above,a divisional structure may be more appropriate to support the diversification strategy). The organisational culture should be one in which rules must be followed.AlIthough Kirk Nola had great influence-and still does-and staff were happy to follow his decisions,the culture of NT would appear to be relaxed and informal and there is no evidence to suppose punishment is part of it.Attempting to impose a rules-based culture or punishments culture may therefore further exacerbate levels of staff unease.Staff may be open to rewards,however,similar to existing benefits such as the annual Day of Play,say,or additional opportunities to volunteer or an augmentation of the bonus scheme. The capability for managing change and the readiness for change can be key to determining an appropriate leadership style -from a collaborative approach when capability and readiness is high,via participative and persuasive approaches when readiness or capability drop,to a directive approach when capability and readiness are low,and the need for change is urgent. Given the request for advice on leadership and change management,and the fact that NT has always followed the same overall strategy,it is likely that the board is inexperienced in this area,and there appears to be little readiness for change within thee organisation.Top-down direction could therefore be the most appropriate leadership style for senior management of NT to adopt to manage the change process. The type of strategic change to be implemented will also have a bearing on the leadership style most suited to manage thee change.Strategic change can be classified according to the speed of change needed and the extent of the change.Given thee urgency allocated to the board's instruction from ZC,and the extent of cultural change required as set out above,a revolution' is necessary to break down the constraints which the existing culture has placed around NT's strategy.Leading change in such circumstances will involve giving clear strategic direction,introducing new senior management (to indicate the significance of the change to staffand other stakeholders),recognising the cultural change cannot be achieved immediately and that somee aspects may need to be retained and built upon,and setting and monitoring clear targets aligned to the change agenda. Summary An appropriate leadership style to ensure the professional and effective implementation of change will therefore need to be predominantly directive,and focus on controls,systems and targets-in other words mostly transactional in nature-whilst taking account of current staff attitudes and feelings. In practice,leaders typically combine elements of both styles,the best leaders adjusting their leadership style to the situation they face,and this could be the most appropriate strategy to ensure professional and effective implementation of change. Note: An alternative approach based on laissez faire/autocratic/democratic styles would be acceptable. 2 Report To:Finance director From:External adviser Date:6 June 20X3 Subject:Diversification-financial appraisal and risk analysis Introduction This report provides a critical assessment of the financial appraisal prepared by the finance assistant of a potential move into thee computer games industry by NT,identifies the risks of such a move and recommends suitable ways of mitigating them. (a) Techniques used Calculating a net present value using discounted cash flow techniques is theoretically the best method to determine whether an investment should be undertaken.The negative NPV would indicate that NT should not move into computer games,but thee points raised below and their impact on what is a very small negative NPV should be brought into consideration. Time frame Given that the computer games industry represents a new market for NT,aditional research and analysis should be carried out to determine the appropriateness of applying a five-year investment time frame to this financial appraisal. Timing The potential for earning revenue from the new operation within a year of initial investments should be questioned.Computer games can take significant periods of time to develop,and there is no guarantee that games willbe popular and generate revenue on launch. Revenue It is unrealistic to assume that both the revenue of the global video games market and NT's projected market share will not change over the five years of the analysis.It could be argued that there would be litle point investing in a market unless it was growing and the NT share of it was expected to grow.The approach taken is at least conservative,although analysts expect the market to generate more than $260 billion by 20X5 and hence it may be more appropriate to reflect this increase.Were thee market to grow to this extent,NT's annual revenue would increase by$18·90 million to $70·20 million(based on the O·027% market share used by the finance assistant). If the finance assistant has not taken into account the risks associated with a move into the computer games industry (see below)when arriving at the 0·027%market share,the figure should be adjusted down,leading to an even worse NPV. Given the size of the market,any small error in the anticipated market share could have a significant impact on the revenue. For instance,were the market share to be 0·02%rather than 0·027%,revenue would be $13·30 million less per annum. The project is therefore very sensitive to the anticipated level of revenue. Contribution The use of the average contribution to sales ratios of five computer games companies with annual revenue less than $75 million (versus NT's projected $51 ·30 million)seems reasonable in the absence of any other indicator,although it may be prudent to lower the ratio slightly,at least for the first few years of the analysis,to reflect NT's lack of experience in the market and the impact that inexperience could have on the level of costs incurred. It is assumed that the costs of developing the games have been accounted for when arriving at a figure for contribution. These can run into tens of millions of dollars,and so more analysis may be needed to ensure such costs have been estimated correctly. Other costs The use of the company's ratio of marketing costs to sales may not be appropriate.There is curently a lot of free marketing via celebrity endorsements and the Nola Bricks product is well established and well known.Current marketing expenditure is therefore likely to be relatively low.The launch of a new product type will require substantial marketing activity,at least in the short term,and could well require alternative methods if a different audience to that of Nola Bricks needs to be reached. Indeed,computer games can cost tens of millions of dollars to market and so the amount included in the appraisal may be far too low. Further investigation is also needed to determine other costs which could be incurred as it seems unlikely that only marketing costs are relevant.There will no doubt be specific administration costs of moving into computer games,for instance.And there does not appear to have been any account taken of distribution costs-although the level of such costs will depend on whether NT sells physical or digital games. Taxation The corporation taxation effects of the net revenue have been included correctly,but it is unclear whether any taxation allowances which can be claimed against the initial investment have been included.If these have not been included,they will increase the post-tax net cash flows and improve the NPV. Initial investment As with the contribution to sales ratio used,the approach to determining the initial investment seems reasonable in the absence of alternative information.If possible,however,an assessment of the assets actually needed and how much they would cost at current prices should be made to arrive at a more accurate figure. The inclusion of the net realisable value of the non-current assets at the end of year 5 is only appropriate if NT plans to pull out of the market at that point.This seems unlikely given the scale of the investment. Other points Were the company to diversify,the resulting change programme and management's focus on the new area could impact on NT's current operations and revenue streams and estimates of these impacts should be included in the financial appraisal. Summary A re-assessment of cash flows associated with the project as set out above should be made and an NPV recalculated to assess the proposal from a financial perspective.Further sensitivity analysis and/or probability analysis could be applied to provide additional information on the feasibility of the investment. (b)Brand new industry A diversification strategy is invariably risky as it is likely to require significant investment and new competencies,but the level of risk is dependent on the relationship with the existing business.Given that 18%of the global computer games audience is the same as the market at which NT's current products are aimed,a move into computer games could be classed as related diversification-NT will be offering new products to (potentially,at least initially)an existing market in terms of both end-users (children)and those who buy the products(parents). Whilst NT has litleknowledge of the computer games market,a lot is known about a segment of the customers and users,and this knowledge can be used to inform decision making and reduce risk.NT already works with customers and users on new product ideas and could take the same approach and involve them in the project development process. It is true,however,that new resources and capabilities will be needed,that unfamiliar new technologies and processes will need to be mastered.The resources and expertise can be bought in,however.This might include hiring staff with the right skills as employees or consultants.Alternatively,NT could consider acquiring a potential competitor or forming a strategic alliance with one,in order to access either intangibles such as skills and competencies which are required to produce and sell computer games or tangible resources such as distribution channels. Inability to gain market share There is very significant competition within the industry,which would make it very dificult for NT to gain a large share of the market.Given the size of the market,however,even if NT were to operate as a smaller,niche provider,a 0·01%market share would provide $95 million of revenue over five years.The financial appraisal carried out by the finance assistant puts the potential market share at 0·027%,with annual revenue at over $51 million.Again,acquiring a competitor or forming a strategic alliance with a potential competitor would reduce this risk too. Lack of marketing insight The market share used by the finance assistant was based on data of other niche market players and the overall strength of thee NT brand rather than marketing insight about the potential popularity of a Nola Bricks computer game.Whilst NT does have the strength of the Nola Bricks brand to support product success,market research should be carried out as a matter of urgency to determine the market appetite for the product.This is not new to the company-feedback is collected from customers at every opportunity-and NT could apply its strength in this area to assemble information about the market appetite for the new product. Impact on current operations Ensuring in advance that NT is appropriately prepared for the implementation of the strategy,via an effective change management programme,would be a means of ensuring that current NT operations continued to deliver. Resources could be freed up to support the diversification strategy by outsourcing certain internal business functions,such as finance or HR,to enable staff to focus on new processes and information needs. Impact on brand Any change in strategic direction is likely to have some brand impact and the only way to avoid this is not to diversify.It is dificult to ensure all stakeholders are happy all the time,and hence some level of risk will have to be tolerated.Given NT's success,and its market-leading position,whilst some customers may be lost,it is unlikely to be a significant number.Market research-NT has good feedback loops with customers-will provide information on the extent to which there is likely to be a negative reaction. Ethical concerns It is possible that,unless the computer games industry takes a lead,more markets will face government intervention to control usage and some governments could even enforce legislation to control the addiction issue.This raises regulatory risks (that regulation will be brought in which affects the way the industry currently operates),legal risks (that legal action could be taken if NT were not to operate in line with the regulation,with the associated impact on reputation)and compliance risks (that NT could be subject to fines if shown to be non-compliant). To minimise these risks,NT could develop a competitive advantage in taking responsibility,especially given that products will at least initially be aimed at children,ensuring that there is clear messaging and notifications throughout games about thee potential risks.Games could be designed so that the amount of time and money spent is minimised and NT could work with a range of agencies to promote the support available,providing funding as necessary.Others might argue that it is up to thee parents to police the amount of time and money their children are spending,and that as a profit-making organisation this should not be an issue for NT.This would not seem to sit wellwith the overall ethos of the company,however. Inherent riskiness of the computer games industry Excellent project management will reduce the risks associated with computer games development.It is likely that suitable skills exist within the planning and forecasting area of the business to support this.In addition,with an investment in big data analytics,NT can increase the likelihood of producing successful games. Cybersecurity concerns NT will need to ensure that it leads the market by developing sophisticated security within its products,for instance including stronger authentications and other security controls.Given the potential age of those playing any games being developed,it will be vital to do everything possible to make players aware of,and educate them on the implications of,the risks. Product obsolescence Additional research should be caried out to determine the preferred format of games for the target audience,as this may be affected by the market segment(under 18s,it is assumed)being targeted.It may be that the physical product should be a loss leader,to encourage initial adoption,and a plan would need to be in place to move to a digital format,supported by a stringent financial analysis.Alternatively,both product formats could be launched if no clear preference is established by the research, but again strong financial analysis would be needed. 3 For the attention of the operations director of NT Briefing notes on the relevance of NT's current value-adding activities The business development executive's analysis of the computer games industry set out the factors citical to success in the computer games industry,many of which align closely to NT's current value-adding activities. Design The way NT approaches innovation as part of the design activities adds value to the current offering:innovation is informed by customers,by what they want,rather than it being innovation for the sake of it;a wide range of ideas is considered but ideas are only progressed if the costs of the innovation can be kept in check.Innovation is key to success in the computer games industry and applying this approach to a computer games operation would add value,helping to develop games which customers want and keeping the costs of the innovation process under control. Manufacturing The extent to which NT's manufacturing activities can add value to a computer games operation will depend on whether the focus is on digital games,given they already represent over 80%of the market,or whether NT produces physical games as well as-or instead of-a digital offering. The current manufacturing activities could be applied to and would add value to a computer games operation based on or including a physical product:NT would need to be able to respond quickly to customer demand for computer games;and eficiency of operations and the use of automation will be important.Any investment which needs to be made in new automated processes should be included in a financial appraisal to determine the viability of physical product production. If NT decides to offer only digital games,the current manufacturing activities are unlikely to add value to a computer games operation.NT can,however,look to apply the principle of efficiency to any new processes needed for the development of digital games. Planning and forecasting Given the importance of launching products on time and the inherent riskiness of the computer games industry,NT's current expertise in planning and forecasting would add value to a computer games operation by ensuring efficient scheduling of work, appropriate resource allocation,accurate forecasts supported by both market research and(for physical products)feedback from retalers and so on. Sales,marketing and brand management The protection of the NT brand will continue to be important to a computer games operation and NT's experience in protecting and managing the brand can be applied equally to a computer games operation.Whilst the current marketing activities might not be as 'aggressive’as needed in the computer games industry,they can add value by ensuring an immersive brand experience is also built into computer games,capitalising on the brand as a competitive advantage.In addition,the innovative approach taken to thee development of the current online presence,and the knowledge and experience gained,could be applied to and add value to thee development of digital computer games. The branded stores could provide an eficient route to market.Even if the games are instantly downloadable,they could be made available via a download code in a box and the boxes sold via the stores.The website could also be used as a channel to market for digital games. After sales service NT's current after sales activities have the potential to add value to a computer games operation if customer feedback is collected and then acted upon.A few poor online reviews from influential players can cause a game to fail shortly after its release:if NT can engage with and collect information from such players at release,potentally involving them in pre-release testing,online games in particular can be improved before there is huge reputational impact. Logistics NT may be unable to source the appropriate sills and competencies to operate a viable computer games operation.The current activities supporting the successful outsourcing of the logistics function could therefore be applied to the outsourcing of activities for the computer games industry. The sustainability agenda attached to the current outsourcing partnership can be applied to both current activities and the new activities needed to support the move into the computer games industry.This would add value to those customers looking for an increased focus on sustainability in the organisations they interact with. Summary Most of NT's current value-adding activities could be usefully applied to a computer games operation and would help deliver on thee critical success factors of that industry.Despite the need for a change management programme due to the diversification strategy, it is encouraging to note that many of the existing processes and functions from which NT derives value would be relevant to both parts of the business going forward and would facilitate some level of continuity and consistency. 4 Slide 1 Big data What opportunities could it present? Improving existing games Increasing the likelihood of developing successful games Generating revenue through in-game purchases Personalisation of advertisements to increase revenue Notes Opportunity-big data can improve existing games once they have been developed by NT Once NT has launched one or more digital games,an analysis of big data would allow the company to interpret the play habits and trends of gamers and then create a gaming experience which is ultimately more gratifying for them,improving retention and generating more positive reviews. The ability to process large data sets in real time means NT could respond quickly to identified or potential issues or 'bugs'n games.For example,once a game has been launched,data can be collected and analysed to determine features which might need improvement.Player performance can be tracked to gauge areas of a game which are too easy or too ificult-either of which might make a player leave a game. Features which are not generating player engagement and hence require improvement-maybe in-game instructions which are not clear enough -can be identified.Game chats can be analysed to establish unpopular aspects of games.Tweaks or modifications can then be made to the game to optimise player experience and ensure customer retention. Being able to react at speed means such problems could be dealt with before they become more widely discussed on social media, say,or,indeed,before any bad reviews are posted. Opportunity-big data can increase the likelihood of developing successful games within a competitive market NT could use information gathered from analyses of playing habits from its digital computer games to determine which games to invest in in the future,which games will be popular and sell in high volumes.Big data can indicate how games are played,when they are played and who gamers are playing with.For example,big data from an NT game aimed at children could reveal if it is played at school,as part of homework or as a leisure activity;whether children are playing the game alone,with school friends,or with parents and family members.This information would help inform,in this instance,the creation of future,successful games for children. Opportunity-big data can help generate revenue in games If games are free to play,revenue could be generated by providing opportunities for players to make small payments(microtransactions) during the game which improve their experience or give them advantage over other players. NT could use big data to measure,predict,and track player behaviour to be able to enhance their experience.It would also provide insight on where they are hitting obstacles and where they stop playing,or what exactly prompts them to purchase additional features.This insight could therefore be used both to make modifications to existing games and to design new games which encourage in-game spending-albeit not to excess,so as to maximise retention. With the help of data generated by players every day,NT could also personalise the microtransactions to a very intimate level,to make more and more players opt for them. NT must consider carefully the ethicality of encouraging children to spend in-game,however,and the potential impact on its brand. There are media reports of minors building up significant levels of debt on their parents'bank and credit cards and some markets are considering initiatives and legislation to limit this.As with the issue of addiction to computer games,NT could gain a competitive advantage by leading the market in protecting children from excessive in-game expenditure. Opportunity-big data can enable personalisation of advertising Too many advertisements in free-to-play digital games,or irelevant advertisements,can be a barrier to players engaging with a game and significantly affect retention. Data collected on players,however,could be used to ensure that any advertisements included were personalised and relevant to each individual player,matching their demographics,interest and location,say,which means they are more likely to engage with them. By placing advertisements in-game,their viewability is likely to be higher than if they are out-of-game.Another added benefit is that when advertisements occur naturally within the gaming environment,players feel less resistance towards them,and may even begin to associate the advertised brands with their positive gaming experiences. Effective advertising would generate additional revenue streams for NT,providing funds which could,for instance,be invested in thee development of future games. Big data What are the threats which using big data could create? Security Cost Data overload Notes Threat-security The computer games industry is a target for cybercrime;holding large quantities of data would only increase the risk from the threat of hackers,who would want to exploit or corrupt the data NT holds.NT would be holding not only vast amounts of personal data about those playing games(mostly children,it is assumed),but also information about their gaming habits and about purchases (whether by the players themselves or their parents),NT would have to ensure that this data is held securely,which is likely to increase the costs of a move into the computer games industry. There is also the threat from computer viruses to consider,which could corrupt and render useless the data NT holds. The threats posed by hackers and viruses raise legal considerations,especially if stolen or corrupted data relates to individuals.NT could face legal action if the measures in place for protecting data were deemed insufficient Threat-cost Because of the variety of information which NT could capture-from games,from social media-there would need to be in place appropriate ways of collecting,storing and processing it to ensure that NT gained value from it.The company may therefore have to upgrade its IT hardware and software,quite possibly at significant cost and potentially on a regular basis,as well as bearing the cost of acquiring and training staff with the necessary skills to utilise and manage the system effectively,assuming that those skills will be readily available.Given the size of the potential cost,NT would need to ensure that the investment provided an adequate return. Threat-data overload Given that NT has no current experience in big data,management could spend time trying to analyse it at the same time as seeking to diversify the business and move into a new industry,which of itself is likely to put pressure on management and risks them not focusing sufficiently on current business areas. NT should also bear in mind that simply having lots of data does not mean that it would be useful or that there will be patterns or trends which could be used commercially,although given the nature of computer games this is unlikely.Alternatively,its veracity may be low,due to hidden biases or inconsistencies,which would severely affect its usefulness.For example,were legislation to be introduced to limit the screen time of young players,this could affect the veracity of data on when and why players leave games. This information should be used to answer the tasks within your chosen response option(s). 1.An overview Nola Toys Ltd (NT)is a market-leading toy manufacturer established by Kirk Nola 50 years ago. Kirk Nola designed a building brick for children which he called the Nola Brick.The bricks encouraged children to play imaginatively and became a global bestseller.The unique design of the brick and its name are strictly protected through patents and trademarks. Following the Nola Brick’s success,Kirk felt no need to design any other toys,and the Nola Brick range remains NT's only product.The bricks are sold in packs which allow models to be built, including houses,castles and space rockets. Kirk Nola retained full ownership of NT until 18 months ago,when he retired and gave each of his three children one-third of NT's shares.Kirk's children had no interest or involvement in NT and they recently sold all their shares to Z-Co (ZC),an institutional investor in the leisure and media industries,for a substantial sum of money. ZC's main interest in purchasing NT was to obtain the Nola Brick trademark and brand.ZC believes that the trademark could be used for computer games,theme parks,television programmes and films.Three months ago,ZC dismissed NT's chief executive officer(CEO)and appointed a replacement who was instructed to fully exploit the value contained in the Nola Brick brand via diversification. You are an external adviser engaged by ZC to support the new CEO and the other members of the board. It is currently June 20X3. 2.NT website extracts Website last update:17 January 20X1 OUR STORY Our headquarters building,which looks as if it is made of Nola Bricks (as do all our buildings), is still in the village in Feeland where Kirk Nola had his first workshop.And thanks to Kirk Nola's hard work and perseverance -the popularity of Nola Bricks continues.We have won numerous awards over the years,including the prestigious Toy of the Year award.Celebrities post photographs of their children playing with Nola Bricks on social media.Customers who played with Nola Bricks when they were young are now buying them for their children and grandchildren. OUR CORE VALUES QUALITY -We believe children deserve only the best quality products. PLAY -Play can change the world and we encourage everyone to do it. FOCUS AND PRUDENCE -We focus on what we do best -providing children with imaginative play -and aim for sustainable growth,not growth at any cost. INFORMED INNOVATION - Rather than carefree creativity we adopt informed innovation, informed by our customers rather than by shareholder demands. LOYALTY AND PRIDE -We are loyal to the ideals of our founder,to our history and our values and we are proud of who we are and what we do. PEOPLE POLICY We want Nola Toys to be a great place to work for everyone.Our staff like working here as we have consistently been awarded Excellent Workplace status. We want our staff to work well and play well.Our recruitment process is based on candidates' ability to play and use their imagination. Our staffare vital to our reputation for quality and high levels of customer satisfaction.Our reward system includes an annual bonus payable to all staff -including directors and senior management-based on two key performance indicators(KPIs),which are customer satisfaction and zero defects. We are committed to paying our staff at above market rates and provide an extensive benefits package for all staff,irrespective of their role. Our staff are encouraged to participate in our renowned and successful Global Play Initiative, which was established by our founder and,with the support of a range of non-governmental organisations(NGOs),aims to provide educational and imaginative play for children worldwide,but particularly those in crisis settings. 3.Intranet posts from June 20X3 Let your colleagues know how you feel about working at NT by posting on this area of the intranet.Don't worry,your posts are anonymous. 'NT has always been a really nice place to work.I love our annual Day of Play when we have a day off to play like children.I suppose that won'thappen now we have a new CEO and a new ' owner. 'I hope they don’t change the look and layout of our offices.I really like the bright primary colours,the play zones,even senior management sitting next to us in the open plan areas.' I Reply ‘Any changes will be to ensure the long-term success of NT,which means we will have more job security.Surely that is more important than the colour of the offices?' 'I trusted Kirk Nola,I think we all did.I admit he made all the decisions;he didn’t like being disagreed with,especially on issues relating to the Nola Brick brand.But he never made a wrong decision in my opinion.He made the company great.' 'I’m sure we all remember Anders Hansen,the previous operations director.He disagreed with Kirk Nola,he said we should be changing how we did things,we should introduce new products, that children wanted more than bricks,that times had changed.He became so unpopular after that,he left.But he would probably like to work for zC.' 'I've heard that they are going to break up our departments -design, logistics,sales,marketing.I like that we have clear departmental boundaries, that we are in specialised departments.The new CEO doesn’t understand how things work here;he doesn'tunderstand what has made NT so successful.' I Reply 'Actually,the opposite is true -companies that ZC have invested in have really grown and flourished with their involvement.' 'I think Zc and the new CEO want to change everything.I like how things are.We are supposed to be loyal to Kirk's values and our history.I will not support any of the changes and I’II make sure no one in my team does either.' 4.Computer games industry information Background on the computer games industry Of the two billion computer game players (gamers)worldwide,18%are children(who are the main users of NT products).In 20X2,the industry generated $190 billion in revenue and analysts expect it will generate $260 billion by 20X5. Success relies on reputation and brand recognition,innovation,aggressive marketing and on-time product launches. Competition in the industry is extremely high.A small number of large global organisations hold up to 75%market share of revenue in most key markets.The only potential opportunity for NT is to be a small,niche provider. Games can cost tens of millions of dollars to develop and similar amounts to market.Many fail.Cancelled projects,unanticipated long delays,cost overruns and unexpected quality problems are very common within the industry. A decade ago,75%of computer games were sold as physical copies (e.g.CDs).In the last 12 months,this has reduced to below 20%,with over 80%of games now being sold in digital form (meaning that once the game is downloaded over the internet,it is immediately ready to play). Current issues in the industry The computer game industry is a target for cybercrime,putting at risk players'sensitive personal data. The prevalence of social media and online reviews means that a few poor ratings from influential gamers can cause a game to fail within weeks of its release. Many digital games allow gamers to play for free.However,revenue can be generated by encouraging players to make purchases whilst playing free games.These in -game purchases,which enable players to enhance their experience or get an advantage over other players,are known as microtransactions.Additional revenue can also be generated from advertisements placed in digital games,but these can irritate players if they are irrelevant in content or excessive in nature. In some key computer game markets,concerns about ‘addiction' to computer games has led to government interventions to limit usage.Psychologists have claimed that warning messages and notifications are not enough,and the industry needs to design games responsibly. 5.Financial appraisal Financial appraisal of a move into the the computer games industry Prepared by Finance Assistant June 20X3 Note Year 0 Year 1 Year 2 Year 3 Year 4 Year 5 Sm Sm Sm Sm Sm Sm Revenue 1 51.30 51.30 51.30 51.30 51.30 Contribution 2 12.83 12.83 12.83 12.83 12.83 other costs 3 -6.90 -6.90 -6.90 -6.90 -6.90 Pre-tax cash flows 5.93 5.93 5.93 5.93 5.93 Taxation 4 -1.48 -1.48 -1.48 -1.48 -1.48 Post-tax cash flows 4.44 4.44 4.44 4.44 4.44 lnvestment 5,6 -22.60 3.60 Cash flows -22.60 4.44 4.44 4.44 4.44 8.04 Discount factor 7 1.00 0.93 0.86 0.79 0.74 0.68 Discounted cash flows -22.60 4.11 3.81 3.53 3.27 5.47 NPV -2.41 Note 1 Based on the average market share of other niche suppliers of computer games and the strength of the NT brand,anticipated market share of the computer games market is 0.027%. 2 Based on the average contribution to sales ratio of five computer games companies with revenue under$75 million per annum. 3 Principally marketing costs,based on our current ratio to sales. 4 Taxation at a rate of 25%is paid on pre-tax cash flows. 5 Based on average initial investments in non-current assets of five computer games companies with revenue under$75 million per annum. 6 Estimated realisable value of non-current assets at the end of NT’s investment appraisal timeframe. 7 Discount factor is NT's weighted average cost of capital. 6.Value-adding activities HOW DO WE ADD VALUE TO OUR NOLA TOYS CUSTOMERS? Business area The value-adding activities involved Design . We limit the costs of innovation, not the generation of ideas.After our creative team has generated ideas,only those ideas which can be designed within rules limiting the creation of new colours,shapes and materials are progressed.By controlling the number of parts,ensuring they are inexpensive to source and using a standard design,we are agile and can react more quickly to consumer trends.The creative team tests new ideas on customers and encourages them to submit ideas and be part of our innovation process Manufacturing Manufacturing facilities are close to core markets,which means Nola Bricks are on shop shelves quickly and we can respond at speed to last-minute demands.Our operations are effi cient- we use the most advanced manufacturing technology with high levels of automation. Planning and forecasting We work with large retailers to forecast demand levels to ensure product availability, particularly at peak sales times such as the run up to festivities and celebrations or when new products are launched. Sales,marketing and brand management Our brand is protected using patents and trademarks.We operate branded stores to create immersive brand experiences for customers and have invested innovatively in our online presence such that 25%of our sales are now through our website.We deal with a limited number of retailers to minimise costs. After sales service We collect feedback from our customers at every opportunity to ensure we deliver an exceptional experience whenever they interact with us. Logistics We have a very successful sustainable outsourcing partnership with a global distribution company which is responsible for our inbound and outbound logistics,storage and packing,in line with our focus on our core competencies. 7.Potential use of big data REPORTTHE USE OF BIG DATA IN THE COMPUTER GAMES INDUSTRY 1JUNE 20X3 Prepared for the board of NT By IT Consultant 1 Introduction This report considers some of the uses of big data generated by digital computer games. 2 Potential uses of big data Big data generated by a digital computer game can be used to measure,predict and track gamers' behaviour. -It can provide insight into how and when gamers are playing and who they are playing with, their levels of engagement and when they are leaving games and not returning. -It can indicate where there are problems in the game,where gamers find it hard to complete tasks or where the tasks are too easy. - Interactions between gamers(game chats)can be saved and tracked for complaints and recurring issues. 3 Example of how big data is being used The approach is already being used by a major streaming service.It uses big data and analytics to: -Inform decisions on which shows to invest in. 一 Personalise customers' screens with recommendations based on their viewing preferences,thereby supporting customer retention. - Conduct custom marketing('trailers' for new shows)based on what customers have watched previously -for instance,customers who have watched romantic shows will get a trailer focused on the love interest in an upcoming show. 4 Conclusion If NT is to develop a computer game (or computer games)inspired by Nola Bricks,the big data generated could therefore provide solutions to a range of current computer industry issues which the company could face. Corjetz headline results and cost breakdown 1 育 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 1 1.Introduction Corjetz is a low-cost regional airline business.It was established 2o years ago in Corland,a country located in the continent of Hundria.Over the years,it has positioned itself as one of the world's leading low-cost airlines and is currently the second largest low-cost airline business operating in Hundria(in terms of passenger numbers). Corjetz currently operates nearly 1,00o routes(destinations)in over 30 countries across Hundria. Its head office is based at Near City airport,located in the south of Corland.Hundria is a politically stable and economically developed continent of the world.Most of the countries in Hundria form a trading bloc which facilitates free trade and free movement of people within it.Each country in 批注[2]: 介绍 批注[3]: Corjetz 是一家低成本的区域航空公司。它于20 年前在位于Hundria大陆的Corland 成立。多年来，它已 将自己定位为世界领先的低成本航空公司之一，目前是在 Hundria 运营的第二大低成本航空公司(基于乘客数 量 ) 。 Hundria uses its own currency.Corland's currency is the $. Corjetz operates a ‘point-to-point'service,meaning that it operates its flights directly between destinations.This means that its passengers do not have to travel to or change flights at a ^hub' airport to reach their destination,which helps Corjetz to reduce its operating costs.It also keeps costs low through its 'no-frills'approach to flight delivery.This approach means that in-flight meals are not provided(unless paid for separately)and includes the automation of a wide range of its processes,including bookings,flight documentation,baggage drop and flight check-in. Additionally,it operates a profitable and popular portfolio of routes between over 130 airports across Hundria. 2.Airline industry information Overview The airline industry encompasses a wide range of businesses called airlines which offer an air transport service to customers.Normally referred to as commercial airlines,these businesses offer scheduled commercial flights which carry both human passengers and cargo, and in most cases are carried out using jet aircrafts.Commercial flights are those which transport passengers from one destination to another with a pre-booked ticket.Non-commercial flights are either operated privately or by the military. The airline industry forms just one sector of the wider aviation industry, which also includes all other aviation-related businesses,such as jet engine suppliers,aircraft manufacturers and aerospace companies. The airline industry directly and indirectly supports global trade,tourism,and economic growth. Therefore,it has had a major influence on globalisation in many other industries. Air traffic control(ATC) Air traffic control(ATC)is a vital aspect of air transportation.It is carried out by ground-based personnel specially trained in monitoring air traffic in a specific location.ATC is responsible for moving aircraft safely and efficiently through the airspace system.Air traffic controllers ensure that aircraft remain at set distances apart whilst moving them between airports along set flight paths. Customer service management -including gate assignment,and pre-flight facilities management(such as operating shops and waiting areas in airports). 4 批注[23]: 出行方式对比： 中转式出行 (Hub and spoke)和点对点 (point-to-point) 出行 批注[24]:航空公司如果采用的是中转式出行(hub and spoke model),那么是从出发地先飞到中转机场 (hub airport), 然后再到目的地。采用这个模式，可以使飞行 次数增加，同时优化航空公司的网络覆盖范围。 批注[25]:航空公司如果采用的是点对点出行(point-to- point model), 即提供从出发地直接到最终目的地的航 线。那么就不需要中转机场(hub airport) 了，而是直接 从出发地城市到目的地城市。事实证明，这种模式近年来 深受欢迎，而且大部分的廉价航空公司采用这种模式。 批注[26]: 20X2年航空运输的关键数据 批注[27]:每年的乘客数量 批注[28:平均每天的商务航班数量 批注[29]:客运收入 批注[30]:飞行管理活动 飞行管理活动由机场提供。航空公司支付相关费用给机场。 批注[31]: 空中交通管制 空中交通管制 (ATC) 是航空运输的一个重要方面。指的 是受过专门训练的地面人员在特定地点进行监测，保证飞 机安全高效地通过空域系统。这些人员会观测飞机是否沿 着设定的飞行线路在移动，飞机之间是否保持了安全距 离 。 批注[32]: 地勤服务 地勤服务包括在地面为乘客提供的服务，以及在飞行起飞 前、降落后的其他地面服务。 具体包括： 批注[33]:-行李管理-包括分拣乘客行李以及将行李上 下飞机。 批注[34]:-客户服务管理-包括登机口分配和飞行前设 施管理(比如经营商店和机场等候区)。 - Aircraft boarding management -including operating shuttle bus services between the airport and the aircraft,ramp facilities and passenger travel documentation management. Aircraft towing and re-fuelling services -including movement of an aircraft to or from a parking stand or from one gate to another and the re-fuelling of aircrafts using fuel purchased by the airline. Airport security-including pre-flight passenger screening and security checks,baggage scanning,passport control and all on-site security management activities carried out to protect airport buildings,runways,and machinery. Industry challenges Demand is cyclical,with demand for flights driven by economic growth.In the last ten years, low-cost carriers have increasingly taken market share from full-service carriers by offering passengers affordable and low-cost air travel.This has made air travel more accessible to a much wider customer base globally.It has also introduced a high level of competition to the industry. The airline industry has been impacted by several geopolitical events in recent years,such as terror attacks,cyber-attacks and extreme weather events caused by climate change.These have led to both short and long-term consequences for demand.Terror and cyber-attacks have impacted on customer confidence in the industry and climate change is increasingly prominent on the global political agenda. Fuel is one of the biggest costs for airlines,and one of the most volatile.Fuel represents over 20% of most airlines'cost base annually.The price of jet fuel is strongly correlated to the international price of crude oil which has been highly volatile in the last five years.Fuel costs are particularly prone to foreign exchange movements,which can also have a significant impact on this major The airline industry is still some way away from operating aircraft with alternative fuels such as biofuels,and most airlines are still fully reliant on crude oil.Governments and customers are becoming increasingly environmentally sensitive and the expectation for airlines to operate in a‘greener'way is increasing.If the airline industry is to survive in the long term,it will need to invest significantly in alternative fuel sources. Environmental factors are a major and increasingly significant issue for the airline industry, affecting both passenger demand and operating parameters.Customers are increasingly aware that their carbon footprint from airline travel is contributing around 3%of global carbon emissions. Therefore,many are actively considering alternatives to air travel. Despite environmental concerns,commercial international air traffic(aircraft operating in the skies)increased by 5%in 20X2.According to the latest industry data,the international air traffic control environment is struggling to cope with increasing flight numbers,evidenced by the number of delayed flights rising by nearly 20%in 20X2.Similarly,airports around the world are struggling to cope with demand from airlines for flight slots¹.This is leading to increased congestion at airports,resulting in more delays for passengers.This is also driving up aircraft ground handling service charges for all airlines. 1A flight slot is the authorisation to either land or take off at a particular airport on a particular day during a specified time period.Flight slots are paid for by the airlines. 批注[35]:-飞机登机管理-包括摆渡车服务、停机坪设 施和旅客乘机的相关文件管理。 批注[36]:-飞机牵引和加油服务-包括飞机在停机位或 登机口之间的移动，以及使用航空公司购买的燃料为飞机 加油。 批注[37]:-机场安全-包括飞行前对乘客进行的安全检 查、行李扫描、护照检查以及其他安全管理活动，比如保 护机场建筑、跑道和机器等。 批注[38]: 行业挑战 批注[39]:需求是周期性的，航班需求是由经济增长驱动 的。在过去的十年中，低成本航空公司通过提供廉价和低 成本的乘客航空旅行，从全服务航空公司抢走了越来越大 的市场份额。这使得航空旅行在全球范围内获得了更广泛 的客户基础。它还为行业带来了高水平的竞争。 批注[40]:近年来，航空业受到一些地缘政治事件的影 响，如恐怖袭击、网络袭击和气候变化造成的极端天气事 件。这些因素从短期和长期来看都给需求造成了影响。恐 怖和网络攻击影响了客户对行业的信心，气候变化在全球 政治议程上日益突出。 批注[41]:燃料对于航空公司来说是最大的成本项目之一， 也是波动最大的因素之一，它占大多数航空公司每年成本 的20%以上。航空燃料价格与国际原油价格格密切相关， 而国际原油价格在过去五年中波动很大。燃料成本特别容 易受到外汇流动的影响，这也可能对这一主要成本产生重 大影响。 批注[42]:航空业仍然未能使用生物燃料等替代燃料来运 营，大多数航空公司仍然完全依赖原油。而政府和消费者 对环境越来越敏感的当下，人们对航空公司以“绿色”方 式运营的期望也在增加。如果航空业要长期生存下去，就 需要对替代燃料进行大量投资。 批注[43]:对于航空业来说，环境因素是一个日益重要的 大问题，它既影响乘客需求，也影响运营参数。客户越来 越意识到，他们从航空旅行中产生的碳足迹约占全球碳排 放的3%。因此，许多人正在积极考虑航空旅行的替代方 案。 批注[44]:尽管存在环境问题，商务性的国际空中交通 (空中飞行的飞机)在20×2年增长了5%。根据最新的行 业数据，国际空中交通管制环境正在努力应对不断增加的 航班数量，20x2 年航班延误数量增加了近20%。同样，世 界各地的机场都在努力应对航空公司对flight slots的需求 。这导致机场拥堵加剧，更多乘客延误。这也推高了所 有航空公司的飞机地勤服务费用。 5 批注[45]: A flight slot是在某个指定的期间，某个机场在某 一天对起飞或者落地的批准。航空公司需要为此付费。 Industry key performance indicators(KPIs) Airlines use multiple performance metrics to measure and monitor their performance. Some examples are provided in the table below:| KPI 1.Available seat kilometres(ASK) 2.Revenue per available seat kilometre(RASK) 3.Load factor 4.Revenue passenger kilometres(RPK) 5.COz per passenger (grams) 6.Punctuality Definition ASK is calculated by taking the number of seats available and multiplying by the distance flown.It is a measure of airline capacity. RASK is calculated by dividing the airline's total revenue by its total ASK.It is a measure of potential revenue generation. Calculated as the percentage of checked-in passengers in relation to an aircraft's available seats.It is a measure of utilisation. RPK is calculated as the number of passengers multiplied by the total distance travelled.It shows the number of kilometres travelled by paying passengers who are referred to as 'revenue passengers'. Calculated as total COz produced by the airline divided by the number of passengers. The percentage of flights which land on time at the destination airport.Typically,this means landing within 15 minutes of the scheduled arrival time. Industry bodyl The airline industry has a global trade association.Its role is to help formulate industry policy and support airline activity.It promotes awareness of the benefits which the airline industry brings to national and global economies.Its role is also to develop global standards for the air transport industry,and it provides advice on airline safety and security by setting clearly defined rules. 3.Corjetz overview Background Corjetz was established in Corland 20 years ago by entrepreneurial brothers Eric and Axel Madson.They both had worked for many years in the airline industry and had a keen interest in travelling.With money inherited from their parents,they acquired two second-hand aircraft.They started by operating flights from Near City airport,located 40 miles from Corland’s capital city in the south of the country,to airports located close to two cities in the north of the country. Using their knowledge and experience of the airline industry,Eric and Axel spotted a gap in the market in Corland for more convenient and affordable low-cost air travel.Up to that point, Corland's airline industry was dominated by full-service carriers. The low-cost business model in the Corland airline industry proved extremely popular with air passengers,who liked the convenience of Corjetz's point-to-point service and the significantly cheaper ticket prices than those offered by existing full-service carriers. Over the years,Corjetz has focused on operating flights to and from airports across Hundria with the highest customer demand and continually looks for opportunities to extend its network.Cost 批注[46]: 行业关键业绩指标 (KPls) 批注[47]:航空公司使用多种绩效指标来衡量和监控其绩 下表提供了一些例子： 批注[48]: ASK的计算方法是将可用的座位数乘以飞行距 离。这是衡量航空公司运力的一个指标。 批注[49]: RASK的计算方法是将航空公司的总收入除以总 ASK。这是衡量潜在收入的一个指标。 批注[50]:按已办理登机手续的乘客占飞机可用座位的百 批注[51]:RPK 的计算方法是乘客数乘以总飞行距离。它显 示了付费乘客所飞行的公里数，这些付费乘客被称为“收 入乘客”。 批注[52]:每位乘客碳(排放量)(克数)的计算方法为 航空公司产生的二氧化碳总量除以乘客数量。 批注[53]:准时，是航班准时到达目的地机场的百分比， 通常为在预定到达时间的15分钟内着陆。 批注[54]: 行业机构 批注[55]:航空业有一个全球性的行业协会。它的作用是 帮助制定行业政策和支持航空公司的活动。它提高人们对 航空业给国家和全球经济带来的好处的认识。它的作用还 在于为航空业制定全球标准，并通过制定明确规定的规 则，就航空安全和安保提供咨询意见。 批注[56]: Corjetz 公司的背景信息 批注[57]:20年前，企业家Eric和 Axel Madson兄弟在 Corland 创立了Corjetz。他们都曾在航空业工作多年，而 且非常喜欢旅行。他们用从父母那里继承来的钱买了两架 二手飞机。他们从一个南部城市 Near City 机场开始运营航 班 ，Near City距离 Corland的首都40英里，航班 从南部飞往靠近该国北部两个城市的机场。 批注[58]:利用他们在航空业的知识和经验，Eric和Axel 发现了Corland 在更方便和廉价的航空旅行方面的空白市 场。在此之前，Corland的航空业由提供全面服务的航空 公司统治。 批注[59]:Corland航空业的低成本商业模式在航空乘客中 非常受欢迎，他们喜欢Corjetz的点对点服务的便利性和 比现有的全方位服务航空公司更便宜的机票价格。 efficiency has been its overriding strategic focus.This has been delivered through developing and maintaining long-term strategic partnerships with aircraft manufacturers,key airports,ground handling operators and maintenance contractors who maintain Corjetz's owned aircrafts,and by operating the latest and most efficient aircraft. Fleet_ Corjetz operates a modern fleet of 300 aircraft,of which 75%are owned outright and the remaining 25%are leased (leasing is a common form of financing in the airline industry).Corjetz has invested significantly in the latest aircraft which are more fuel efficient and environmentally friendly.This has contributed significantly to its low-cost strategic position and has also positively impacted on reducing carbon emissions.Corjetz's average aircraft is 4·5 years old,which is low compared to the industry standard. Corjetz focuses on leisure passengers rather than on business passengers,therefore its aircraft cabins consist of economy class seats only with a high-density layout(this gives Corjetz 150 seats per aircraft compared to 140 seats on similar sized aircraft operated by its competitors). This higher seat density allows Corjetz to offer lower ticket prices per flight which is attractive to customers. Technology Corjetz applies data analytics extensively throughout its business and considers data to be a key business resource.It has always been an early adopter of the latest technologies in the airline industry.It currently makes extensive use of technology throughout its business operations, including:_ -A mobile app which allows passengers to book flights,check in for flights,download boarding passes(to either print off or store as a mobile boarding pass on the passenger's smartphone)and receive real-time flight status information.The app significantly reduces the cost of issuing,distributing and processing physical tickets.An enhanced feature of the mobile app is the integration of live data from the airport's information systems with the passenger's booking and flight details,allowing personalised instructions and updates to be sent to passengers before their journey. In most airports Corjetz operates fully automated baggage check-in machines to help reduce check-in queues. All aircraft are fitted with electronic touchpads for the crew,to replace laptops and printed navigational charts for pilots.This has replaced the need for each flight to carry heavy printed flight logbooks which has helped to reduce COz emissions per flight. All Corjetz's aircraft are fitted with big data servers to collect continuous in-flight data about flight and engine performance. 8 批注：Corjetz拥有盈利且广泛的航线组合，往返于 Hundri130 多个机场。这使得它非常受客户欢迎，客户们 发现 Corjetz 航班提供的的目的地范围和飞行频率非常方 便、而且具有吸引力。它定期审查其航线网络，以利用最 新的需求机会。 批注：机队 批注：Corjetz运营着一支由300架飞机组成的现代化机 队，其中75%是完全自有的，其余25%是租赁的 (租赁是航空业的一种常见融资形式)。Corjetzz 大量投 资最新飞机，因为燃油效率更高，对环境更友好。这对有 助于低成本战略，而且对于减少碳排放发挥正面作用。 Corjetz 飞机的平均使用年限为4.5年，与行业标准相比， 这是较低的。 批注：Corjetz专注于休闲乘客而不是商务乘客，因此，其 飞机客舱仅由高密度布局的经济舱座位组成(这让 Corjetz每架飞机有150个座位，而其竞争对手运营的类似 尺寸飞机的座位数是140个)。这种更高的座位密度使 Corjetz 能够提供更低的票价，这非常吸引客户。 批注：技术 批注：Corjetz 在其整个业务中广泛应用数据分析，并将数据 视为关键业务资源。它一直是航空公司最新技术 的早期采用者。它目前在其业务运营中广泛使用技术，包 括 ： 批注：一个移动应用程序，允许乘客预订，办理登机手 续，下载登机牌(打印或作为移动登机牌存储在乘客的智 能手机里),并接收实时状态信息。该应用程序明显减少 签发、分发和处理实体机票的成本。这个移动应用程序的 乘客的预订和航班信息整合，允许个性化说明和更新， 在乘客旅行前发送给他们。 批注：在大多数机场，Cojetz 都设有全自动行李托运机， 以帮助减少签到队列。 批注：所有飞机都为机组人员配备了电子触摸板，以取代 笔记本电脑和印刷版飞行员导航图。这已经取代了每趟航 班都要携带沉重的印刷版航海日志的需要，帮助每趟航班 减少二氧化碳排放量。 批注：Corjetz的所有飞机都配备了大数据服务器，以收 集飞行中数据和发动机性能。 Corjetz continually searches for new technologies to enhance and support its business operations,to improve efficiency and reduce costs.People_Corjetz employs nearly 11,800 staff,including over 2,000 pilots and 6,500cabin crew.The remaining staff fulfil a number of roles,including staff operating at airports and head office staff operating in IT,finance,customer services and human resourcemanagement.Regular updates are provided to employees on key business issues which include a regularly updated intranet,a monthly‘meet the board'online chat,and a weekly staffnewsletter.All Corjetz employees undergo a full induction programme and have access to online learning resources,as well as career development opportunities.
