Threats Public and private sector collaborations often fail.Therefore,the first potential threat is that should the programme be perceived by the participants and/or the wider public as a failure,it could damage our reputation. There is also a potential threat that current members may be adversely affected by this proposal,in that qualified fitness instructors will be absent from our clubs during the times when they are delivering the programme within schools.This may affect the quality of our members'experience in the gym or in the fitness classes they attend. This programme is likely to be incredibly time consuming for our staff,who will be expected to not only deliver this programme but also willbe responsible for monitoring and reporting in the performance of children taking part.Most will not be used to working in such an environment.Extra training willbe needed and presumably we will need to ensure that our own gyms and fitness classes are adequately covered when staff are working on the programme.It may be difficult to source enough temporary staff to support this and the cost may also be high.There is also a potential threat that our current instructors may not be happy to work on this programme as they may feel unprepared or under pressure.Staff may leave, rather than be forced to take part in this programme,which may then leave us under-resourced. A further threat is that the Department of Health is likely to have different overall objectives for this programme to Optima.Its focus will be on the measurable improvements to the health and fitness of the children participating.For Optima,our objective will of course be the same, but in addition we must also consider the impact of the programme on our long-term business reputation and its impact on profitability.Our investors willwant to be reassured that our focus remains on being profitable. There willalso be challenges in managing the partnership.It is clear from the current outline proposal that the mechanisms suggested to measure performance have not been fully finalised and I would suggest that we need to be involved in the agreement and finalisation ofany performance measures to ensure that we are in agreement as to how these will operate and how our own performance willbe measured.It is going to be extremely difficult to establish the direct impact of our participation in the programme,as its success will be influenced by many external factors,particularly as our staff will only be in contact with children for short periods of time over the duration of the programme.Therefore,we must establish how the Department of Health intends to measure the overall success of the programme and how they intend to measure our impact on that.It is likely that any direct outcomes will only be confirmed in the long term,certainly beyond the timeframe of the programme.We must 批注[67]:提出观点+解释观点：relationship with government 批注[68]:提出观点+解释观点：raise profile with driven customers and potential investors. 批注[69]:解释观点：为什么会损害企业名誉 批注[70]:总结观点： damage reputation 批注[71]:提出观点： affect on current members 批注[72]:解释观点：项目为什么会损害现有客户 批注[73]:提出观点： time consuming for our staff 批注[74]:解释观点：为什么会耗时? 批注[75]:提出观点：员工不适应，需要培训。 批注[76]:观点+解释：没有足够人员来既满足项目又满足 企业自身的需求 evidence在 exhibit3 批注[77]:观点+解释：员工不愿意参加 not happy+为什么不 愿意unprepared or pressure+会造成什么威胁/结果 leave us under-resourced. 批注[78]:提出观点：目标不一致 different objective 批注[79]:解释观点：政府和企业的目标分别是什么。 Government:improvements to the health and fitness of the children participating; Optima:same as government+reputation+profitability evidence在 exhibit 2 &exhibit 6第 1 & 2 段 批注[80]:提出观点：绩效机制未确定 批注[81]:对于观点给出建议：企业应该参与绩效制定 批注[82]:引申即便参与绩效制定，但是企业自身局限性是 什么。 真题解析2021-SepDec 20 ensure that our own reputation is not damaged by inappropriate short-term measures which are not reflective of our own input into the programme. (b)Value for money(VFM) VFM principles focus on the three aspects of economy’,‘efficiency'and ‘effectiveness'. Economy focuses on delivering services within budget and within the resource constraints. Efficiency focuses on achieving an acceptable return on the resources invested in a service (that is,getting as much as possible from the amount put into a service).Effectiveness focuses on the degree to which the organisation has delivered what it intended to deliver. As a public sector organisation,the Department of Health is funded by the government of Ceeland and will be tightly controlled in what it can do and how it spends the money which it has been allocated.As it is funded by public money,it must be efficient in the resources it has been allocated and be strategically effective in the delivery of the objectives it has been set. This VFM focus may create challenges for Optima in participating in the delivery of the B-Fit programme.In terms of ‘economy',our own business operates as a mid-to-high quality fitness provider and our strategy is one of differentiation,with a focus on quality rather than being a low-cost provider.However,the Department of Health's required focus on ‘economy' will be based on providing the highest quality for the most appropriate/lowest cost possible. Ensuring quality will be an important factor in this programme and while this should not be compromised,we must ensure that whatever we deliver is within the budget set($10 million). This equates to $500 per pupil.Therefore,we will need to carefully assess what we can realistically achieve over the six-month trial period which will achieve this budget level. In terms of ‘efficiency'in achieving an acceptable return on the resources invested in the programme,our current focus on efficiency may not be completely in line with the VFM approach of the Department of Health.As a private sector organisation,of course our shareholders willexpect that we do not waste resources and that we invest money to generate a return.However,our primary focus is on delivering top quality facilities and services and creating an excellent experience for our members.This may not always be in line with optimising efficiency and therefore we may not be fully aligned in our overall approach here with the Department of Health requirements. In terms of effectiveness,Optima's primary focus is on the delivery of excellence and achieving high levels of overall quality of services and facilities we offer.Currently,this is completely determined and influenced by Optima.However,the effectiveness of the outcomes ofthis programme willdepend on many other external factors and on the efforts of the children and schools taking part in the programme.Optima should not be held solely responsible for the effectiveness of the delivery of the programmes and the final outcomes,as we are only one factor in their achievement.Therefore,having only partial control of the effectiveness of the programme may be a challenge for Optima. 批注[83]:解释这些自身局限性会带来什么威胁 批注[84]:提出分析VFM 应该从3个方面进行 批注[85]:3个E 分别什么意思。给出定义满足题目要求。 批注[86]:给出总体挑战： resource has been allocated and strategically efffective 批注[87]:提出观点：经济上的挑战是什么。 企业：自身战略是差异化，关注质量而不是低成本 政府：既要便宜又要高质量 批注[88]:面对这个挑战，企业应该怎么做。仔细评估项 目在预算基础上找寻最高质量 数据来自Exhibit 6 S10million: 倒数第二段 ¥500 per pupil=S10million/20000(proposal requirement 第五 条 ) Six months:proposal requirement 第四条 批注[89]:提出观点，在效率上和政府有分歧 批注[90]:解释效率的分歧是什么?政府要什么样的效率， 而企业想要达到的效率是什么。 批注[91]:观点的解释，为什么企业对于这个项目结果不能 达到完全控制 evidence在 exhibit6 proposal requirement 和 performance review中 批注[92]:给出观点：对于项目结果企业不可控 真题解析2021-SepDec 21 As a private sector organisation,we may have some differing overall aims and objectives to those of the Department of Health and therefore we may not be completely aligned with all of the VFM principles driving this programme which we must take into consideration before progressing further. Conclusion This is a highly interesting proposal and one which Optima is well placed to deliver.There are several potential benefits to participation in the programme but we must fully consider the potential challenges which it may also create.It is recommended that we open up a dialogue with the Department of Health as soon as possible. 批注[93]:对于VFM 这一部分的总述，企业无法完全符合 VFM 批注[94]:跟 introduction 前后呼应 ○NLINE DELIVERY 真题解析2021-SepDec 22

3. 结合big data和 mobile technology相关知识内容，并联 系题目中案例，进行回答 真题解析2021-SepDec 23 Answer for Q3 FAO:Jerry Krantz,Finance director From:Senior corporate business analyst (a)Opportunities and threats of implementing big data analytics Opportunities Big data analytics could help us examine the data we already currently collect from members and create actionable information,especially that which relates to retaining our members. 1.Timely decisions We could identify patterns and trends which may give us better insight into our business,on a continual basis.One issue with the exit interviews which we undertake,as mentioned in the meeting by the marketing director,is that this information comes too late.If we had meaningful information which we analysed continually,we could know much earlier why a member would want to cancel and therefore be able to address this in advance.Therefore,the exit interviews highlighted by the marketing director,though useful to understand why members are leaving,may not help us to retain those specific members. 2.Understand behaviour and building loyalty By undertaking big data analytics, we can detect member behaviour and steer them in a direction which is best for them and for us.Perhaps a club has a member paying a premium price for an all-access membership who only uses the gym at weekends.By analysing their behaviour and usage patterns,we could encourage that member to switch to a gym member pass only.Having a more engaged member,even if it is at a lower price point,willbetter satisfy that member and therefore hopefully retain them.Ultimately,building loyalty is about giving people what they want and responding to the things they do not like.We can only know that ifwe collect and analyse a wide range of customer data continuously,as members' needs are changing continuously. 3.Better use of data held As highlighted by the marketing director,we collect a significant amount of information about our members,through surveys,emails and membership forms.Through big data analytics,we would use the information we have better.This could be done by connecting all of the member feedback and membership data in ways which allow us to better understand which clubs are doing well,what is keeping members engaged,what makes members loyal to a club and what the threats to that loyalty are(such as competition nearby or a change in club policy).Big data analytics is not about collecting more data,as suggested by the marketing director;it is about analysing better and more productively the information we already have. We must ensure that this data is held securely which,in turn,is likely to increase costs. 2.Cost The commercial director correctly mentioned the cost of big data analytics,It could cost a significant amount in software installation and in acquiring or training staff with the necessary skills to utilise and manage the system effectively.As we are not entirely sure of the return on such an investment and have no current expertise in big data analytics,costs may be significant without an adequate return.Therefore,we will need to undertake a detailed cost benefit analysis. 3.Data overload It would be easy to make the wrong decisions based on too much information.With no curr ent experience of big data analytics,management could spend time trying to determine patterns and trends in the data instead of concentrating on running the business.As the marketing director commented,having lots of data does not guarantee that it will be useful. A mobile app would allow us to build a sense of community and affiliation-even when members are not physically at the club.By being constantly present in members'lives,even when they are not on site at the club,allows us to actively engage with members and retain their interest in us. Q4 The senior management team of Optima has always placed emphasis on local autonomy, allowing local managers at each fitness and leisure club to make decisions which best suit their local members and market conditions.However,the finance director has received an internal auditors report on Optima's control activities which has identified several weaknesses at some of Optima's fitness and leisure clubs.The finance director has asked you to help him prepare a report which he will present at the next board meeting. Prepare a report for presentation at the next board meeting which evaluates the potential impact of the weaknesses which have been identified in Optima's internal control activities and recommends actions to address these weaknesses. (15 marks) Professional skills marks are available for demonstrating evaluation skills to objectively appraise the implications of the internal control weaknesses identified and to make well-reasoned recommendations for improvement.(3 marks) 批注[132]:题目中management team of Optima has always placed emphasis on local autonomy, 对照背景资料中给到的 Exhibits, 该背景资料中至少会给出管理层这么做的原因， 至少需要用到Exhibits 7.Exhibits 7中总结了各个部门资深 管理团队的会议发言。 批注[133]:看到 received an internal auditors report,对 照 背景资料中给到的Exhibits, 该题目回答至少需要用到 Exhibits 8 。Exhibits 8这份邮件总结了报告中发现的重大问 题，是关于企业内部控制的问题。 批注[134]:格式要求：报告 报告对象：Finance director 时间分配：15 marks*2.5=37.5 分 钟 考纲知识点：F1c 分数分配：15分 四大类内控缺陷，每一个内控缺陷得1分，针对内控缺陷 给出的建议得1分，每一大类内控缺陷得分点不超过2分， 建议不超过2分，总分不超过15分。 职业技能：评估准确度 针对报告中提出的内控问题，提出解决方案。职业技能分3 分。 真题解析2021-SepDec 28 Answer for Q4 To:Jerry Krantz,Finance director From:Senior corporate business analyst Date:10 September 2021 An evaluation of Optima's internal control activities Introduction The recent internal audit review of Optima's internal control activities has identified several weaknesses which need to be addressed.This report evaluates those weaknesses and makes several recommendations on how these may be addressed.The report willalso briefly consider the importance of the control environment in ensuring an effective system of internal control. Issue 1:Review of monthly performance reports The lack of review by 20%(or 11 of the 55)club managers of their monthly performance reports is a weakness in our management controls.Performance reporting is a critical aspect of the control environment and it is an expectation of all club managers to use these reports to monitor and manage their club's performance.Reviewing of performance reports is a key activity for all managers at all levels of the organisation and if not done,then poor performance can go unchecked without suitable corrective action.On a monthly basis,some issues may not seem significant,but if left unnoticed and unchecked over several months, these could become major operational issues.Control activities must be carried out by club managers based not only on their own in-depth knowledge and understanding of their club but also on the performance information they receive.Club managers are obviousy receiving performance information but are not using it,which demonstrates a weakness in their understanding of its importance to the operations of the whole business. Recommendation: All club managers should be required to report to a senior manager at head office or to a regional level manager on a monthly basis to demonstrate the actions they have taken in response to the performance report. Tolerances should be set for any variances in performance which must be reported on by club managers on a monthly basis to ensure that action is being taken where necessary.All actions required must be followed up by a senior manager at head office to ensure that issues are actively addressed. Issue 2.Unqualified staff Over 12%of the current fitness instructors employed are not appropriately qualified.Having such a high proportion of fitness instructors employed in our gyms across Ceeland without the relevant qualifications or without having undergone the correct induction and training is an example of weaknesses in our personnel controls.By employing unqualified and untrained staff,we run the risk that they could provide inappropriate advice or assistance to members which could,in turn,result in accidents and/or injury and consequently,potential litigation. This could have disastrous consequences for Optima's reputation. 批注[135]:Report 格式 批注[136]:涉及题目中材料：E8-P2, 该信息应有助于考生 把握案例细节并回答题目 每一个内控缺陷标注得1分，针对内控缺陷给出的建议得1 分，每一大类内控缺陷得分点不超过2分，建议不超过2 分 批注[137]:标注要点点明了相应的内控缺陷 批注[138]:标注要点回答了所对应内控缺陷给出的建议 批注[139]:涉及题目中材料：E8-P3, 该信息应有助于考生 把握案例细节并回答题目 每一个内控缺陷标注得1分，针对内控缺陷给出的建议得1 分，每一大类内控缺陷得分点不超过2分，建议不超过2 分 批注[140]:标注要点点明了相应的内控缺陷 真题解析2021-SepDec 29 Recommendation: Human resource staffat head office must authorise all appointments and must check all CVs and employment references before appointment commences,particularly if Optima engages in the B-Fit programme which may involve physical trainers working with school children.All staff must undertake the required interview process,inductions and training prior to commencement of their duties on site and this process must be fully documented and authorised by an appropriate member of the human resources department. Annual performance appraisals should also be reviewed by HR staff to ensure that actions relating to training needs identified are properly actioned.Additionally,staff should be encouraged to undertake continuous professional development(CPD). Issue 3. Security issues Unauthorised access and potential security breaches within nearly 15%(8 of 55)of clubs is an example of weaknesses in our physical controls.The physical controls we set up,in relation to access codes and security passes given to our members,have been breached on many occasions meaning that potential non-registered and non-authorised individuals are gaining access to our facilities.This not only leads to a loss of revenue but also poses serious security risks to both our members and our staff. The lack of action by the club managers who are aware of these weaknesses in the physical controls is a serious issue and a breach of management control,as they are aware of the control weakness but seemingly are not doing anything to rectify the issue. Recommendation: Our access and security systems must be improved immediately. Facilities such as member security pass scanners,security barriers or physical checks of members'passes by reception staff must be fully operational at all clubs.Access to areas must be restricted to designated pass holders only and these need to be randomly checked by staff on a daily basis.Club managers who know about these security weaknesses and who are not actioning them must be disciplined.Security systems should be tested regularly and any faults must be reported and actioned immediately. Issue 4.Approval and payment refurbishment work The organisation of the swimming pool refurbishment and the authorisation of the work without following the proper tendering process is a breach in the management controls and in the control of segregation of duties. The club manager should not have authorised this work themselves without following the company-wide tendering process.This breach of controls could be viewed as potential fraud and would certainly be viewed as quite unethical,since the work was carried out by a business owned by the club manager's husband.This could impact on the reputation of Optima,if this becomes public knowledge. Recommendation: The club manager should be disciplined and instructed on the proper tendering process which must be followed to undertake such work.All club managers must be made aware of their responsibilities and their authority limits in tendering for capital 批注[141]:标注要点回答了所对应内控缺陷给出的建议 批注[142]:涉及题目中材料：E8-P4, 该信息应有助于考生 把握案例细节并回答题目 每一个内控缺陷标注得1分，针对内控缺陷给出的建议得1 分，每一大类内控缺陷得分点不超过2分，建议不超过2 分 批注[143]:标注要点点明了相应的内控缺陷 批注[144]:标注要点回答了所对应内控缺陷给出的建议 批注[145]:涉及题目中材料： E8-P5, 该信息应有助于考生 把握案例细节并回答题目 每一个内控缺陷标注得1分，针对内控缺陷给出的建议得1 分，每一大类内控缺陷得分点不超过2分，建议不超过2 分 批注[146]:标注要点点明了相应的内控缺陷 真题解析2021-SepDec 30 investment projects. As well as offering a place for local to meet and socialise,NCCP offers four part-time courses: computers for beginners,website design,finding a job and business skills.[ Where relevant,the courses include a certificate of completion to provide participants with evidence to support job applications the courses are also of interest to those seeking an opportunity for social interaction.All participants pay a course fee which is heavily subsidised. The content of these courses has remained largely unchanged since they were set up. Richard continued to make substantial donations to NCCP until his retirement five years ago. Since then,the executive board has tried to seek out other sources of funding and now relies heavily on Ceeville Council which provides financial support for job-related training,however,this funding has not been sufficient to prevent NCCP from continuing to have financial difficulties. Does Yexmarine need a new direction? By Gerald Mynne, Yacht Owner magazine's chiej correspondent Visiting the Neoland national boat show last month,I spent some time in the cruising yachts area .The market for these yachts in Neoland has for some time been dominated by the ‘Big Three’local manufacturers,Janrom,Kelabby and Yexmarine. They have the resources that smaller competitors lack.Foreign companies have not,as yet,made a big impact in the Neoland market. What I found most interesting was that the three companies’ exhibition stands very much reinforced the impression I,and I am sure many others,have of them.Janrom's display emphasised its recent design innovations,which should mean its yachts reaching their destinations quicker and more smoothly than its competitors'yachts. Kelabby's stand emphasised comfort and style, Kelabby's stand emphasised comfort and style, even though its yachts are not luxury yachts.Its display showcased the new features which customers can choose. At Yexmarine's stand,its friendly staff engaged enthusiastically with the mostly aged over 50 yacht owners.Yexmarine attracts plenty of loyalty even though its yachts'performance is inferior to Janrom's yachts,and its range of additional features is more limited than Kelabby's.There are no recent engine or power developments like those introduced by Janrom, and Yexmarine's yachts look less fashionable than Kelabby's. At the boat show I spoke to Yexmarine's chief executive,Tony Bursham,while visiting Yexmarine's stand. Tony chatted enthusiastically about the company's planned move to a new site in Lympool.He was keen to stress that the move will allow it to introduce more automation in production,improving standardisation and efficiency.This willbe accompanied by changes in processes,business structure reorganisation and a quality management programme.Tony promised me that these changes will result in an improved product and service offering. (3 marks) 批注[54]:CEO正在起草成立董事会下属的一系列专业委员 会，其中第一个想要成立的是战略委员会。 CEO需要你的意见，关于他的这个提议的好处和存在的问 题。(如前所述，这里可以快速对应到材料3) 批注[55]:Letter格式 报告对象：Chief executive 时间分配：14marks*2.5=35分钟 考纲知识点：B5f 分数分配：14分 该提议的好处，每一个要点不超过2分，总分不超过6分； 该提议存在的问题，每一个要点不超过2分，总分不超过6 分；合计总分不超过14分。 职业技能：分析 考虑该提议的好处、以及存在的问题。职业技能分数3分。 真题解析2022-MarJun 13 Answer for Task 1 Tranart Consultants Anystreet Lympool 8 March 20X2 Dear Tony Establishment of strategy committee at Yexmarine Thank you for giving me the opportunity to comment on your proposal.I agree with you that there is a case for establishing a strategy committee,given the important strategic decisions which Yexmarine will need to take over the next few years and the need for reappraisal of governance structures if Yexmarine seeks a listing.Governance regulations will require the setting up of other board committees,for example,the audit committee,and you will need to consider how the strategy committee fits within the overall committee structure. Benefits of strategy committee Strategic planning The terms of reference you propose include strategic planning.Decision-making will be enhanced if investment projects are viewed in the light of plans to achieve long-term strategic objectives.Long lead times and the large capital expenditure required make it important for decisions on investments to fit within the strategic planning framework. Review of environment The terms of reference emphasise the importance of the committee actively seeking to be aware of environmental factors and particularly what competitors are doing.Consideration of these factors is very important in determining the suitability and feasibility of strategic options.If competitors are already established or planning a large investment in a particular market,Yexmarine may not succeed in that market or willneed to spend too much to make an impact in it. Consideration of strategic options Establishing a strategy committee should ensure that sufficient time is given to considering strategic options.Board meetings have to consider a range of issues,and this may limit the time available for a full discussion of strategy. Problems with strategy commttee Confusion of responsibilities Governance best practice states that certain decisions must be reserved for the main board,including decisions about major investments.This is to ensure proper board accountability.The proposal that the strategy committee should be responsible for choosing strategic options to be recommended for board approval may lead to confusion between the responsibilities of the committee and the board.It could also mean the board being required to act as a ‘rubber-stamp'for decisions made in this committee,which would limit the input of directors who are not members of the strategy committee. It would be more in line with governance best practice for the committee to evaluate strategic options and to then present them to the board for consideration and decision-making. 批注[56]: Letter 格式，寄信人地址(咨询公司)靠右对齐， 收信人地址省略。 批注[57]:信的主旨：关于成立战略委员会。 批注[58]:开门见山点明主旨，Yexmarine 公司面对的背景， 同意成立战略委员会。公司治理的监管规则要求上市公司 成立其它必要的专业委员会，公司需要考虑要成立的战略 委员会与公司整体的董事会架构相适应。 批注[59]:关于成立战略委员会可以带来的好处(材料3中 谈到的)。 批注[60]:有助于战略规划：你的提议谈到了战略规划。如 果投资项目的评估是根据否能有助于实现长期战略目标， 那么决策质量将会提升。巨额的资本支出和较长的等待期 都要求投资项目符合长远的战略规划框架。 批注[61]:有助于审核环境：你的提议强调了了解环境因素 尤其是竞争对手在做什么非常重要。考虑这些因素在帮助 决定战略选择是否恰当和是否能够执行方面非常重要。如 果竞争对手已经非常成熟或者正在规划针对特定市场的一 项大笔投资，Yexmarine很有可能无法在这个市场上获得成 功或者就不会在这个市场上投入过多资源。 批注[62]:有助于考虑战略选择：成立战略委员会可以确保 有足够的时间被用来考虑战略选择。董事会会议不得不考 虑各种问题，这有可能限制对战略讨论投入的时间。 批注[63]:关于成立战略委员会存在的问题(材料3当中没 有谈到但是也需要考虑的问题)。 批注[64]:对职责的迷惑：最佳治理实践认为某些特定的决 策是需要留给董事会来决定的，包括重大投资决策。提议 当中战略委员会负责挑选恰当的战略给董事会批准，这有 可能引起困扰，误以为董事会的作用就是对战略委员会的 决策“盖橡皮章”,这就会限制不是战略委员会成员的其 他董事对决策的投入程度。更符合最佳实践的做法是让战 略委员会评估战略选择、然后提交给整个董事会考虑并做 决策。 真题解析2022-MarJun 14 Role of non-executive directors Governance requirements also recommend the need for strategic proposals to be scrutinised and challenged.This is,in particular,the responsibility of non-executive directorsHowever,the proposed membership of the committee does not include any non-executive directors.Non-executive directors can bring wider perspectivesbased on their own experience,to strategic decision-taking and can also help ensure decisions are taken in the light of what is acceptable to key stakeholders. Frequency of meetings The terms of reference suggest that the frequency of meetings should be determined by the decisions which need to be taken.This can be a factor,but the committee's terms of reference also include environmental monitoring,which needs to be continuous.A minimum number of meetings each year, to ensure that regular consideration is given to environmental issues,should be specified,with one-off meetings being held if there is a particularly significant opportunity or threat arising from the environment which needs to be considered. Financial aspects There is no specific mention in the terms of reference to the committee considering the financial aspects of strategies.The finance director has not been included as a member of the committee.This may mean that financial aspects are given insufficient consideration,so you should consider whether the finance director should be a committee member.The terms of reference also should specify the need for the committee to consider financial aspects.This should be supported by specification of the financial information which the committee needs to consider. Links between corporate and operational strategy The terms of reference also fail to mention the links between corporate strategy and operational strategy.There may potentially be confusion about how much autonomy functional directors have in decision-making in the light of the overall strategic framework determined by the strategy committee. Human resources In addition,the terms of reference do not have anything about the committee considering internal resources and capabilities.Whilst members of the committee can provide assessment of the areas where they have operational responsibilities,the exclusion of the human resources director from the committee means there is a danger that personnel issues may not be considered.These may be particularly important in the light of the changes which employees will experience when they move to the new facility. I hope you find these comments constructive.Please do not hesitate to get in touch if you wish to discuss these points further. Yours sincerely Consultant 批注[65]:非执行董事的作用：治理规则也建议需要对战略 提议进行审核并提出质疑。这尤其是非执行董事的职责。 但是，提议中关于成员构成没有包括非执行董事。非执行 董事基于他们各自的经验，能够带来更广泛的视角，也能 够确保决策的制定是关键利益相关者可接受的。 批注[66]:会议的频率：你的提议当中建议会议的频率应当 按照要讨论的决策来制定(在探讨重大决策的时候应当更 频繁地开会)。这是一个要考虑的因素，但是，战略委员 会需要监管环境因素，这是一个持续的过程。应当规定每 年最少开多少次会议，来确保对环境因素进行定期评估： 如果有特定重大机会或威胁出现，可以召集一次性会议。 批注[67]:财务方面：你的提议当中没有具体提到战略的财 务方面，财务董事没有被包括在成员范围内。这可能意味 着财务方面没有给到足够的重视。因此，你需要考虑财务 董事是否应当成为一员。你的提议也应当指明战略委员会 需要考虑财务方面事宜。比如，可以指定战略委员会需要 考虑的财务信息的具体细节。 批注[68]:公司战略与运营战略之间的关系：你的提议没有 考虑这二者之间的联系。大家可能会有困惑，按照战略委 员会决定的整体战略框架，不同职能的董事们在决策上有 多大的自主权。 批注[69]:人力资源方面：你的提议没有谈到战略委员会对 内部资源和能力的考量。没有把人事董事纳入战略委员会， 意味着人事问题可能没有被考虑。(针对案例情境)尤其 是当搬到新总部，员工将要经历变革过程的时候，对人事 的考虑尤为重要。 真题解析2022-MarJun 15 Task 2 Yexmarine's chief executive is concerned about the results of the recent employee satisfaction survey. He feels that a lack of motivation from staff could jeopardise the success of the planned programme of changes.He believes that Yexmarine must fully utilise its employees'skills and experience and regards a high level of employee retention as a vital factor in his plans for expansion.The chief executive therefore believes that the board must address employees'concerns and reward them,as appropriate,for their contribution to introducing the proposed changes. (a )Prepare briefing notes for the board which advise how employee dissatisfaction may impact upon each stage of the organisational change process. (10 marks) Professional skills marks are available for demonstrating analysis skills by linking evidence about employee dissatisfaction to consequences for the organisational change process. The stages are getting employees prepared for changes in behaviour,processes and operations(unfreezing),the move to new premises itself(change)and the rapid settling down into the new situation (refreezing). Employee dissatisfaction could have consequences for each stage of this process. Unfreezing Employees'beliefs that their opinions will be ignored will hinder any process of consultation during this stage. Employees may be reluctant to offer opinions if they feel no notice will be taken of them, or if they fear that expressing opinions which are unpopular with management could be held against them.There also seems to be a risk that more experienced or specialist employees will leave the company.This will mean that there will be a lack of motivated employees who could lead the process of change and help convince other employees. Change The actual move to the new premises is likely to require employees making a commitment of time greater than their normal employment to ensure disruption is kept to a minimum.Employees may be unwilling to make ths extra commitment ifthey are not rewarded for it. This is also the time when new processes will be introduced.If employees have not tried to learn them properly or resent them,there is a risk that management could face active resistance from employees, increasing the level of disruption which the move will cause.At the extreme,management direction or coercion could be required.Whilst this may be effective in pushing through the changes,it may also be counter-productive,increasing employees'resistance and requiring further management time to resolve.Allof these could result in service to customers being disrupted. Refreezing Given the attachment of many employees to the current culture,there is a significant risk that they willrevert to old routines after the move has taken place.This will require management time to correct and is likely to increase resentment,particularly if they still regard Yexmarine's management as coming from a different culture.In turn,this could mean decreased operational efficiency due to significant employee turnover.As a result,Yexmarine may find it difficult to keep the promises the chief executive has made of improvement in service to customers. 批注[73]: Briefing notes格式，说明主旨。 批注[74]:根据题目要求，判断使用Lewin's three stage model回答(员工不满意)对企业变革流程每一个阶段的 影响。 批注[75]: Unfreezing stage: 员工如果认为他们的意见会被 忽视的话，将会阻碍与员工的咨询沟通的过程。最后带来 的后果(风险)可能是有经验的或者是有专门技术的员工 离职，这意味着原本有动力的员工可以领导变革过程并且 帮助说服其他员工，但是组织会失去他们。 批注[76]: Change stage: 1.搬到新总部可能要求员工比正常工作投入更多的时间，以 确保工作中断降到最低限度。如果不能获得奖励，员工可 能不愿意做这种额外的投入。

Sailing dinghies Financial benefits The project has a positive net present value of $5·5m,indicating it should create value for Yexmarine. It seems that revenue would soon be earned from the sailing dinghies and the payback period of under four years appears acceptable,given the five-year time horizon set by the chief execuive.The investment would leave surplus funds of S6m for investment elsewhere. As a cheaper and simpler craft,the market for sailing dinghies is likely to be wider,and the demand may not vary as much as for cruising yachts due to economic conditions,so the investment may represent effective diversification.Fashion changes are unlikely to affect the demand for sailing dinghies and they have the potential to become a steady long-term cash cow for Yexmarine. As the largest company producing sailing dinghies in the country,it should be able to take advantage of economies of scale,particularly after the move to the new premises. Financial drawbacks The gross and operating profit margins of 35%and 12%are lower than Yexmarine's current margins and the chief executive's targets.Having a selling price of S4,000 which is lower than the sector average of $5,000 may initially help Yexmarine establish itself in this segment of the market,but often sailing dinghies willbe bought as a one-off purchase,so having low prices to encourage repeat purchases is not effective. Having the lowest price might also be a problem if the dinghies are seen as lower quality.The target market appears to be families who are learning to sail with their children,and who are likely therefore not to wish to compromise on a perceived lack of safety features which they may associate with a lower price option. Non-financial benefits By entering this market,Yexmarine would be differentiating itself from its main competitors.It would be adopting a clear cost leadership strategy in the new market,going some way to address the concern that it currently lacks a clear strategy.The standard design of sailing dinghies is simpler than cruising yachts,so this may allow for a learning curve effect and ultimately more efficient production. A customer purchase of a dinghy could be the start of a relationship which will result in subsequent business for Yexmarine,including repair and maintenance work and perhaps,in some instances, eventual trading up to a more expensive yacht. 批注[86]:报告格式(形式简略，建议采取完整的报告格 式 ) 。 批注[87]:非常简明的一句话，说明该报告的主要内容。 批注[88]:针对第一个项目，分别阐述财务方面的优势和财 务方面的劣势，以及非财务方面的优势和非财务方面的劣 势 。 批注[89]:财务方面的优势： 1.NPV$5.5m 意味着什么,回收期是否可以接受，以及该 投资计划可以留下额外的$6m 投资到其它领域。(背景信 息告诉我们有$10m 可以用于产品投资，这个项目初始投 资要求$4m, 也就是说可以余下$6m 。) 2.该项目产品比较便宜，市场可能更广阔，需求可能不会因 为经济形势受到巨大波动，而且也不太可能会受到时尚变 化的影响。 3.作为本国最大的供应商，应该能够利用规模效益，尤其是 在搬到新总部之后。 批注[90]:财务方面的劣势： 1.GPmargin and OP margin 相比当前如何?售价$4000低于 行业平均售价可能会在后续带来怎样的负面影响? 2.售价最低也可能带来一个问题，就是被认为是质量低。当 低价与质量低安全措施欠缺关联在一起的时候，目标市场 客户可能不希望为此而对安全进行妥协。 批注[91]:非财务方面的优势： 1.进入这个市场可以有效地与主要竞争对手区分开来。很显 然公司采取的是cost leadership strategy。这一定程度上解 决了公司当前缺乏清晰战略的问题。新产品的标准化设计 比当前产品要简单的多，可以利用学习曲线带来的效果、 并且最终更有效率的生产。 2.当客户开始购买这款新产品就意味着一段新的客户关系 的开始，这会为Yexmarine 带来后续的业务，包括维修和 维护，甚至最终升级到更贵的产品。 真题解析2022-MarJun 21 Non-financial drawbacks Yexmarine faces a significant amount of local competition in this market.Although other manufacturers are smaller,they are long-established.As the design of sailing dinghies is simpler than cruising yachts,it may be difficult for Yexmarine to differentiate its dinghies from its competitors. Risk assessment Assessment of risks should be more detailed than whether the risk is serious or non-serious.As a minimum,the overall impact and probability of each risk should be assessed as high or low to help determine how much priority management of each risk should be given and what actions need to be taken to manage the risk.There is also no indication of how the seriousness of risks was determined. Flooding risk Assessment of risk The assessment of flooding risk as non-serious could be justified if the risk was said to be remote. However,as Yexmarine's new site will be in a coastal town with recent experience of flooding and more predicted to occur,the risk seems potentially serious. If flooding did occur,the consequences could be more than temporary,involving damage to Yexmarine's premises and assets,business infrastructure and the yachts t was constructing or repairing.The interruption to business could lead to a significant loss of income.Yexmarine might also need to compensate customers for damage to their yachts.There may also be a reputation risk,as news that Yexmarine has been flooded may deter yacht owners from having their yachts worked on at Yexmarine's premises. Adequacy of risk mitigating activities If the risk is assumed to be low probability but high possible impact,transferring the risk to an insurance company would be an appropriate general strategy.However,if the risk is considered to be higher probability,insuring might become very costly or the insurance options open to Yexmarine become limited. Watching for flood warnings would give advance warnings of trouble,but there is no mitigating activity to reduce the impact of floods if they do occur.Yexmarine will be dependent on the state of local flood defences,but they may not be enough. Further risk mitigating activities Yexmarine should take some flood precautions itself to prevent or slow the impact of flooding. Yexmarine should also have a contingency plan to reorganise premises or move some assets,for example,electrical machinery or computers.The plan should also include steps to clean its premises up as quickly as possible if they have been flooded.However,contingency planning will not be able to prevent damage to the premises or the yachts themselvesif flooding is serious. 批注[101]:报告格式(形式简略，建议采取完整的报告格 式 ) 。 批注[102]:非常简洁一句话说明这份报告的主要内容。 批注[103]:咨询师对运营经理的风险评估做了整体评价。 主要问题在于： 1.对于风险的评估不仅仅是严重还是不严重，而是需要更多 细节； 2.不仅要考虑风险带来的潜在影响，而且要考虑发生的可能 性； 3.没有说明风险严重还是不严重是如何判断的。 批注[104]:针对洪水风险： 1.风险严重还是不严重、以及原因； 2.风险防范措施是否充分； 3.更进一步可以采取的风险防范措施。 批注[105]:注意However 后面的表述，借用材料4信息， 说明了该风险可能是严重的(运营经理判断为不严重可能 是不恰当的)。 批注[106]:基于上述判断，洪水发生带来的后果可能不是 短暂的临时性的。对业务的中断可能带来重大损失。可能 需要补偿客户。可能影响声誉。 批注[107]:如果发生的可能性较小，但是一旦发生会带来 严重后果，(按照TARA模型),可以把风险转移出去。如 果发生的可能性较大，那么使用保险公司的服务可能非常 贵或者缺乏相应的保险公司愿意提供保险服务。 批注[108]:注意这里阐述为什么运营经理建议的风险防范 措施是不足够的。 批注[109]:Yexmarine可以采取的更进一步的措施。 特别注意需要有后备计划contingency plan, 但是， contingency plan 不能防止灾难(和破坏)的发生。 真题解析2022-MarJun 25 Product obsolescence risk Assessment of risk It is unlikely that Yexmarine's current cruising yachts will become completely obsolete.As regards the possible new products,product obsolescence risk in sailing dinghies is likely to be low as their technology is relatively simple.With luxury yachts,the issue may be failing to keep up with fashion and changing tastes. The article in Yachting World indicates,however,that Yexmarine faces a serious risk arising from being perceived as not innovating and falling behind its competition.This is likely to increase if Yexmarine does not introduce new products or significantly redesign its cruising yachts.This perception could affect sales and income in Yexmarine's main cruising yacht market,and also lead to more general perceptions affecting Yexmarine's brand overall.The Yachting World article also suggests that Yexmarine's customer base may be mainly more mature buyers,suggesting ultimately a reducing target market and loss of market share. 批注[110]:同样地，针对产品过时风险： 1.风险严重还是不严重、以及原因； 2.风险防范措施是否充分； 3.更进一步可以采取的风险防范措施。 批注[111]:注意这里阐述为什么产品过时的风险不严重。 批注[112]:按照材料2的信息，Yexmarine 面对的一个严重 风险是被认为缺乏创新、落后于竞争对手。 同时，这份材料也暗示了Yexmarine的客户基础主要是比 较成熟年长的客户(超过50岁)。 Adequacy of risk mitigating activities Certainly Yexmarine needs to be aware of what its main competitors are doing.However,just trying to catch up with competitors will take time and is essentially a reactive strategy. Adding additional features to current models may be seen as being reactive and not making Yexmarine distinct from its competitors,and expenditure on limited changes could produce negative returns. Further risk mitigating activities Yexmarine needs to take steps to be aware of developments internationally as well as locally,so it can recognise innovations which may takeit ahead of its competitors. Yexmarine also needs an overall innovation strategy.This would start by developing an infrastructure which includes advanced information technology,human resource design capability and production processes flexible enough to be able to cope with continual evolution.Product development processes can be built on the back of this infrastructure.Yexmarine also needs a new marketing message and rereshed image,and to make better use of marketing media. 批注[113]:(基于上述判断，Yexmarine需要管理和防范的 是更为严重的风险，即缺乏创新和客户老龄化) Yexmarine当前采取的两个措施都是不足够的，而且是比较 被动的reactive strategy,并没有使Yexmarine与竞争对手 区分开来。 批注[114]:Yexmarine 可以采取的更进一步的措施。 不仅知道国际上、也需要知道本国在创新方面的最新进展， 力争超过竞争对手。 Yexmarine 需要一套完整的创新战略。而且需要全新的市场 营销信息与品牌形象相配合，也需要充分利用营销媒介(比 如，社交媒体)。 真题解析2022-MarJun Task 5 Yexmarine's chief executive is looking at the ways in which Yexmarine's boats are marketed. Website At present,Yexmarine's website seems to focus on the company as a provider of reliable,well-crafted boats.This is a traditional message which may not fit best with Yexmarine's product base.An external review could help identify what messages need to be put across most strongly in relation to the cruising yachts.The search engine optimisation services and banner ads can also attract more traffic to Yexmarine's website at a time when it wants to enhance its visibility. Social media Seeyachts Co's proposal also stresses Yexmarine's greater visibility on social media,which can enhance messages about its products which Yexmarine is trying to promote and give it a more up-to-date image.It can also enhance its visibility more widely at a time when it is looking at proposed investments which it hopes willachieve significant international sales. The emphasis on online communities can help maximise the benefits from the loyalty which Yexmarine already possesses amongst its customers.Online communities can also facilitate a two-way dialogue with customers,prospective customers and others in the yachting world,providing valuable feedback which could lead into innovation and product development.Yexmarine may also be able to gain an advantage over its main competitors in Neoland,as it appears that neither of them is using social media well. Personalisation Seeyachts Co's proposals in relation to individual customers is consistent with one of Yexmarine's strengths,of close relationships with its customers.They enable a systematic approach to be taken to establishing well-targeted relationships online and extending reach to other customers. Event marketing Yexmarine may be making a major product investment soon which could require considerable promotion to establish itself in the market. Yexmarine lacks recent experience of this sort of launch and Seeyachts Co can supply the experience.This should enable marketing to be targeted so that it is as effective as possible and Seeyachts Co's experience should help provide this.Employing Seeyachts 批注[117]: Briefing notes格式。 批注[118]:这段话引入比较长。介绍Yexmarine当前确实需 要专业的营销服务与经验。而且伴随着变革，Yexmarine 也 需要考虑是否需要新的品牌形象和全新的市场营销方法。 Background on the computer games industry Of the two billion computer game players(gamers)worldwide,18%are children (who are the main users of NT products).In 20X2,the industry generated$190 billion in revenue and analysts expect it will generate $260 billion by 20X5. Success relies on reputation and brand recognition,innovation,aggressive marketing and on-time product launches. Competition in the industry is extremely high.A small number of large global organisations hold up to 75%market share of revenue in most key markets.The only potential opportunity for NT is to be a small,niche provider. Games can cost tens of millions of dollars to develop and similar amounts to market.Many fail. Cancelled projects,unanticipated long delays,cost overruns and unexpected quality problems are very common within the industry. A decade ago,75%of computer games were sold as physical copies (e.g.CDs).In the last 12 months,this has reduced to below 20%,with over 80%of games now being sold in digital form (meaning that once the game is downloaded over the internet,it is immediately ready to play). Current issues in the industry The computer game industry is a target for cybercrime,putting at risk players'sensitive personal data. The prevalence of social media and online reviews means that a few poor ratings from influential gamers can cause a game to failwithin weeks of its release. Many digital games allow gamers to play for free.However,revenue can be generated by encouraging players to make purchases whilst playing free games.These in-game purchases, which enable players to enhance their experience or get an advantage over other players,are known as microtransactions.Additional revenue can also be generated from advertisements placed in digital games,but these can irritate players if they are irrelevant in content orexcessive in nature. In some key computer game markets,concerns abou 'addiction to computer games hasled to government interventions tolimit usage.Psychologists have claimed that warning messages and notifications are not enough,and the industry needs to design games responsibly. 批注[11]:电脑游戏行业-背景信息和当前问题，由商务 发展总监编制 批注[12]:电脑游戏行业背景： 全世界200万游戏玩家当中，18%是儿童 (NT 产品的主要 用户)。20x2年，该行业创造了1900亿美元收入，分析师 预计截止到20×5年会创造2600亿美元。 成功依赖于声誉和品牌认可，创新，激进的市场营销和及 时的产品上市。 行业竞争非常激烈。少数大型全球组织持有大多数关键市 场不超过75%的份额的收入。 NT唯一的机会是成为小众领 域的供应商。 游戏的开发可能耗费千万美元，以及千万美元的市场营销。 许多公司失败了。被取消的项目、未预料的长期的延迟、 成本超支、以及未预料的质量问题，是行业普遍问题。 十年前，75%电脑游戏是通过CD 光盘销售，在过去12个 月，这个比例已经减少到20%,超过80%的游戏现在以数 字形式销售(意味着一旦下载，可以立即开玩)。 批注[13]:行业当前问题： 电脑游戏是网络攻击的目标，游戏玩家的个人敏感数据暴 露于风险之中。 社交媒体的盛行和线上评论意味着有影响力的玩家的某些 负面评论可以让游戏在面世的几周内失败。 许多数字游戏允许游戏玩家免费试玩。但是，如果玩家在 免费试玩的时候购买了该游戏，就可以产生收入。这些游 戏内消费使玩家能够提升体验或者获得优于其他玩家的优 势，被称作微交易。数字游戏内发布的广告也可以带来额 外的收入，但是，如果这些广告与内容无关或者量过大， 有可能激怒玩家。 在某些关键的电脑游戏市场上，因为担心玩家‘沉迷于’ 电脑游戏，已经引起政府干预。心理学家声称警告和通知 是不足够的，游戏行业需要负责任地设计电脑游戏。 5.Financial REPORT THE USE OF BIG DATA IN THE COMPUTER GAMES INDUSTRY 1JUNE 20X3 Prepared for the board of NT By iT Consultant 1 Introduction This report considers some of the uses of big data generated by digital computer games. 2 Potential uses of big data Big data generated by a digital computer game can be used to measure,predict and track gamers'behaviour. - It can provide insight into how and when gamers are playing and who they are playing with,their levels of engagement and when they are leaving games and not returning. - It can indicate where there are problems in the game,where gamers find it hard to complete tasks or where the tasks are too easy. -Interactions between gamers (game chats)can be saved and tracked for complaints and recurring issues. 批注[20]:大数据的潜在用途-IT咨询师关于电脑游戏行 业使用大数据的报告 批注[21]:1.报告介绍。 2.大数据的潜在用途： 数字电脑游戏产生的大数据可以用来度量、预测并且追踪 游戏玩家的行为。 ·可以提供关于游戏玩家如何和什么时候玩游戏、以及与 谁玩、玩的深度、什么时候离开游戏并且再也没有回来。 ·揭示游戏哪里有问题，哪里游戏玩家很难通关、或者哪 里游戏任务太简单了。 -游戏玩家之间的互动聊天可以保存并且用来追踪投诉和 反复出现的问题。 3 Example of how big data is being used The approach is already being used by a major streaming service.It uses big data and analytics to: -I Inform decisions on which shows to invest in. - Personalise customers'screens with recommendations based on their viewing preferences,thereby supporting customer retention. - Conduct custom marketing(trailers'for new shows)based on what customers have watched previously-for instance,customers who have watched romantic shows willA get a trailer focused on the love interest in an upcoming show. 4 Conclusion If NT is to develop a computer game (or computer games)inspired by Nola Bricks,the big data generated could therefore provide solutions to a range of current computer industry issues which the company could face. 批注[22]:大数据是怎么被使用的例子： 这个方法已经被一家主流的流媒体服务供应商使用。它使 用大数据及分析： 做决策，投资哪一部剧 个性化用户的屏幕并给用户提建议，以用户的浏览偏好 为基础，确保客户留存率 -实行个性化的市场营销(为客户发送新剧“预告”), 基于客户之前观剧，比如，观看浪漫型剧集的客户将会收 到与爱情相关的即将上映的新剧的预告。 结论 ： 如果NT公司打算开发电脑游戏，因此产生的大数据可以为 电脑行业当前的一系列问题提供解决方案。 8 Task 1 The NT board has been given a clear instruction by ZC,for immediate implementation,to diversify.The board recognises that many existing staff are uneasy about the appointment of the new CEO and are likely to react negatively to any diversification.It has therefore asked for your advice on how to implement change professionally andeffectively. computer games,but the points raised below and their impact on what is a very small negative NPV should be brought into consideration. Time frame Given that the computer games industry represents a new market for NT,aditional research and analysis should be carried out to determine the appropriateness of applying a five-year investment time frame to this financial appraisal. Timing The potential for earning revenue from the new operation within a year of initial nwestments should be questioned.Computer games can take significant periods of time to develop,and there is no guarantee that games will be popular and generate revenue on launch. Revenue It is unrealistic to assume that both the revenue of the global video games market and NTs projected market share will not change over the five years of the analysis.It could be argued that there would be litle point investing in a market unles it wasgrowing and the NT share of it was expected to grow.The approach taken is at least conservative,although analysts expect the market to generate more than $260 bilion by 20X5 and hence it may be more appropriate to refect this increase.Were the market to grow to this extent,NTs annual revenue would increase by S18-90 milion to S70-20 milion (ba sed on the 0-027%market share used by the finance assistant). If the finance assistant has not taken into account the risks associated with a move into the computer games (see below)when ariving at the 0-027%market share,the figure should be adjusted down,leading to an even worse NPV. Given the size of the market,any smallerror in the anticipated market share could have a significant impact on the revenue.For instance, were the market share to be 0-02%rather than 0-027%,revenue would be $13·30 million less per annum. The project is therefore very sensitve to the anticipated level of revenue. experience.Outlandia experienced a serious earthquake that completely destroyed the services and communication infrastructure to 5%of the population.We successfully formed our first convoy,in a neighbouring country,and supplied water to an estimated 35,000 families over a period of six weeks. Durania 2016 The severe drought in the summer of 2016 left many remote areas of Durania with dried-up wells and boreholes.Local aid organisations coped until a series of wildfires spread through the East of the country.We provided two convoys to assist the local emergency services.After 3 weeks the fires were under control and the drinking water supply was restored enabling us to withdraw. Lugania 2017-18 Severe flooding polluted water supplies in a mountainous region.Despite the developed nature of the country,local services were stretched to breaking point. Occasionally,we withdraw our aid on the assumption that people now have access to suitable drinking water,only to find later that this is not the case. The disasters that we deal with often involve serious damage to wels,boreholes and supply pipes,which can take months or even years to fix.Often,local service providers are unable to respond effectively and this can lead to a serious risk of disease and on-going water shortages. 批注[12]:CEO发送给咨询师(你的角色)的邮件 批注[13]:说明这风邮件的目的，希望你能评估一下该组织 打算扩大业务范围、给受影响地区提供可持续引用水的战 略计划。 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 批注[14]:关于该战略计划： 背景：提供瓶装水毫无疑问挽救了很多生命，但是不是一 个长期的可持续的解决方案。 自然灾害通常会严重影响水井、钻孔、供应管道，需要几 个月甚至几年才能修复。而且当地不能有效地修复，这会 导致严重的疾病风险和持续的水资源短缺。 Proposal Jess,the Board and I,all support a proposal to extend the service provided by ThirstAid by adding an infrastructure element.This would mean ThirstAid remaining 'in place',probably with a different team of volunteers,after our final convoy delivery. The new team would: √Assess the infrastructure needs including water supply pipes,boreholes,wells or wind-pumps. √Acquire the necessary hardware to provide a sustainable water supply. √Construct and commission the requiredinfrastructure. √Train local volunteers to repair and maintain the new water supply hardware,if the local organisations require assistance. 批注[15]:提议：董事会，主席Jess和我提出这个计划：在 convoy配送瓶装水之后，我们仍然留在当地，可能与另一 队不同的志愿者。 批注[16]:我们这个新团队留在当地进行的活动。 这个计划使得我们从现在提供短期的紧急服务，转换成长 期可持续计划。这可以大大减少我们后期再到访该地区的 可能性。 While this development is a significant expansion of services,we see it as entirely logical.It takes us from our current position of providing emergency aid in the short term,to one where we can follow up short term aid with long term sustainable benefit. A positive consequence from this change of approach would be a significant reduction in the risk that we might need to re-visit an area,as was the case in Durania. Consequences We have started to think through the consequences of this new strategic direction. 批注[17]:该战略计划可能的结果，包括需要让各个利益相 Our initial list of the most obvious issues is as follows: 关者知道，以及我们需要的资源和开发的系统。 1.We will need to let our stakeholders know 2.We willprobably need to recruit new staff,and develop the capability to do new things.For example,we lack expertise in engineering.We also need to decide how much work willbe done by ThirstAid staff and how much by volunteers. 3.We willneed to find suitably skilled and expekenced volunteers and add them to our database. 4.We will need access to money to fund the infrastructure projects. 5.We will need to develop new procedures to cover medium-term projects.At the moment, everything is geared towards short-term convoys. 6.We willneed to develop IT systems to cope with a new way of working.Staff on medium-term projects cannot be out of touch with us,or their families,for months.Staff on the ground will also need to consult experts,order materials,transfer funds and pay suppliers. 7.We will need to consider legal and insurance implications. Q1a suggested answer Marking guide Task 1 requirement(a) The marking guide below organises issues that could be included in the answer using headings from SWOT.This provides some organisation to the issues listed. Answers are not required to use SWOT.Candidates may use any model or approach. Up to 1marks for each point relevant to strategic position Examples of points that could be included: Strengths -Proven track-record of successful aid projects -Strong reputation with key stakeholders -Knowledgeable and skilled staff -Access to a pool of capable volunteers -Extensive network of contacts Weaknesses -Fundraising seems reactionary(follows on from a disaster) -Reliant upon the cooperation of governments -Limited capacity,ideally one project at a time -'Demand'is dependent on events which are out of ThirstAid's control -Reliant upon the goodwill of volunteers -Reliant upon the funds of donors Opportunities -Take a more proactive approach to fundraising Could look to establish partnerships with other charities -Could seek opportunities to leverage their reputation and 'brand' Threats 批注[30]:虽然建议答案中提到了SWOT 模型，但是除了 SWOT之外，考生也可以使用任何其他的模型或方法。 组织内部： ●Strengths 优势： 有成功的援助项目记录； 在主要利益相关者中享有良好声誉： 拥有知识渊博、技术熟练的员工； 拥有大量有能力的志愿者； 拥有广泛的社交联系网络 ●Weaknesses 劣势： 筹款比较被动(紧随灾难之后); 政府的合作有限； 理想情况下是“一次一个项目”; 对于ThrstAid“需求”并不在其控制范围中； 依靠志愿者的善意； 依靠捐助者的资金 ●Opportunities 机遇： 采取更积极主动的筹款方式； 寻求与其他慈善机构建立伙伴关系； 寻找机会利用自己的声誉和“品牌” ●Threats 挑战： 其他机构可能会更受欢迎； 捐助者可能会“转向”其他机构； 志愿者的不当行为可能会导致声誉受损； 无法应对的灾难数量可能会增加 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 17 -Other causes and charities may become more popular -Donors may switch'to other charities or causes -Volunteer misconduct could cause reputational harm -May not be able to cope with an increase in the number of disasters [A maximum of 3 marks per category,up to a maximum of 8marks in total]3 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 18

Q1b suggested answer Markingguide Task 1 requirement(b) Up to 2 marks for each point relevant to the new strategic direction Examples of points that could be included: Suitability -Natural disasters and extreme weather are expected to increase -Many governments struggle to provide effective water supplies -ThirstAid lack infrastructure knowledge and skills -Involvement in longer-term projects may hinder emergency supply projects Acceptability -The new direction is consistent with ThirstAid's current mission -Jess and the Board of Trustees have expressed their support for the new direction -The support of reevant governments would be required - Volunteers with different skills would need to be recruited -Donors would need to be comfortable with the wider remit Feasibility -Additional fundraising or debt finance would be required to finance infrastructure projects - Additional resources,including people with relevant skills,would be required -It will be challenging to operate longer-term projects in some regions at the same time as operating an emergency project elsewhere [A maximum of 6 marks for each category and a maximum of 12 marks in total] 批注[31]:●Sutability 一个战略是否“合适”,指其是否与 公司的目标能够契合，主要考虑外界环境对于战略的影响 力以及组织的应对(战略)和外界环境搭配起来，多大程 度上能促成目标的达成 For: 自然灾害和极端天气预计会增加； 许多政府努力提供有效的水供应； Against: 缺乏基础设施知识和技能； 参与长期项目可能会妨碍紧急供应项目 ●Acceptability 战略的被接受程度，指从投资者(股东、债 权人，财务角度)和其他利益相关者(非财务角度),不 同的利益相关者对战略的态度如何，以及这些利益相关者 是否能对组织施加影响 For: 新的方向与ThirstAid目前的使命是一致的； Jess 和董事会已经表达了他们对新方向的支持；Against: 需要有关政府的支持； 需要招募具有不同技能的志愿者； 捐助者可能需要做出更多的捐款 ●Feasibility 战略的成功离不开资源的准备，包括但不限于 资金、人力、知识储备等，主要考虑内部环境对于战略的 影响力 Against: 为基础设施项目融资将需要更多的资金； 需要额外的资源，包括具有相关技能的人员； 在某些地区经营较长期项目的同时在其他地方经营紧急项 目将是一项挑战(资源的分配可能不够或者不合理) ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 19 Task 2 Jamal has requested that you deliver a presentation at the next Board of Trustees meeting which will be attended by all members of the ThirstAid senior management team. Required: Produce presentation slides with accompanying notes for your presentation which: (a ) Identifies and evaluates the current resources and competences of Thir stAid. (9 marks) Professional skills marks are available for demonstrating appropriate scepticism when evaluating information regarding resources and competences. (3 marks) (b) Advises the meeting how those resources and competences must change,if the new strategic direction is to be implemented successfully. (6 marks) Professional skills marks are available for demonstrating communication skills in persuading the senior managers. (2 marks) Note:Your presentation should contain no more than 10 slides.Include brief notes for each slide. 批注[32]:情景设置：在Board of Trustees meeting给高管做 演讲。 批注[33]:格式：幻灯片。注意考试时用幻灯片答题而不是 word。 批注[34]: 确认并平价ThirstAid现在的资源及竞争力。 问题本身永远是最重要的。 审题1,重要名词确定答案内容： resources 资源 and competences 竞争力。分两个部分写。 共9分，预计每个部分5分。 快速关联学过的知识点：资源(6M 模型)、竞争力共同决 定Strategic Capability战略能力。模型提供思路不一定照搬。 审题2,关键动词：题目要求干什么? Identifies and evaluates, 确认、评估(balance: 正反面)相应的资源、竞 争力，确认和评估两个要求，所以每个点预计2分，大致 估计 resources and competences各写3点。 审题3:根据分值确定答题时间。 批注[35]: Professional mark 3分 。Professional skill: 职业怀 疑 。 批注[36]:如果要想成功实施新战略，资源、竞争力需要做 出哪些改变?给Board of Trustees提供一些建议。 需结合案例，给出建议，找支持建议的案例信息论证，要 有说服力，建议才被采纳。 预计每个点1-2分，保守起见写6点。(考官实际给分是 一 点 2 分 ) 批注[37]:专业技能：说服力。因为是给建议，所以要注意 建议清晰、论证符合逻辑、结合案例提供论据。 批注[38]:每个幻灯片要有说明note 。 不要超过10张幻灯 片。 ONLIN 20

Q3a suggested answer Examples of points that could be included: -To achieve their mission,ThirstAid require effective information systems -For information systems to be effective and reliable,requires effective system controls -ThirstAid hold personal data which willbe subject to data protection regulations -Ineffective controls which facilitate a data breach would cause reputational damage -Privacy is likely to be important to many donors-poor system controls may hinder fundraising -The benefits of ensuring effective controls are highly likely to exceed the cost of impleme ntation

Q3b suggested answer -Poor physical access control Poor practices including shared user names and users remaining logged-in -No additional,application passwordsmeaning sensitive data such as payroll and donor information is available to all users -The transportation of data on removable storage media brings unnecessary risk -The use of devices and storage media disconnected from the network risks data inconsistencies and version control issues -The office network Wi-Fi password is freely avalable -The absence of a hardware update polcy has led to the use of outdated hardware which brings increased risk of hardware failure -The absence of a software maintenance and update policy has led to the use of unsupported software which brings increased risk of inaccurate,unreliable data -Weekly back-up of data held on the server is insufficient-up to a week's data could be lost -Holding back-ups on-site exposes ThirstAid to increased risk of data loss -The absence of a comprehensive disaster recovery plan increases the risk of an extended period of system downtime -Systems are outdated.Cloud-based systems would facilitate more effective controls 批注[55]:有效的信息系统控制的必要性： 1.为了实现组织的使命，需要有效的信息系统 2.为了确保系统有效而且可信赖，需要有效的控制 3.该组织持有大量的个人数据，需要符合数据保护监管规定 4.缺乏有效的控制系统，可能违反数据保护监管规定，影响 组织声誉 5.数据隐私对于捐赠者来说是十分重要的，缺乏有效的系统 控制可能会阻碍捐赠者捐赠资金 6.有效的系统控制带来的好处很有可能超过维护控制发生 的成本 批注[56]:该组织信息系统控制的充分性： 1.系统接触控制薄弱 2.某些具体操作不恰当 3.缺少密码，敏感信息缺乏保护 4.外接设备的使用可能导致数据不一致以及版本问题 5.办公室不需要密码，可以免费接入网络 6.硬件设备更新不及时，使用过时设备可能带来额外的风险 (设备瘫痪) 7.软件缺乏维护和更新，可能导致数据不准确，不可靠 8.每周的数据备份可能导致不到一周的数据没有来得及备 份 9.备份没有单独保存 10.缺乏一份整体规划的系统恢复计划 11.系统过时，云计算为基础的系统能够实施更有效的控制 25 Task 4 It has been decided that ThirstAid will pursue a new strategic direction including helping to deliver long term changes in local infrastructure to provide sustainable clean water.Jess,the ThirstAid Chair,has agreed to chair a Steering Committee to consider how ThirstAid's information systems can support the new strategy.The committee includes two trustees, yourself and Johan. Q5b suggested answer Examples of points that could be included: The timing of payments is a major issue ThirstAid would need to find SN300 million at the start of the campagn -This cash requirement occurs at the same time as cash will be required for infrastructure projects -We must review our cash position.It is highly likely that a loan will be required to finance the early years of the campaign -Committing significant funds to a marketing campaign is likely to concern some existing donors who prefer to see funds committed to aid projects -Committing significant funds to a marketing campaign may damage ThirstAid's reputation as an efficient charity which ensures value for every dollar donated -The contractual arrangement means donations above the guaranteed level are retained by the advertising agency which may breach charity regulations as donors intend to donate to ThirstAid, legal advice should be sought -Donors deserve transparency regarding how their donations will be used.This may not be possible if some donations are retained by an advertising agency. -Are we comfortable with the structure of the contract in the context of our own reputation and our ethical standards? -Although the donation website and SMS service will be provided by the agency these services will be closely associated with ThirstAid so must be professional and efficient to prevent reputational or brand damage -There is a risk of phishing -imitation websites and fraudulent communication designed to trick donors,ThirstAid need to ensure the marketing agency has appropriate safeguardsin place 批注[70]:接受该(市场推广)提议的风险： 1.收到捐赠的时间 2.初始需要投入300m 3.每一年都需要现金投入 4.需要考虑当前的资金状况，可能需要贷款以满足初始投入 5.把资金投入到市场推广，可能引起某些捐赠者的不满 6.需要考虑合同安排条款当中谈到的募集资金超过最低保 证金额，超过部分是否归属于广告公司，这可能与捐赠者 的初衷相悖 7.需要考虑募集资金透明公开 8.需要考虑我们的声誉和商业伦理是否会受到影响 9.考虑捐赠者捐赠资金的渠道由谁来控制，防止我们的声誉 和品牌受到影响 DELIVERY-PLATINUM 10.防止网络钓鱼(电信诈骗)的风险 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM 31 2023年9月考季改革后 Specimen 2 解析 Exhibit:Introduction This case study relates to three tasks. (4 marks) 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 批注[10]:公司董事会正在考虑财务董事总结的两个投资 项目。公司将要使用出售两个总部带来的额外的资金来支 持其中任一个项目。但是，公司没有足够的资源同时投资 两个项目。CEO 正在寻求你的观点是否应当选择这两个项 目中的任一个。(这里可以快速对应到材料5) 特别要注意 公司只能投资其中一个项目 批注[11]: Report格式报告对象： The Board 时间分配：26marks*2=52 分 钟 分数分配：26分 财务方面，每一个要点不超过1分，每个项目的总分7分。 非财务方面，每一个要点不超过1分，每一个项目不超过 7 分 。 职业技能：评估 能够平衡地考虑两个投资项目各个方面，提供合理化建议。 职业技能分数4分 Answer: Task 1) Report to board ofYexmarine Introduction This report evaluates the two proposed investment projects-a new range of sailing dinghies versus a new range of luxury vachts. There is $10m available to invest in either project,but not enough funds to complete both. This report evaluates the two projects and makes a recommendation. Sailing dinghies Financial benefits The project has a positive net present value of$5.5m,indicating it should create value for Yexmarine.It seems that revenue would soon be earned from the sailing dinghies and the payback period of under four years appears acceptable,given the five year time horizon for improvements in Yexmarine's financial performance set by the chief executive. The investment would leave surplus funds of $6m for investment elsewhere. As a cheaper and simpler craft,the market for sailing dinghies is likely to be wider, and the demand may not vary as much as for cruising yachts due to economic conditions,so the investment may represent effective diversification.Fashion changes are unlikely to affect the demand for sailing dinghies and they have the potential to become a steady long-term cash cow for Yexmarine. As Yexmarine is larger than the other companies that currently manufacture sailing dinghies in Neoland,it should be able to take advantage of economies of scale, particularly after the move to the new premises. Financial drawbacks The gross and operating profit margins of 35%and 12%are lower than Yexmarine's current margins and the chief executive's targets.Having a price of $4,000 which is lower than the sector average of $5,000 may initially help Yexmarine establish itself in this segment of the market,but often sailing dinghies will be bought as a one-off purchase,so having low prices to encourage repeat purchases is not effective. The stages are getting employees prepared for changes in behaviour,processes and operations(unfreezing),the move to new premises itself(change)and the rapid settling down into the new situation (refreezing).Employee dissatisfaction could have consequences for each stage of this process. Unfreezing Employees'beliefs that their opinions will be ignored will hinder any process of consultation during this stage.Employees may be reluctant to offer opinions if they feel no notice willbe taken of them,or if they fear that expressing opinions which are unpopular with management could be held against them.There also seems to be a risk that more experienced or specialist employees willleave the company.This will mean that there will be a lack of motivated employees who could lead the process of change and help convince other employees. Change The actual move to the new premises is likely to require employees making a commitment of time greater than their normal employment to ensure disruption is keptto a minimum.Employees may be unwilling to make this extra commitment if they are not rewarded for it. This is also the time that new processes willbe introduced.If employees have not tried to learn them properly or resent them,there is a risk that management could face active resistance from employees,increasing the level of disruption which the move will cause.This could result in service to customers being disrupted Refreezing There is a significant risk that employees will revert to old routines after the move has taken place.This will require management time to correct and is likely to increase resentment,particularly if employees still regard Yexmarine's management as coming from a different culture.This could mean decreased operational efficiency due to signficant employee turnover.As a result,Yexmarine may find that the quality of service given to customers falls. 批注[37]:根据题目要求，判断使用Lewin's three stage model 回答(员工不满意)对企业变革流程每 一个阶段的影响。 批注[38]: Unfreezing stage: 员工如果认为他们的意见会 被忽视的话，将会阻碍与员工的咨询沟通的过程。最后带 来的后果(风险)可能是有经验的或者是有专门技术的员 工离职，这意味着原本有动力的员工可以领导变革过程并 且帮助说服其他员工，但是组织会失去他们。 批注[39]: Change stage: 1.搬到新总部可能要求员工比正常工作投入更多的时间，以 确保工作中断降到最低限度。如果不能获得奖励，员工可 能不愿意做这种额外的投入。 (搬到新总部之后)也是新的流程引入的时间。如果员工 没有恰当地学习或者痛恨新流程，管理层可能将要面对来 自员工的极力抵制。极端情况下，管理层可能采用强制手 段。尽管这能有效地推进变革，但是有可能会产生反效果， 员工的抵制增加，需要管理层花费更多时间来解决。所有 这些都可能导致服务中断。 批注[40]: Refreezing stage: 这是要防止员工重回旧文化 的阶段，因为员工已经习惯了当前的(旧)文化。这要求 管理层花费时间来纠正并且有可能增加员工的痛恨，尤其 是当员工仍然认为公司的管理层是来自于另外一个文化 (材料4信息)。因此，这可能意味着因为大量的员工流 失而导致运营效率降低。如果这样，Yexmarine 会发现很难 实现CEO 的承诺，改善对客户的服务 Task 3 Yexmarine's chief executive wishes to obtain independent assurance that the company is taking sufficient steps to effectively manage significant risks.The chief executive has asked the operations director to recommend how four specific risks should be managed.The chief executive wants you to evaluate the information which the operations director has supplied. Opportunities This contract could raise the profile of PSS across Zeelande,and lead to an increase in sales and market share in the competitive industry of bed mattress manufacturing. Manufacture of the Badin mattress requires springs.The cost of springs is susceptible to increases per the risk register. The audit and risk committee has highlighted that guarantee claims are increasing. Although this is currently due to another type of mattress,the possibility that PSS would be exposed to a higher level of guarantee claims in relation to the Zeelande Health contract must be considered.The guarantee is for ten years but the average life span for this type of mattress is only eight years and it can be argued that the life span of a mattress used in a hospital would be substantially shorter than the eight year average.PSS runs the risk of having to replace a high number of mattresses to Zeelande Health free of charge. The Zeelande Health contract is not always consistent with PSS values.It will be difficult for PSS to balance high standards of quality and product testing against the reduced margins that this contract offers. The contract is also not for an innovative new product,but rather for the Badin mattress that is already manufactured.The introduction of this contract will affect PSS's retail partners(due to manufacturing already being at 100%capacity)who are currently PSS's only customers for the Badin mattress,meaning a reduction in customer service to the retail partners. The contract could also negatively impact PSS's sustainability value. Delivery of the mattresses across Zeelande along with the collection of the old mattresses for recycling could mean that it is more difficult for PSS to remain carbon neutral. Conclusion Although this is an interesting proposal which offers PSS the opportunity to increase its profile and potential market share,PSS needs to carefully consider the potential challenges which it also creates.It is recommended that extensive work on establishing the potential additional costs involved in undertaking the contract are considered before any further negotiation takes place. 批注[47]:审计和风险委员会已确定，与Badin 床垫制造相 关的专利已过期。这意味着PSS 在生产Badin床垫时使用 的特定弹簧安装不再受到保护，任何人都可以使用。PSS 需要确保Zeelande Health知道 Badin 床垫的设计在这方面 不再受专利保护。 批注[48]:审计和风险委员会强调，担保索赔正在增加。 虽然目前是关于另一种类型的床垫，但必须考虑PSS 可能 面临与Zeelande Health合同相关的更高水平的担保索赔 。担保为十年，但这种类型的床垫的平均寿命只有八年， 可以说，医院使用的床垫的寿命将大大短于八年的平均寿 命。PSS 面临着不得不免费为Zeelande Health 更换大量 床垫的风险。 批注[49]: Zeelande Health 的合同并不总是与PSS 的价值 观一致。PSS 将很难在如下两者之间保持平衡：保持高质 量标准和该合同带来的低利润率下进行产品测试。该合同 也不是针对创新的新产品，而是针对已经制造的Badin床 垫。该合同的引入将影响PSS 的零售伙伴(由于制造已经 达到100%的产能),他们目前是PSS 的Badin 床垫的唯一 客户，这意味着将减少对零售合作伙伴的客户服务。 批注[50]:该合同还可能对PSS 的可持续性价值产生负面 影响。在Zeelande 运送床垫以及收集旧床垫进行回收可能 意味着PSS 更难保持碳中和。 批注[51]:虽然这是一个有趣的提案，为PSS 提供了增加 其形象和潜在市场份额的机会，但PSS 也需要仔细考虑它 带来的潜在挑战。建议在进行任何进一步谈判之前，考虑 制定签订合同所涉及的潜在额外费用的广泛工作。 Task 1b) Slides EMAS-actions PSS would need to take to comply with EMAS · Formalise policies · Objective and target setting · Employee training Notes PSS is already committed to sustainability and the protection and preservation of Zeelande's natural resources. Answer for Task 2 Task 2a) Briefing paper FAO: Chief executive From: Business analyst Briefing paper:Staff Survey In the recent staff survey,employees raised concerns about a possible health and safety issue and suggested ways to improve PSS's performance. Health and safety concern Employees raised a health and safety concern in relation to the chemicals that they may have been exposed to due to the use of the cheaper,lower quality foam supplied by Quellings Foam.This cheaper foam is used in the production of the Charu mattress.These concerns were raised across all four manufacturing plants. Assessment of the issue: This poses a significant threat to PSS's reputation.It may mean that there is a cost- cutting culture within PSS which is introducing high levels of risk and encouraging unethical practices.There are also possible communication issues within PSS if the financial director believed that the foam was on special offer rather than a change in purchasing policy. 批注[70]: Briefing paper格 式 批注[71]: Introduction: 开篇简要说明主题内容。 批注[72]:健康、安全问题： 由于使用Quellings Foam公司提供的廉价、低质量的泡沫 ,员工提出了接触化学品有关的健康和安全问题。这种便 宜的泡沫被用于生产Charu床垫，所有四个制造厂都提出 了这些问题。 批注[73]:健康、安全问题评估： 这是一个潜在的严重问题，对员工和客户都有害。PSS需 要认真管理内外沟通问题，并进行全面调查，以确定更便 宜的泡沫是否是危险的，以及危害的程度。这对PSS的声 誉构成了重大威胁。它可能意味着PSS内存在着一种削减 成本的文化，这种文化带来了很高的风险，并鼓励了不道 德的做法。如果财务董事认为泡沫只是特价而不是采购政 策的变化，说明采购、财务之间也可能存在沟通不畅的问 题 。 Actions that need to be taken: ·The quality control teams at all four manufacturing plants should test the cheaper foam received from Quellings Foam to ascertain if there is a health risk. The test results will determine whether the foam can continue to be used or not. ·Assuming the foam can continue to be used,in future the foam should be tested as part of the routine quality checks that the teams undertake. ·Policies and procedures in relation to testing and quality checks of raw materials need to be reviewed,updated,and communicated to ensure that all staff are aware of the high standard expected of raw materials. ·Policies and procedures in relation to whistleblowing should be reviewed, updated and communicated to ensure that all staff are aware of how to raise concerns in a timely manner. ·Any foam that does present a health risk should be destroyed. PSS will need to consider the impact that any negative publicity will have on the Zeelande Health contract -even though that contract is for a different type of mattress,questions may be raised about the quality of all of PSS's raw materials.It willalso affect EMAS compliance. Assessment of the issue: Employees posing as customers to write fake positive reviews on mattress review websites is unethical and if the public became aware that this was happening, the reputation of PSS would be damaged. Actions that need to be taken: ·An email should be sent to all staff to explain that this is unethical behaviour and should not be undertaken. ·Staff should be given training on acting ethically. · The importance of ethics as part of PSS values and PSS's membership of ZMF should be emphasised in all PSS communications. ·Employee focus groups should be set up to give staff an opportunity to suggest improvements in PSS products which will improve ratings and increase sales.
