Innovative products Staff commented that PSS mattresses are not as innovative as some of the products that are available from its competitors. Assessment of the issue: Although innovation is one of PSS's values,the four mattresses that PSS currently produces do not seem to be as innovative as other products that are available on the market.If staff are aware of this, customers will be too,and this lack of innovation Actions that need to be taken: ·PSS should review its overall strategy in terms of product offerings and future plans. ·Staff forums should be held to share ideas on more innovative mattress offerings. This is in line with PSS values. ·Data on features that consumers want should be analysed from the DTC sales and this data should be incorporated into future strategies and product designs. · Budget should be set aside for formal research into creating more innovative mattresses. · The design and production of more innovative mattresses could be included as a central part of a talent management programme. Summary The health and safety concern and the suggestions for improvement all require immediate steps to be taken by PSS in order to ensure that it complies with regulations and its own values. 批注[78]:产品创新 员工评论说，PSS床垫不如其竞争对手的产品有创新性。 对该问题的评估： 虽然创新是PSS的价值观之一，但PSS目前生产的四款床垫 似乎并不像市场上其他产品那样具有创新性。如果员工意 识到这一点，客户也会意识到，而这种缺乏创新 可能会影响PSS的销售。 批注[79]:需要采取的行动： ●PSS应审查其在产品供应和未来计划方面的总体战略。 ●应举办员工论坛，让员工分享更多创新床垫产品的想法 。这与PSS的价值观是一致的。 ●应该从DTC销售中分析消费者想要的功能数据，这些数据 应该纳入未来的战略和产品设计中。 ●应留出预算用于正式研究创造更多创新床垫。 ●设计和生产更具创新性的床垫可以作为人才管理方案的 核心部分。 批注[80]:总结 健康和安全问题以及改进建议都要求PSS立即采取措施， 以确保遵守法律法规和PSS自己的价值观。 Task 2b) Email To: From: Subject: Human resources director Business analyst Talent management at PSS 批注[81]: Email 格式 Talent management is concerned with attracting,identifying,developing,and retaining staff members that are considered to be particularly important to the future success of PSS.If implemented correctly,a talent management programme could help support PSS's values. 批注[82]:首段介绍段，结合题目引出本邮件的主要内容， 人才管理的价值和挑战，并简单解释概念， However,there are also negative implications of introducing a talent management programme at PSS,which are considered below. Supporting PSS's values Bed mattress manufacturing is a competitive industry that has seen substantial innovation over the last few years.PSS is not currently manufacturing truly innovative products,and this has been noted by employees that raised the issue in the staff survey.Therefore,if PSS is to remain innovative,in line with its values,it may benefit from identifying talented staff and providing them with the opportunity to suggest ways that PSS could become more innovative. Staff that are being developed by a talent management programme and work at an operational level should be better able to identify areas of improvement and innovation within the manufacturing process itself which may save time,money and improve the quality of PSS products. A talent management programme could help to encourage staff at PSS to challenge assumptions underpinning PSS's operations.Such challenges may result in fundamental,longer-term,changes to operations and products which better address customer needs and would help PSS achieve its values. A talent management programme would not only help in improving operations and products,but it willalso enable talented employees to develop in the long term into more senior positions and managerial roles.This will help with staff retention and reduce the risk of talented employees leaving PSS as they believe that better prospects are available at competitor firms. Developing staff for internal promotion can also ensure that the ethos and culture of PSS remains unaffected.PSS values place a strong reliance on ethics and sustainability and the importance of these could be emphasised further through a talent management programme. 批注[83]:价值与挑战各自起小标题，每部分至少写6 点，以确保尽可能多的得分点。 公司目前不够具有创新性，人才可以提供建议创新的方法。 省时省钱，提高产品质量； 冲击根本假设； 满足客户需求； 员工发展&保留； 维持企业文化：强调道德和可持续性 批注[84]:床垫制造业是一个竞争激烈的行业，在过去的 几年里已经有了实质性的创新。PSS目前没有制造真正的 创新产品，这一点已经被员工注意到，他们在员工调查中 提出了这个问题。因此，如果PSS要保持创新，符合其价 值观，它可能会受益于确定有才能的工作人员，并为他们 提供机会，建议PSS如何变得更加创新。 批注[85]:由人才管理方案培养并在业务层面工作的工作 人员应该能够更好地识别制造过程本身的改进和创新领域 ,这可能节省时间、金钱并提高PSS产品的质量。 批注[86]:可能有助于鼓励PSS的员工挑战支撑PSS运营的 假设。这些挑战可能会导致业务和产品发生根本性的长期 变化，从而更好地满足客户需求，并帮助PSS实现其价值 批注[87]:人才管理计划不仅有助于改善运营和产品，而 且还将使有才能的员工在长期内发展到更高级的职位和管 理角色。这将有助于留住员工，降低有才能的员工离开 PSS的风险，因为他们相信竞争对手的公司有更好的前景。 批注[88]:培养内部晋升的员工也可以确保PSS的精神和文 化不受影响。PSS 价值观强烈依赖于道德和可持续性和这 些的重要性可以通过人才管理计划进一步强调。 Challenges Whilst a talent management programme may be motivating for PSS staff;it will also make them even more attractive to competitors.Therefore,PSS may need to increase salaries and offer other incentives alongside a talent management programme to avoid losing staff that PSS has spent time and money investing in.This is important as it already seems to be the case that competitor firms are paying higher salaries.The loss of key staff members could harm future business plans and damage PSS's competitive position. PSS would need to consider succession planning for staff.A talent management programme which does not offer the prospect of a more senior role in PSS could be demotivating to staff rather than leading to improved performance.The current induction and on-the-job training offered to staff is focused on their current roles and there does not appear to be any formal development of staff.This is not consistent with a talent management programme and would require a change in training mindset. As a talent management programme can be expensive in terms of time,resources, and financial costs,PSS will need to ensure that the benefits from introducing a talent management programme outweigh these costs.This means that PSS will need to set clear objectives for the talent management programme at the outset and will need to monitor the success of the programme at regular intervals It is important that PSS staff are motivated to engage in the talent management programme.If staff are not engaged in the programme it will not deliver its objectives and will not succeed.PSS needs to ensure that staff have time to invest in the talent management programme as the plants are currently working at 100%capacity and the operations director has suggested that staff are already very busy.If the talent management programme places additional time burdens and increased workloads on staff,this may be viewed as a disincentive. ConclusionA successful talent management programme will require management to listen to suggestions that are being made as it will only be successful if it is led and supported at board level. Conclusion There could be benefits to PSS if a talent management programme is implemented, but PSS needs to set clear objectives for the programme and clearly communicate progression opportunities to staff.Due to the cost of implementing the programme and the current heavy workloads of staff,it may be better if PSS improves internal communication and offers regular staff forums rather than a formal talent management programme. 批注[89]:挑战： √员工可能会流失到竞争对手那里，可能需要加薪；竞争 对手已经支付了更高的薪水； √必要的继任规划； √ 当前的培训心态需要改变； √ 昂贵成本可能超过收益； √监控成功； √积极的参与者； √管理层需要倾听 批注[90]:虽然人才管理计划可能会激励PSS员工；这也将 使他们对竞争对手更具吸引力。因此，PSS可能需要提高 工资，并在人才管理计划的同时提供其他激励措施，以避 免失去PSS花费时间和金钱投资的员工。这一点很重要， 因为竞争对手似乎已经在支付更高的薪水。关键工作人员 的流失可能会损害未来的商业计划，损害PSS的竞争地位 批注[91]: PSS需要考虑工作人员的继任规划。 不能顺利升职的员工将会被打消积极性。目前向工作人员 提供的入职和在职培训集中在他们目前的角色上，似乎没 有对工作人员进行任何正式的发展。这与人才管理方案不 一致，需要改变培训观念。 批注[92]:由于人才管理计划在时间、资源和财务成本方 面可能是昂贵的，PSS需要确保引入人才管理计划的收益 超过这些成本。这意味着PSS需要在一开始就为人才管理 计划设定明确的目标，并需要定期监测计划的成功。 批注[93]:要激励员工参与人才管理计划。如果工作人员 不参与该方案，将无法实现目标。PSS需要确保员工有时 间投资于人才管理计划，因为工厂目前正以100%的产能工 作，运营总监指出员工已经很忙了。如果人才管理方案给 工作人员带来额外的时间负担和工作量增加，这可能被视 为一种不利因素。 批注[94]:一个成功的人才管理计划需要管理层听取正在 提出的建议，因为只有在董事会层面的领导和支持下，它 才能成功。 批注[95]:结论段 将上述正文内容中的价值贡献和挑战的核心点用3句话左 右论述一下。 Task 3 The finance director has emailed you with details of two key issues identified by the audit and risk committee during its latest review:data from online customers and internal control weaknesses.The finance director will discuss these issues at the next board meeting and has asked you to assist him in preparing for the meeting. Benefits of data analysis

1. Driving innovation If PSS analyses the data that it gathers through its website in relation to customer orders,customer and website visitor activity,preferences and feedback,it can identify patterns and trends that will give better insight into customer behaviour on a continual basis.Analysing this data willreduce the time taken to decide on the direction that PSS should follow in relation to its strategy and future plans.If PSS had meaningful information that was analysed continually,it could know much earlier what consumers look for in a mattress and this could then be used to evolve PSS's marketing and influence future PSS products. 2.Building loyalty The bed mattress industry usually has low levels of brand loyalty but if PSS could analyse a wide range of customer and website visitor data continuously and tailor products and services based on the data collected,customer loyalty may be improved. Competitive advantage It may be possible for PSS to gain competitive advantage and an increased market share of the mattress sector if the analysis of data helps to identify trends and preferences that have not been identified by rivals 批注[99]: Briefing notes 格式 批注[100]:通过PSS网站从客户和潜在客户那里收集到大 量不同的信息被称为大数据。而对这些数据进行分析和使 用，既会为PSS 提供潜在的好处，同时也会带来相应的威 胁。 批注[101]:其中的优势就包括推动创新，背景信息中多次 提到，企业当前处在竞争非常激烈的环境中，只有创新， 才能够给企业带来一定的竞争优势。 而通过对收集到的数据进行分析，可以识别客户的购买模 式和趋势，从而能够更好的持续性的去了解客户的行为， 更早的知道客户对床垫的需求，对PSS 未来营销策略的建 立和产品的发展都带来了积极的影响。 批注[102]:客户信息的收集还可以帮助企业建立客户的忠 诚度。 床垫行业的品牌忠诚度通常较低，但如果PSS 能够持续性 的收集数据，并根据收集到的数据进行分析，为客户提供 定制性的服务，会非常有利于提高客户的品牌忠诚。此外 ,还可以通过电子营销的方式，实现营销策略在成本上的 有效性。 批注[103]:如果数据分析能够帮助企业确定竞争对手尚未 确定的趋势和偏好，可能会为企业创造竞争优势，从而提 高在当前行业的市场份额。 Threats of big data 1.Security Holding large quantities of data opens PSS up to the threat of virus and hackers. As PSS holds personal information from customers it must ensure that this data is held securely which,in turn,is likely to increase costs.The audit and risk committee has said that it is unclear what controls are currently in place.This needs to be investigated and a stronger,more transparent control system needs to be implemented as soon as possible. 2.Cost It could cost a significant amount of time and money to interpret data that may be of limited use to PSS.Due to the competitive nature of the mattress industry,it is unlikely that PSS would be able to pass this cost onto customers.Any time delay in interpreting data willreduce its value and reduce any advantage that PSS has over its rivals. 3.Wrong decisions It would be easy to make the wrong decisions based on the information thathas been gathered. Issue 1:Guarantee claims The lack of detailed monitoring and review of guarantee claims is a weakness in the management controls.As the increase in claims has not been investigated it is not clear what has caused the increase. It has not been established whether the mattresses that were the subject of the guarantee claims were manufactured at a particular plant or not.It is unclear whether the mattresses that were subject to guarantee claims were old or not More guarantee claims are expected for the Abhu mattress as it has an average life of six years,but the guarantee is for a ten-year period.But this would not explain the substantial increase in guarantee claims.A full investigation is needed to understand the cause of the increase. If the guarantee claims relate to recently manufactured Abhu mattresses,there is likely to be a problem with either the raw materials used in that mattress that have not been detected by the quality control teams or a problem in the manufacture process which has not been discovered by the completion teams. Detailed monitoring of guarantee claims is a critical aspect of the control environment and is necessary to ensure that poor quality products do not go unchecked without suitable corrective action.If problems are left unnoticed and unchecked over several months,these could become a major operationa issue for PSS.The lack of management understanding in relation to the importance of detailed monitoring of guarantee claims is a concern as problems with operations may go undetected. Recommendations: ·The finance team should report guarantee claims on a monthly basis to the board of directors for review. ·These reports should detail the age of the mattress involved,the type of mattress and the reason for the guarantee claim. ·If it appears that there is a manufacturing fault,this should be referred back to the operations director and the relevant manufacturing plant for a full and immediate investigation.The relevant manufacturing process and policies will need a careful review.A manufacturing fault would be harmful to PSS's reputation as sold mattresses may need to be recalled and replaced ·Actions that have been taken in response to the report should then be reported back to the finance team. ·All actions must be followed up by the finance and operations directors to ensure that issues are actively addressed. 批注[108]:质保索赔问题 缺乏详细的监管和审核是管理控制上的一个重大缺陷。因 为质保索赔的增加还没有调查，尚不清楚索赔增长的原因。 目前也不清楚被索赔的床垫是否是某一个特定工厂生产的 床垫。也不清楚被索赔的床垫是使用很多年的还是新的。 预期更多的索赔来自与Abhu 床垫，因为它的平均寿命是 六年，但是质保却是十年。但是这不能解释质保索赔的显 著增长。为了了解索赔增长的原因，仍然需要进行全面的 调 查 。 如果该质保索赔是与最近生产的Abhu 床垫相关，很有可 能是因为原材料或者是因为生产流程问题。 详细的监管是控制环境的重要方面，并且确保质量不过关 的产品能够及时被发现。如果存在的问题几个月都没有被 注意到，这意味着PSS 在运营方面的重大问题。 批注[ 109] : 质保索赔问题的改进建议： 1.财务团队应当每个月向董事会报告质保索赔问题。 2.这些报告应当包含的细节包括：涉及床垫的年限，类型 以及索赔的原因。 3.如果是因为生产问题，应当反馈给运营董事以及相关生 产企业立即做全面调查。应当仔细审核相关的生产流程和 政策。生产问题将会影响PSS 公司的声誉，涉事床垫可 能需要召回并替换。

4. 已经采取的措施应当反馈给财务团队。 5.财务董事和运营董事应当追踪采取的所有措施，以确保 问题积极解决。 Issue 2:Patents Patents are an important element of the PSS business and are used to ensure that the PSS mattresses are distinct in a competitive market. The patents also offer protection to PSS for its own spring arrangements,mattress layer compositions,foam combinations and mattress covers.By allowing patents to expire,PSS runs the risk that competitors could copy these elements of PSS design and manufacture.Once a patent has expired there is no legal protection offered to PSS and competitors would be able to make use of the previously patented processes without having to pay PSS a royalty fee.If PSS continues to claim that processes are patented,when the patent has expired,this could have a negative impact on PSS's reputation. Recommendations: ·Regular checks of patents should be undertaken and allocated to an appropriate member of staff. ·A report on each check should be made to the board of directors. ·A review of all patents that exist should take place and it should be established whether any designs or processes currently being used in the manufacturing plants are covered by patents held by other companies.If this is found to be the case,this should be reported to the board of directors as soon as possible. ·The board of directors should assess the impact of the patents that have expired in relation to the Badin mattress and establish the scale of the problem and formulate a strategy to deal with the issue. Zeelande Health is made aware that some of the processes involved inproducing this mattress are no longer patented in case Zeelande Health believethese patents are in place and that has contributed to its decision to enter intoa contract with PSS.·As these expired patents relate to the Badin mattress,it is important that Zeelande Health is made aware that some of the processes involved in producing this mattress are no longer patented in case Zeelande Health believe these patents are in place and that has contributed to its decision to enter into a contract with PSS. 批注[110]: 专利权问题 专利权确保PSS 的床垫在竞争市场上是独特的。专利也 为 PSS 提供了保护。考虑到专利过期，PSS 面临着竞争 对手复制 PSS 的设计和生产的风险。一旦专利过期，缺 少了法定保护，竞争对手能够利用之前的专利流程，而不 需要向PSS 支付使用费。如果专利已经过期而PSS 继续 宣称受专利保护，这会影响PSS 的声誉。 批注[11]: 专利权问题的改进建议： 1.相关人员应当定期检查专利。 2.每一次检查应当报告董事会。 3.对专利进行审核，并且明确当前生产工厂使用的任何与 设计和流程相关的专利是否由其他公司持有专利权。如果 是，需要立即报告给董事会。 4.董事会应当评估过期专利给 Badin 床垫带来的影响，明 确问题的大小，并且制定相应战略。 5.因为这些过期专利与Badin 床垫相关，需要让 Zeelande Health 知道生产这款床垫的专利已经过期，不 再受专利保护，防止Zeelande Heath 认为这些专利仍然 有效，并且是因此与PSS 签订合同。 GOLDEN EDUCAT ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Strategic Business Leader-Specimen Paper 2 解析 Introduction Rail Co is a public sector rail company responsible for delivering passenger rail services within Beeland.Rail Co is govermed by a supreme goveming committee known as the Rail Co Trust Board. The main parties involved in the agency relationship of Rail Co are the government,in the form of the Ministry for Transport of Beeland as the principal and the Rail Co Trust Board and the board of directors,comprising a mix of executive and non-executive officers,acting as the agents.As stated above,ultimately the chief executive of Rail Co is responsible to the government for Rail Co's management and stewardship of the public funding it receives.It is important to note that the way in which Rail Co is regulated and governed is focused on value for money rather than on the achievement of profits. There is also a further agency relationship within Rail Co,in that the Rail Co board of directors is accountable to the Rail Co Trust Board.It is the responsibility of the Rail Co Trust Board to set a range of performance targets each year and to hold the Rail Co board to account for the effective and efficient use of the funds allocated by govemment and by the fare paying passengers. A further aspect of the agency relationship in Rail Co is that the ultimate principal is the taxpayer and the customers,in that it is he or she who pays for the railservice and Rail Co exists for their benefit.It is the ultimate responsibility of the board of Rail Co to ensure that Rail Co carries out its passenger services on behalf of those who fund the activity(mainly taxpayers)and those who use and pay for the services(rail passengers).Funders (ie. taxpayers)and customers are sometimes the same people(for instance,taxpayers who commute on Rail Co's trains) but sometimes they are not,and this could give rise to disagreements on how much is spent and on what particular provisions.Rail Co has a responsibility to all of its principals to deliver its services efficiently,effectively and offering good value for money.As is evidenced in the recent article in the Beeland Herald,many customers are not satisfied with the value for money offered by Rail Co. It is the responsibility of the Rail Co Trust Board to ensure that the key outcomes of Rail Co are delivered by setting a range of performance targets,against which performance is measured periodically 批注[批注60]:Briefing paper 格式 批注[批注61]:Rail Co属于政府机构，在公共部门内部运 作。就战略目标而言，RailCo 的存在是为了执行政府在客 运铁路服务方面的政策。因此其目标在很大程度上取决于 Beeland 政治领袖。归根到底，RailCo 的CEO有责通过Rail Co 信托委员会向Beeland 政府报告Rail Co 如何管理其获 得的公共资金。 批注[批注62]: Rail Co代理关系中政府，以Beeland 交通 部为委托方，Rail Co 信托委员会和董事会由执行和非执 行董事为代理方。RailCo 的CEO负责管理收到的公共资金。 Rail Co的管理方式关注的是物有所值，而不是利润，因为 它是公共事业部门。 批注[批注63]:Rail Co内部还存在着进一步的代理关系， 即 RailCo 董事会对Rail Co信托委员会负责。Rail Co 信 托委员会的责任是每年制定一系列的业绩指标，Rail Co 董事会应有效地使用政府拨款及火车票的收入。 批注[批注64]:RailCo 代理关系即纳税人和客户。Rail Co 董事会的最终责任是确保Rail Co 代表纳税人和乘客进行 客运服务。纳税人和乘客有时是同一群人，但有时不是， 所以这可能会在支出多少和具体条款上产生分歧。Rail Co 有责任向其所有负责人提供并提供物有所值的服务。正如 Beeland Herald最近发表的一篇文章所说的那样，许多客 户对铁路公司提供的资金价值不满意。 批注[批注65]:Rail Co信托委员会有责任确保RailCo 的 主要成果是通过制定一系列绩效目标来实现的，并定期对 绩效进行衡量。 (b)The role and value of the non-executive directors on the board ofRail Co Not-for-profit or public sector organisations must also be directed and controlled appropriately,as the decisions and actions of a few individuals can affect many individuals,groups and organisations which have little or no influence over them.Public sector organisations,such as Rail Co,have a duty to serve the government,but must act in a way that treats stakeholders fairly. The non-executive directors(NEDs)are not employees of the company and are not involved in its day-to-day running.The non-executive directors usually receive a flat fee for their services,and are engaged under a contract for service. The role of NEDs is to provide a balancing influence on the board of directors and help to minimise conflicts of interest.The Higgs Report,published in 2003,summarised their role as:[ - to contribute to the strategic plan - to scrutinise the performance of the executive directors - to provide an external perspective on risk management - to deal with people issues,such as the future shape of the board and resolution of conflicts. Importantly,the NEDs should have high ethical standards and act with integrity and probity.Their main role is to support the executive directors of Rail Co and monitor its conduct,demonstrating a willingness to listen,question, debate and challenge. It is recognised as best practice that a company should have more non-executive directors than executive directors. This is the case for RailCo,as can be seen from its structure highlighted on the Rail Co website.The NEDs of Rail Co are responsible for running the four board commitees which are set up to monitor the performance of Rail Co in key areas such as health and safety and audit and risk management The NEDs are also responsible for setting and reviewing the directors'remuneration and evaluating the corporate governance structure and activities of Rail Co and ensuring that the board is adequately govemed,structured and staffed.This is particularly relevant and important to a public sector company such as Rail Co,where transparency and public scrutiny are prevalent. 批注[批注66]:题号和副标题可以很好的让考官区分答案 的归属 批注[批注67]:非营利或公共部门必须得到适当的控制，因 为少数个人的决定和行动可能影响到许多个人、团体和组 织 。RailCo 等公共部门有义务为政府服务，但必须公平对 待利益相关者。 ——处理人的问题，如董事会未来的形态和冲突的解决方 案 。 批注[批注71]:NED 应有高道德标准。他们的主要职责是支 持 Rail Co的执行董事并监督其行为。 批注[批注72]:公司的NED数量应大于执行董事。 Rail Co 的非执行董事们负责4个委员会。这写委员会负责监控 Rail Co关键领域的绩效，例如健康、安全、审计和风险的 管理。他们还负责设置和审查董事的报酬、评估Rail Co 的公司治理结构和活动，确保董事会管理适当。对于Rail Co 等公共部门来说，这一点尤其重要，因为在这些组织中， 透明度和公众监督非常普遍。 The chairman of Rail Co is also a non-executive director and plays a key role in the business.The chairman has theultimate role of leading the board,whilst the chief executive leads the business.Therefore,the roles of chairmanand chiefexecutive are complementary and interlinked.The NEDs of Rail Co can add value to the business by:-broadening the horizons and experience of existing executive directors,particularly if they come from a wide range of both public and private sector organisations.- facilitating the cross-fertilisation of ideas,particularly in terms of business strategy and planning.-playing a vital part to play in appraising and commenting on Rail Co's achievement of value for money and advising Having thoroughly reviewed the Rail Co performance data,the recent board meeting minutes of Rail Co and the transport report in the Beeland Heralda number of internal control weaknesses can be highlighted. First,there appears to be a serious weakness in the control of passengers accessing trains without tickets.This is referred to in the Beeland Herald transport report,where it is mentioned that this issue had already been raised in the last two annual reports.This creates a significant business risk which does not seem to have been acted upon or mitigated by the Rail Co board.It has been highlighted for over two years that Rail Co believes that significant numbers of passengers are travelling on RailCo's network without tickets.A key internal control weakness would appear to be that approximately 40%of Rail Co's stations do not operate ticket barriers,allowing the potential for customer ticket fraud.This potentially will have seriously damaging consequences on the performance of Rail Co in that revenues are not being optimised. Second,there appears to be a weakness in staff management and safety procedures,in that there has been an increase in the number of injuries to staff reported by Rail Co in the last year.This is evidenced in the recent performance information analysed by one of my colleagues in Exhibit 3.Although Kim Lun has assured the BHSO that a thorough investigation would be undertaken immediately,it indicates that Rail Co has potential weaknesses in safety procedures and also in staff training procedures.The performance statistics indicate that Rail Co has lower levels of training than its competitors and Kim Lun(non-executive director)has noted that there had been a lack of investment in the training of staff in the last three years,which is also a key internal control weakness and one which could seriously impede the performance of RailCo.Staff who are not appropriately trained are more likely to have accidents and to make mistakes.Again,this will have serious repercussions on the overall performance of RailCo and could have seriously damaging impact upon reputation if serious injuries occur. A further intemal control weakness could be seen as the lack of investment in online booking systems.Several other national train operators offer online booking facilities and evidence suggested that this had positively impacted upon revenue growth and customer satisfaction in all of these businesses (Appendix 3).Lack of focus upon IT investment and development in key strategic information systems could be seen as an internal control weakness and could hamper the long-term performance of Rail Co._ 批注[批注94]:在控制无票进入列车的乘客方面，存在严重 的弱点。这个问题已经在最近两份年度报告中提出。这造 成了一种重大的商业风险，而Rail Co 董事会似乎没有采 取行动减轻这种风险。两年多来，RailCo 一直强调，大量 乘客在网上不买票。造成这个的缺陷是大约40%的RailCo 的车站没有设置检票机器，导致乘客逃票。严重损害 Rail Co的业绩。 批注[批注95]:第二，员工管理和安全程序方面存在弱点， 今年相比Rail Co 去年报告的员工受伤人数有所增加。这 表明Rail Co在安全程序和员工培训方面存在弱点。绩效 数据表明Rail Co 比竞争对手培训水平低，Kim Lun(NED) 已经指出在过去的三年缺乏员工培训是一个关键的内部控 制弱点，严重阻碍Rail Co员工的绩效。不适当的培训更 有可能发生事故。 批注[批注96]:对在线预订系统缺乏投资也是内部控制弱 点之一。其他几个国家火车运营商提供在线预订设施且收 入增长，客户满意度提升(附录3)。缺乏IT 投资和发展的 关键战略信息系统是一个内部控制的弱点，可能阻碍 Rail Co的长期绩效。 A further internal control weakness could be the current pay structure.Poorly paid staff who are dissatisfied will leave or may take strike action.HR policies on fair pay could be considered to be weak if they are not commensurate with the expected pay rate. experience as a CE,having worked at this level since 2000.As a CE of a multinational oil company,he willhave significant experience of managing a highly complex business environment and multiple stakeholder influences,including government.Additionally,his recent CE experience is within the rail industry working for JPS Express in Jayland.Notably,he led the privatisation of JPS trains in 2013, highly positive capability and one which is highly desirable in the current operating climate of Rail Co. Notably,Candidate A demonstrates key skills in change leadership and human performance management.It is clear that a change programme will need to be carried out by the new CE in a number of key 客户忠诚度、品牌等+CRM在运营与分析方面的具体应用) CRM 的目的是建立关系，以影响客户的获取、保留和忠诚， 从而与这些客户建立1对1的关系。这种“以客户为中心” 的方法，包括与Rail Co的客户建立牢固的关系，以及在整 个组织内收集、存储和共享这些客户的信息，这可能会提 高客户的忠诚度。 电子客户关系管理，以在线票务销售系统的形式，将使铁 路公司通过互联网与客户建立尽可能多的联系成为可能。 GOLDEN EDUCATION这种对铁路公司客户的互联网支持可能会采取预售信息的 形式，如时刻表信息、购票服务和售后支持，如在线退款 或客户账户查询。互联网将使Rail Co 能够经常与客户联系， 从而使我们能够运营和维护详细的客户数据库，从而帮助 我们进一步发展更好的客户关系。对于铁路公司来说，互 联网的进一步价值可以从更快的信息流(实时销售数据)和 更一致的通信中看到，这可以通过它的使用而产生。 GOLDEN EDUCATION 客户关系管理系统的有效使用可以帮助铁路公司建立关系， 同时也有助于业务的盈利能力。我们应该设定的一些目标 包括留住现有乘客，提高客户对我们提供的服务的满意度， 以及提高客户的忠诚度。获得优质服务的顾客会保持忠诚， 另外一个好处是他们通过谈论公司的服务提供免费广告。 运营型CRM 包括面向客户的应用程序，如销售队伍自动化、 企业营销自动化和客户服务和支持。铁路公司可能会考虑 实施客户呼叫中心，这也是运营型CRM的一个组成部分。 通过这种方式，可以记录与客户的所有交互，使RailCo 能 够收集更多关于客户的数据，从而跟踪客户。 分析型CRM 也可以用来分析通过操作型客户关系管理产生 的数据，以建立客户的形象。分析型 CRM 包括对存储在数 据仓库中的客户数据的捕获、存储、提取、处理、解释和 报告。这将使我们能够研究客户的行为模式，以便制定针 对特定客户群体的营销和促销策略，例如学生、老年人、 日常通勤者或休闲旅行者。 Risk assessment_ A detaled cost analysis mus been undertaken to assess the financial viability of an investment in an online ticket sales system for Rail Co.Further risks should also be considered including the customer perception of such a system and also a stakeholder mapping exercise wilneed to be undertaken.The main riskis that austomers wil notuse the system,either due toa reluctance to changepurchasinghabitsordue to a lackofawareness of the facity.These risks can be overcome through adequate Exhibit 1 批注[5]:材料1: Fisk 的采访日志，探讨了她的领导方式，以及董事会扮演 的角色。以下为CEO的语录：成为CEO 并不是一件容易的事情。尽管Corjetz公司获得了一定程度上的成功，但是仅仅靠“企业家精神”并不能 持续维持客户关系或者员工满意度。客户投诉率和员工离 职率已经达到10年来的最高值。Magda 说自从她加入公司 以来，带领公司上市，并且使得员工满意度尽量提高(20X2 年达到最高)。在做CEO 的时候，你优先考虑的事项有哪些?十年前，Corjetz公司是等级制，高层和员工之间缺乏沟通。 CEO 的想法是让员工拥有更多自主权去做决策，尤其是在 客户关系管理上。员工的才能没有很好地发挥，同时公司也没有留住高质量的员工。外部环境快速变化，顾客流失 量大，那么其实是需要员工更好地经营客户关系。有什么面临的问题，以及你是如何解决的?领导力集中化的时候，应该更关注惩罚punishment 而不是 奖励reward。高质量员工正在流失，我想采用每个月在线 上“约见董事会”的模式，加强员工和董事会之间的交流， 更好地激励员工。我们成立了员工代表委员会，他们可以提出想法。员工培 训项目也在持续更新，帮助他们提升自己。我认为，客户 服务的关键因素在于员工。我为我们公司这种开放沟通的 方式感到自豪，Corjetz的文化体现在改变和创新上。我们 的乘务组每4年会更换新款的制服，在时尚和专业度上遥 遥领先。为什么说Corjez 公司的董事会至关重要?非执行董事NEDs 发挥了至关重要的作用，体现出董事会 成员的多元化。我们确保NEDs 的独立性，让股东们看到 强有力的公司治理体系。与此同时，我们的执行董事EDs 也是非常多元化的，有不同的企业背景和经验。我为自己 是一名Corland的CEO感到自豪，同时我会持续努力提升 Corjetz 公司领导团队的多元化。来源于商业杂志，是一篇对Corjetz 公司的CEO,Magda Fisk 的采访日志，探讨了她的领导方式，以及董事会扮演 的角色。 以下为CEO的语录： 成为CEO 并不是一件容易的事情。尽管Corjetz公司获得 了一定程度上的成功，但是仅仅靠“企业家精神”并不能 持续维持客户关系或者员工满意度。客户投诉率和员工离 职率已经达到10年来的最高值。Magda 说自从她加入公司 以来，带领公司上市，并且使得员工满意度尽量提高 (20X2 年达到最高)。 在做CEO 的时候，你优先考虑的事项有哪些? 非执行董事NEDs 发挥了至关重要的作用，体现出董事会 成员的多元化。我们确保NEDs 的独立性，让股东们看到 强有力的公司治理体系。与此同时，我们的执行董事EDs 也是非常多元化的，有不同的企业背景和经验。我为自己 是一名Corland的CEO感到自豪，同时我会持续努力提升 Corjetz 公司领导团队的多元化。 Corland Business Review-August 20X3 Airline Focus Interview with Magda Fisk,CEO of Corjetz Taking over from Eric Madson as CEO of Corjetz was never going to be easy for Magda Fisk.Although the brothers had bulit strong foundations for the success of Corjetz,their 'entrepreneurial spirit and drive'could not maintain their early success in customer and staff satisfaction.Customer complaints and staff turnover were at their highest levels 10 years ago. Since joining,Magda has led Corjetz through a successful stock market listing,and customer satisfaction ratings hit their highest level in 20X2. What were your priorities when you took on the role of CEO? 'Ten years ago,Corjetz was hierarchical and there was no connection between senior leaders and staff.I wanted staff to have autonomy to make decisions for areas which they were experts in,particularly in customer relationship management.We weren't makingadequate use of the knowledge and talent that existed in the business nor were we focusing on attracting and retaining high quality staft.Our customer service approach had failed to change with the rapidly evolving external environment,so we had to trust staff to lead us on how to manage customer relationships.' What were the problems you had to overcome and how didyou address them? 'Leadership was centralised and there was a focus on punishment rather than reward.We were losing talented staff who felt that thelr voices were not being heard and were afraid to make mistakes.I introduced the monthly 'meet the board online chats,where board members host an interactive chat on the Corjetz intranet site to highlight key initiatives in the business. This was designed to encourage communication and motivation. We have set up staff representative committes where staff are actively encouraged to share ideas.Training programmes are constantly re-designed to ensure staff have the latest skilIs. I belleve that empowered and engaged staff are the driver of customer service.I am proud that as an organisation we encourage open and honest dialogue between staff and leaders. Corjetz has a dynamic culture which embraces change and innovation.We even ask our cabin crew to re-design their own uniforms every four years.We hold a competition,and the most popular design is selected and professionally createdIt's very popular with staff and fiercely competitive.' Why is the board so important to Corjetz? Our non-executive directors(NEDs)play avital role inthe strategic successof the business and diversity of members is critical in such a dynamic business environment.We are fully focused on ensuring our NEDs are fully independent,to provide shareholders with the reassurance that our corporate governance is sound and robust.Diversity also extends to our executive board members,all of whom come from a wide range of backgrounds and have a depth of experience.I am also proud to be one of a growing number of female CEOs in Corland andi wil continue to promote dlversity in Corjetz's leadership.' Exhibit 2 Proposal overview Investment in artificlal intelllgence(Al)fight simulation technology to dellver pllot training. The alm is to reduce human input and hours needed to traln pllots and to significantly reduce costs ofobtaining/maintaining pllot certifications. Pllot training data Current Proposed (20X3) Al training Initial investment cost NA $500 milion Time taken to train new pilots 12 months 8 months Cost per pllot (initial training for all new pllots) $500,000 $280,000 Cost per pllot (annual update for all pilots) $45,000 $20,000 Training costs each year for Corjetz's pilots is currently over $140 milion. Key features of Al simulation-based pllot tralning Use of AI flight simulators to developpilot in-light skills,providing more realistic simulations. This reduces the need for costly and time consuming live in-fight training. The Al automatically collects training performance data about every aspect of individual pilot performance and behaviour.The data gathered is used to continually update the Al simulation models to improve the training scenarios. -Instructors can use the intellgence gathered to quickly monitor the effectiveness of the training and to tailor it to individual needs. Potential issues A human trainer is needed to give instructions and program the software.This is a highly specialist job and there is limited availability of instructors worldwide. Capability of replicating decision-making in all scenarios is still uncertain,as there are so many variables to be considered when fiying a commercial aircraft. Regulation is moving more slowly than the technology.Due to the high safety level needed in commercial aviation,extensive testing and certifications of pilot competency wil be necessary. - Stakeholders will need to be convinced of the quality,safety and benefits of using Al. Current successful application One Hundrian country has invested the equlvalent of over $2-5 bilion in Al pilot training systems for its military pilots.Feedback has been highly positive in terms of the quality of training and cost reductions. Summary This is an opportunity to reduce a significant cost to the business.It is also an opportunity to gain significant competitive advantage,being the first commercial airline to introduce such cutting-edge technology. 批注[6]:材料2: 是一项投资计划，关于利用AI(人工智能)模拟技术来驱 动飞行员的训练。可以减少人力投入，同时削减相关成本。 数据比较： 引入该项目，有初始投资成本，但是缩短了飞行员训练时 间，同时削减了每个飞行员相关支出。 AI模拟技术训练的主要特征： -使用AI飞行模拟器，可以提高飞行员技能，提供更多可 靠估计。有效地减少了在真实的飞行舱内的训练时间和成 本 。 -A可以自动收集飞行员的个人数据，并且持续更新，使 飞行模拟器训练效果更佳。 -导师可以利用收集到的数据迅速监测训练的有效性，同 时满足个人需求。 潜在的问题： -需要一名专业人员对软件系统给出指示，这对专业技术 的要求比较高，可能不好招聘到相关人员。 -在不同情况下是否能够复制决策存在不确定性，因为实 际飞行过程中很多变动因素需要进一步考虑。 -规章制度比技术的进度更加缓慢，由于航空业需要高安 全性的保证，对飞行员能力和资质的相关测试至关重要。 -使用AI需要让相关利益者们相信其质量、安全性和好处。 现目前的成功运用： 某国家投资了$2.5 bilion到飞行员军事训练系统中，在训 练质量和成本缩减方面的反馈非常好。 总结 ： 这是一个缩减成本的好机会，同时也能获得竞争优势，成 为引入该技术的第一家航空公司。 Exhibit 3 To: Chief finance officer From: Risk manager Date: September 20X3 Subject: Principal risk review Good day As you know,our principal risks continue to evolve in such a fast-moving industry.Therefore,we must continually adapt our risk response activities.In our latest review,the risk and assurance team has concluded that the level of risk associated with the fllowing principal risk areas has increased: CausesExplanationPrincipal risk Causes Explanation Cyber attack-Hardware failure Data centre outage User errorExternal attackon or failure ofcritical technologyThe increasing sophistication of Cyber attack -Hardware failure Data centre outage User error External attack on or failure of critical technology organised crime groups means vulnerability regarding avallability, confidentiality,and integrity of data. This task is carried out by Corjetz's Audit Committee,which is a committee normally made up mostly of independent NEDs which provides an overallcheck on the work of executive management in the areas of financial management and reporting and that suitable systems of risk management and control are in place.Corjetz also has a robust ERM framework in place,which is overseen and monitored by the NEDs. 批注[29]:风险监管和管理 NEDs应当确保财务信息真实公允和完整。他们还必须确 保财务控制和风险管理体系存在、完善。 这项任务由Corjetz的审计委员会执行，该委员会通常主要 由独立的NEDs组成，对财务管理和报告领域的执行管理 层的工作进行全面检查，并确保适当的风险管理和控制系 统存在。Corjetz还拥有强大的企业风险管理框架，由 NEDs监管。 Remuneration and appointment of executive directors Our NEDs also have responsibility for the appointment of new executive directors and for deciding the remuneration of executive directors and senior managers.NEDs play a key role in succession planning for key directorial positions in the organisation.NEDs will also play a role in any contractual negotiations and arrangements for senior positions in the business and will be involved in any disciplinary actions needed at board and senior management level. 批注[30]:执行董事的任命和薪酬 NEDs还负责任命新的执行董事，并决定执行董事和高级 管理人员的薪酬。NEDs在组织中对关键董事职位的继任 计划发挥关键作用。NEDs还将在企业高级职位的任何合 同谈判和安排方面发挥作用，并将参与对董事会和高级管 理层所需的任何监管行为。 Slide 2 (ii)Assess the importance to Corjetz of having a diverse board 批注[31]: Corjetz 公司有一个多元化董事会的重要性 Importance of having a diverse board ·Wider pool of experience and knowledge ·More effective decision making ·Improve reputation ·Improve investor relations Wider pool of experience and knowledge Promoting diversity opens up a much wider pool of talented and experienced board members.This is citically important at a time of increasing competition in the global airline business environment,where a diverse board with varied experience and perspectives of aviation and the wider general business community will mean Corjetz is better placed to understand not only the airline environment but also the wider business environment and the stakeholders within it. More effective decision making A diverse board helps to reduce the potential for 'groupthink'which can occur when board members are either over familiar with each other's ways of thinking or because there is too much similarity in their opinions,styles or beliefs.Diversity should assist in approaching issues from a much wider perspective and set of opinions and therefore there is more chance that challenge willtake place.This should,in turm,result in greater debate and consideration of strategic matters which should result in better quality decisions. 批注[32]:更广泛的经验和知识 促进多元化相当于打开了一个才能与经验兼具的董事会成 员人才库。在全球航空商业环境竞争日益激烈的情况下， 这一点至关重要，因为多元化的董事会拥有不同的经验和 视角。更广泛的商业社区意味着Corjetz不仅能更好地了解 航空业面临的环境，还能更好地了解更广泛的商业环境及 各个利益相关者。 批注[33]:更有效的决策 多元化的董事会有助于减少“群体思维”的可能性，这种情 况可能发生在董事会成员过于熟悉彼此的思维方式，或者 因为他们的观点、风格或信仰过于相似时。多元化有助于 从更广泛的视角和意见方面来解决问题，因此更有可能发 生挑战。反过来，这应导致对战略事项进行更多的辩论和 考虑，从而产生更优质的决策。 Improve reputation 批注[34]:提升声誉 Having a diverse board membership sends out a positive message to our external stakeholders that we are a sound corporate citizen and that we value the opinions of a diverse range of sources.This can help to position us as a socially responsible organisation that is non-discriminatory and by reflecting the diversity of the society which we serve,we willachieve a better social contact with our external environment,and we are likely to attract a more diverse set of employees. 多元化的董事会成员向外部利益相关者发出了一个积极的 信息，即我们是一个健全的企业公民，我们重视各种来源 的意见。这可以帮助我们定位为一个对社会负责的组织， 不歧视，通过反映我们所服务的社会的多样性，我们将与 外部环境实现更好的社会联系，我们可能会吸引更多不同 的员工。 Improve investor relations Our shareholders are likely to be highly supportive of having a diverse board membership. Summary Performance in 20X3,both financial and non-financial has been disappointing overall. E- 12- 1.科目介绍 SBL《战略商业领袖》是 ACCA 于2018年9月考季首次引入的全新必修科目，取代了之前的P1《公司治理， 风险管理及职业操守》和P3《商业分析》课程。 SBL 科目帮助学员了解实际的商业环境，更加贴合现代商业和实务。 在SBL 考试中，考生需要展现出一个有能力的管理者在指导和管理企业时所需要的知识和技能。这就要求 考生能够灵活地应用在Applied Knowledge 阶段 (F 阶段前3科)以及 Applied Skills 阶段 (F 阶段后6 科)所学到的知识，并且能同时展现出优秀的职业技能(5 Professional skills),真正弱化从理论向实 务过渡的壁垒，在真实的商业世界里运筹帷幄。 2.近3年全球通过率 The Global Pass Rate of Strategic Business Leader(SBL) Dec/21 Mar/22 Jun/22 Sep/22 Dec/22 Mar/23 Jun/23 Sep/23 Dec/23 51% 50% 50% 49% 49% 50% 51% 50% 52% 《战略商业领袖》近3年全球通过率，详细数据可登陆ACCA 全球官网，输入“Pass rate ”查 询 高顿ACCA, 为学员赢得世界尊重 3 . Syllabus and Study Guide考试大纲与科目知识结构 Leadership(A) Governance(B)Strategy (C)ProfessionalSkills(I)Risk (D)Innovation,performanceexcellenceand changemanagement(H)Employabilityand technologyskills(J)Organisationalcontrol andaudit(F)Technologyand dataanalytics(E)Finance inplanningand decisionmaking(G) Leadership(A) Governance(B) Strategy (C) Professional Skills(I) Risk (D) Innovation, performance excellence and change management(H) Employability and technology skills(J) Organisational control and audit(F) Technology and data analytics(E) Finance in planning and decision making(G) SBL共包含10个相互关联的版块： Part A Leadership领导力 √ 如何做到并应用优秀的领导力和较高的道德水平； √ 如何在企业中营造并促进积极向上的企业文化和氛围； √ 如何在管理企业业绩表现和价值创造的同时，更好地实现企业整体目标。 Part B Governance 公司治理 √ 如何评估并改善公司治理的有效性； √ 如何构建并促进董事会制度，使其对广大利益相关者负责； √ 如何认知并践行企业社会责任。 Part C Strategy 战 略 √ 如何对企业做战略定位，评估其在外部环境中的竞争位置； √ 如何分析企业内部资源的有效性； √ 如 何 做 战 略 选 择 和 规 划 。 Part D Risk 风险 √ 如何分析并评估企业所面临的风险； √ 如何应对企业风险，并进行风险管理。 Part E Technology and Data analytics 科技与数据分析 √ 企业应如何选择合适的信息科技与数据分析系统； √ 如何应用这些科技帮助企业识别机遇和挑战； √ 如何保证企业的 IT 系统能够在安全的环境下有效运作。 Part F Organisational control and audit 企业内控和审计 √ 如何评估管理报告，内部控制和审计系统的有效性； √ 如何确保公司运营的合规性； √ 如何实现公司目标并确保公司资产的安全。 Part G Finance in planning and decision making 财务在计划和决策中的应用 √ 如何将Skills 阶段所学到的财务和管理知识应用到战略层面，战略计划，战略执行，和战略评估 Part H Enabling success and change management 推动成功和变革管理 √ 如何应用创新以及破坏性技术斩获成功； √ 如何不断提升组织业绩； √ 如何有效地推动并管理变革。 后面两个版块并非单独考核，将结合以上8个知识版块一起体现 Part I Professional skills 职业技能 √ C-Communication 沟通能力； √ Commercial acumen 商业敏锐度； √ A-Analysis 分析能力； √ S-Scepticism 职业质疑； √ E-Evaluation 评估能力 Part J Other employability and digital skills 就业能力和数字技术技能 √ 如何应用计算机技术有效地访问和操纵相关信息 √ 如何使用适当的工具有效地呈现数据和信息 高顿ACCA, 为学员赢得世界尊重 4.新旧考纲的主要变化 最新版教学大纲较上年度没有任何增减变动。 5.科目关联性 Strategic 1. 考试之前需要的准备知识 (Pre-SBL technical skills): 建议考生在学习SBL 之前，先关注以下知识： a.BT Business and Technology《商业与技术》 建议：董事会的构成、企业组织结构，学员务必做好复习工作 b.MA Management Accounting《管理会计》 建议：time series 时间序列分析、regression analysis 回归分析、variance 务必做好复习工作 c.PM Performance Management《业绩管理》 建议：budgeting 预算方法、performance measurement业绩评估、sensitivity decision tree 决策树、平衡积分卡，学员务必做好复习工作 d.FR Financial Reporting《财务报告》 analysis 差异分析，学员 analysis敏感性分析、 建议：财报数据分析，学员务必做好复习工作 e.AA Audit and Assurance《审计与鉴证》 建议：audit committee 审计委员会、internal audit 内审、审计风险，学员务必做好复习工作 f.FM Financial Management《财务管理》 建议：methods of funding融资方式、NPV、IRR、payback period,学员务必做好复习工作

2. 考试之前需要掌握的职业技能 (EPSM): EPSM(Ethics &Professional Skills Model) 道德与职业技能测试 EPSM 共九个单元 (units), 涉及商业环境中的重要职业技能，对学习并通过 SBL 课程有着非常大的支持 性作用，强烈建议考生在通过AS 阶段考试之后、学习SBL 之前完成这个模块! 官方数据显示，提前完成 EPSM 模块的学员在SP 阶段的考试通过率比未完成EPSM 模块的学员有明显差异。 考生可通过MY ACCA 进入 EPSM 模块。 ONLINE DELIVERY-PL 第 一 步 ：sign in MyACCA 高顿ACCA, 为学员赢得世界尊重 Sign in to MyACCA Insert your usemame and password below to access your secure personal MyACCA account. Satisfying these other stakeholders will have a positive effect on company earnings and shareholder returns.This is not a new concept,it was first published in 1984 by Edward Freeman,but it is hugely relevant today,with regards to an organisation's approach towards environmental and sustainability issues. Environmental and sustainability issues have become extremely important across many diverse industries and organisations worldwide,irrespective of the size and strategies of those organisations.Organisations that take positive actions and publicise these can achieve competitive advantage as a result.Organisations that are found to be lacking in this area can be at a disadvantage,leading to loss of market share and investor faith. Risks The risks arising in relation to environmental and sustainability issues can be substantial, including: Business risk - Environmental issues are changing industries,and those failing to predict the changes or act upon them,could find their business strategies become obsolete. Regulatory risk-whilst the ISO14000 family of standards is not mandatory,it can help organisations meet the minimum legal standards in their countries/industries.Meeting the standards can provide competitive advantage,and in some industries,failing to do so can be a disadvantage and cause reputation risk.Countries are also imposing their own regulations on specific industries,and these must be complied with if an organisation is to survive. Reputation risk -This can be caused by falling short of the expectations of stakeholders with regards to environmental issues.Organisations which appear to be acting sustainably but are found to overpay their actions towards the environment(such as ‘greenwashing'in the fashion industry,explained later in this article),can damage their reputation.To avoid this,joining the EU Eco-Management and Audit Scheme (EMAS), applicable worldwide,supports organisations in strengthening their credibility through third-party verification processes.Transparency of accurate and credible information is key to minimising reputation risk and Integrated Reporting<IR>can play a major role in this. 批注[1]:企业有义务对它的利益相关者负责，不仅仅是为 股东赚取回报，而且还要对股东之外的其他的利益相关者 负 责 。 满足其他利益相关者的利益将会对公司收益和股东回报起 到正向作用，这并不是一个新概念，早在1984年， Edward Freeman 就已经提出，只是放到今天更加相关，尤其是考 虑到企业如何对待环境和可持续问题。 那些采取正面措施、并且公开这些措施的企业能够因此获 得竞争优势。而在这方面缺乏的企业会导致市场份额的丢 失和投资者信心的丧失。 批注[2]:与环境和可持续问题相关的风险可以说是非常重 大的，包括如下风险： 批注[3]:企业风险：环境问题正在改变行业，那些不能够 预测或者不能够采取恰当措施的企业，可能会发现他们的 企业战略面临过时的风险。 批注[4]:监管风险：尽管 ISO14000 系列标准不是必须遵循 的，但是能够帮助企业满足国家或者行业的最低法律监管 要求。在某些行业，如果不能满足这些要求，可能会丧失 竞争优势并且带来声誉风险。国家层面也在针对具体行业 实施监管，如果企业想要生存，必然要遵循这些监管要求。 批注[5]:声誉风险： 某些企业看上去采取了可持续发展的 措施、但是被发现过度夸张，比如，在时尚行业的“洗绿”, 有可能损害企业的声誉。为了避免这一风险，可以通过假 如欧盟 EMAS 计划(适用于全球企业),通过第三方认证 加强可信度。提供正确的可信的信息，是减少声誉风险的 关键。IR报告在这方面发挥重大作用。 Operational risk -disruption to operations as a result of environmental effects.An example of this is the effect of severe droughts in Taiwan reducing the availability of computer chips,which require huge amounts ofwater in production. 批注[6]:经营风险：因为对环境的影响造成经营中断。 一 个例子就是台湾因为严重的干旱而减少计算机芯片的供 应，因为其生产需要大量的水资源。 Financial risk -the cost of rectifying environmental issues caused by the actions of the organisation,or the loss of revenue as a result of the reputation risk. 批注[7]:财务风险：企业行为带来的环境问题需要纠正， 因此发生的成本，或者因为声誉风险造成的收入减少。 But there are also opportunities for organisations to overcome these risks by taking a strong environmental stance and adapting their strategies and operations accordingly.Some industries have been in the spotlight for their impacts on the environment and the actions being taken to avoid this. The fashion industry-changing the approachWithin the fashion industry,many ‘fast fashion'organisations have received negative press because of how they operate,and their supply chain practices."Fast fashion'relates to cheap, disposable clothing that meets current trend requirements;the ‘fast'refers to how quickly designs can reach the shops at a low price to customers.Historically,‘fast fashion'companies have been accused of damaging the environment through the volume of clothing they produce,much of which is discarded after a short period. There is a waste of resources used in the production of that clothing.Many organisations in the fast fashion'industry are large,global conglomerates,owning many different fashion labels.If these organisations all sell fashion which is expected to be worn for one season or occasion only,this has a significant environmental impact.This impact includes manufacturing pollution,inefficient use of raw materials and waste in production,and associated waste disposal issues,such as excessive landfill.The extent of dissatisfaction of many stakeholders towards ‘fast fashion’has led to organisations such as Greenpeace taking a stance against the industry,as well as a United Nations initiative,the UN Alliance for Sustainable Fashion.In the past,it could have been argued that if customers continue to buy,and profits continue to be made for the shareholders,these 'fast fashion'companies do not need to take note of the environmental and sustainability issues surrounding them.But many of these organisations have received negative press coverage due to their environmental and sustainability impact and they are now going to great lengths to undertake,and advertise,their actions towards sustainable business.Research has shown(eg Roozen and Raedts1,2020)that negative press can have a significant negative impact on organisations within the ‘fast fashion'industry,and that the power of negative press is greater than positive pressTherefore,it is essential that organisations take action to deal with the sustainability issues surrounding ‘fast fashion'.Several ‘fast fashion'brands have introduced recycling schemes,where customers return unwanted clothing to recycling locations such as labelled bins in stores. A key tool in communicating the actions organisation take to improve sustainability is the use of integrated reporting <IR>to advertise to stakeholders what the issues are and the actions being taken.The major fashion labels issue sustainability reports or include environmental and sustainability issues within their published annual reports.These often include targets, making them accountable to stakeholders for achieving these targets. There are still issues around greenwashing.Greewashing is the term used to describe when a company promotes itself as environmentally friendly to gain support from stakeholders but puts more effort into marketing this than actually taking positive actions. However,these organisations continue to face heavy criticism from pressure groups and may eventually be forced to take stronger,more credible,actions if they wish to continue with their current business successes. Governments are supporting ethical fashion initiatives.In 2019,France launched a 'Paris Good Fashion'initiative which aims to help make the fashion industry greener.Chinese and Indian governments,amongst others,have undertaken similar initiatives.It would seem that failure to act on sustainability issues is likely to lead to the erosion of the large market shares achieved by the large fashion brands existing today.This is due to pressure from government, competitors,consumers as well as from institutional investors and shareholders,worried about future returns and negative press. The motor industry-changing technologies The motor industry faces challenges due to stakeholder concern for the environment,the sustainability of raw materials and political ultimatums.Organisations have no choice but to change if they wish to remain in this industry.The major threat could be perceived to be political.The European Green Deal,for example,forces the industry to consider greener solutions,and the UK,for instance,has banned the sale of new petrol and diesel cars from 2030. Whilst the public sees the changes,through the release of hybrid and electric cars into the market,what is not obvious from the outside are the changes in production processes,supply chains and the workforce skills needed to develop more sustainable methods of manufacture. Changing the nature of industries Threat of new entrants We know from Porter's Five-Forces model,that barriers to entry that reduce the threat of new entrants have a significant influence on both the market share and the profitability of organisations existing within an industry.The increasing importance of sustainability has broken down some of these barriers in many industries,including those mentioned in this article. The focus on sustainability in the fashion industry has opened the door for many smaller The focus on sustainability in the fashion industry has opened the door for many smaller companies to enter the market and be successful.In the past,there were high barriers to entry in the form of customer loyalty and existing operators in the industry benefitted from economies of scale.But now ‘slow fashion'firms are successfully entering the industry and are seeing an unprecedented rise in their success.'Slow fashion'firms are generally much smaller and focus on responsible sourcing of quality raw materials,environmentally friendly manufacturing methods,and longevity of product life.Garments are often more expensive but 'classic'in that they can be worn for longer,rather than follow the season or fast changing fashion trends.'Slow fashion'firms can charge a higher price per product and gain customer loyalty. In the motor industry,which has traditionally had extremely high barriers to entry,new technologies provide opportunities for new players.For example,new entrants have successfully entered the market with a mission to accelerate the world's transition to sustainable energy.Added to this threat are the companies developing self-driving cars and there are likely to be further new entrants from outside the industry,who have developed sustainable and digital technologies and see an opportunity to use them in the motor industry. Threat of substitutes It is not only large multi-national organisations that are affected.The trend towards sustainability has created new,substitute industries,predominantly occupied by smaller organisations.Many of these organisations only exist because of their environmental or sustainable credentials. Examples of these smaller organisations include those which offer refil products; Examples of these smaller organisations include those which offer refil products; packaging-free or loose products The four lines of defence framework helps organisations analyse the overall strength of their control,supervision and review processes.It enables management to ascertain the level ofcomfort each stage provides and also determine what should be included at later stages to remedy limitations of earlier controls. Assurance mapping,linking principal risks to control processes and key performance indicators,and seeing what assurance is provided against the risks materialising,can be an important part of the four lines of defence. important part of the four lines of defence. Assurance mapping and the four lines of defence are included in the SBL syllabus. First line of defence:Control frameworks and day-to-day controls The first line of defence includes the overall risk management systems and contro frameworks.It also incorporates the controls over operational processes and outputs.This stage includes controls over day-to-day transactions and periodic controls,for example cut-off procedures at the month or year-end,as well as procedures such as quality control if they are a regular part of operations.Day-to-day management supervision,for example approval of large transactions,is also part of this stage. Assurance is given by the knowledge and commitment of the staff operating the controls The benefits provided by the first line of defence are that the staff operating the controls know the business and workflow,and may be aware of where controls are potentially weak. Building more controls in at this stage can also mean that mistakes are less likely to happen and can be more easily corrected.Strong day-to-day controls relating to information provision should mean that the information provided in external reports and to external auditors is likely to be more reliable. The main weakness of the first line of defence is lack of independence,that the controls are being implemented by the same staff who are responsible for the operations to which the controls relate.They are effectively certifying their own work(self-review). The main weakness of the first line of defence is lack of independence,that the controls are being implemented by the same staff who are responsible for the operations to which the controls relate.They are effectively certifying their own work(self-review). 批注[1]:四道防线风险管理架构 Four lines of defence 可 以帮助组织分析控制、监管以及审核的强度，使得管理层 确保每一个阶段风险管控带来的舒适度、从而决定在后一 阶段应采取怎样的措施以修正前一阶段的局限性。 批注[2]:Assurance mapping 是把主要风险与控制流程和 KPI连接起来，可以明确针对每一个即将爆发的风险有什么 样的控制措施来防范，assurance mapping 是四道防线的重 要组成部分。 批注[3]:第一道防线：控制框架与日常控制 包括整体的风险管理系统与控制框架。 包括对日常交易的控制、也包括月底或者年末做的截止测 试。 日常监管，比如，某个大型交易的批准，也属于第一道防 线。 批注[4]:第一道防线的好处是执行控制措施的员工对业务 与流程非常了解，而且非常清楚哪里容易出现问题。在这 个阶段实施越多的控制，也意味着出现错误的可能性越少、 并且越容易纠正。 批注[5]:第一道防线的确定是缺乏独立性。实施控制的人 员同时也是要对业务负责的人员。实际上是自己给自己开 证明(自我审核self-review)。 Second line of deence:Management review Second line of deence:Management review 2 The second line of defence relates to review by management or specialists that is separate from day-to-day operations.It includes risk and compliance reviews,financial controls over operational departments and oversight of operations by the board.It can also include quality control reviews that are additional to day-to-day quality checks,for example one-off checking of a range of items where there have been customer complaints. The second line of defence introduces a degree of independence and objectivity,as the reviewers are not staff and managers who are operationally responsible for the areas being reviewed The second line of defence introduces a degree of independence and objectivity,as the reviewers are not staff and managers who are operationally responsible for the areas being reviewed However,the reviewers are still part of the same management team,working with those being reviewed.Other problems at this stage are that the effectiveness of the reviews depends upon the reviewers'expertise.Ensuring that the reviews have a clear purpose and that the purpose is reflected in selecting what is reviewed may also prove difficult. Third line of defence:Internal audit Internal audit is the third line of defence.The effectiveness of internal audit will depend on the extent of its terms of reference.These could be wide-ranging,covering operational efficiency and effectiveness,safeguarding of assets and reliability of reporting.As well as looking at systems overall,internal audit can also focus on specific risks,particularly risks which the first two lines of deence may not have completely counteredInternal audit's role is also likely to be valuable if there are changes affecting the first two lines of defence,or changes in organisational structures,reporting processes and information systems.The effectiveness of internal audit is also determined by the reliability of its risk assessment and linkage between the risk assessment and work done.Assurance mapping(discussed below) ca n be particularly useful for internal audit. Internal audit work has the significant benefit of being done by staff who are independent and separate from line management,who are not involved in operational work and whose Internal audit work has the significant benefit of being done by staff who are independent and separate from line management,who are not involved in operational work and whose remuneration is not dependent upon operational results. Internal auditors'independence can be strengthened by being able to report directly to the board and audit committee,and being able to discuss issues with the board and audit committee without operational management being present. 批注[6]:第二道防线：管理层审核 由独立于日常业务的管理层或者专门人士审核。 包括风险与合规审核、对业务部门的财务控制、以及董事 会对经营的监管。也包括质量控制审核，比如，针对客户 投诉，对一系列业务项目进行一次性审核。 批注[7]:第二道风险引入一定程度的独立性和客观性。 批注[8]:但是，审核者仍然是管理团队的一份子，与被审 核者一同工作。除此之外，审核的效果也取决于审核者的 专业程度。而且，要想确保有一个非常明确的审核目标、 并且通过选择被审核业务或项目来反映审核目标，也是非 常困难的。 批注[9]:第三道防线：内审 内审的效果取决于内审范围，包括经营效果和效率、资产 保全、以及报告是否可以依赖(真实公允)。 内审也可以关注具体的风险，尤其是前两道防线没有完全 抵消的风险。 批注[10]: Assurance mapping 在内审中发挥非常大的作 用。 批注[11]:内审工作如果是由独立于各级管理人员的内部 审计师来做，能够带来重大益处，他们不会卷入经营活动， 而且他们的收入也不依赖于经营结果。 内部审计师的独立性可以通过向董事会和审计委员会直接 汇报工作而加强，而且他们能够在经营管理人员不在场的 情况下与董事会和审计委员会探讨问题。 Fourth line of defence:External auditAssurance mapping Fourth line of defence:External audit Assurance mapping However,internal audit stillhas the drawback of the earlier stage that its staff are employees. However,internal audit stillhas the drawback of the earlier stage that its staff are employees. They can never therefore be completely independent of the organisation and may be influenced by internal politics.Internal factors may also limit the scope of internal audit's work.Its effectiveness willbe weakened if there are‘no-go'areas which internal audit cannot review.Whilst internal audit can make recommendations,it cannot ensure the recommendations are carried out,due to internal audit staff not being involved in operations. Internal audit effectiveness therefore depends on the willingness of the board and senior management to insist that internal audit recommendations are implemented. External audit can look at any aspects of a business's processes and results that are relevant to the audit assignment. External audit's main advantage is the impartial assurance it provides,as external staff are not External audit's main advantage is the impartial assurance it provides,as external staff are not employees of the organisation.As such they are seen to be independent.External auditors can also bring a wider perspective to their work and recommendations,based on their knowledge of other organisations. External auditors may however be disadvantaged by the lack of knowledge they have of the organisation,resulting from only seeing it once a year as opposed to being continuously employed in it.External audit's work will also concentrate on providing sufficient assurance to give a reliable audit report on the financial statements.This will mean a focus on areas connected with the accounting systems and less emphasis on other areas of the risk and control systems.Additional work to the audit work on the financial statements may be required to give assurance on the other areas. Assurance mapping links with the four lines of defence approach by considering individual risks and the extent to which each risk has been mitigated by each of the four lines of defence. It also links the risks with key performance indicators(KPIs)and other means of reporting, thus helping the organisation report reliably on risks and risk management. 批注[12]:但是，内审仍然有前两道防线的缺点，即内审人 Advantages of assurance mapping Assurance mapping can provide a clear link between risks which require management and the elements of control systems used to manage them.It enables the organisation to see if there Assurance mapping can provide a clear link between risks which require management and the elements of control systems used to manage them.It enables the organisation to see if there are any risks where there is limited assurance that controls are effectively operating.Having the information provided by assurance mapping enables stronger and more certain reporting on internal control.It also enables the organisation to manage controls more efficiently and effectively,directing staff's work so that gaps in control are filled and overlap of staff's responsibilities are avoided.The assessment of the risk reduction and the assurance given from the first two lines of defence enables the organisation to see what assurance is required from the internal audit function and therefore decide its resourcing and scope of work. Conclusion This article has discussed how assurance mapping can link with the four lines of defence to show clearly what is being done to manage risks and the assurance given by the controls and This article has discussed how assurance mapping can link with the four lines of defence to show clearly what is being done to manage risks and the assurance given by the controls and reviews undertaken.This systematic approach can give directors comfort that they are discharging their responsibilities to design and operate effective risk management and control systems and use the organisation's resources effectively.It also enables them to report,with more certainty,to external stakeholders on internal controls,strengthening stakeholders' confidence. Introduction Introduction Both Strategic Business Leader(SBL)and Advanced Performance Management (APM)require candidates to be able to establish key performance indicators and critical success factors. A surprising number of candidates do not feel comfortable with these terms,and this article is aimed at explaining and illustrating these concepts.In particular,it willexplain what is meant by. ·Performance ·Objectives ·Critical success factors ·Performance indicators ·Key perfomance indicators. Performance This can be defined as: A task or operaton seen in terms of how successfuly it is performed' Organisations iffer greatly in which aspects of their behaviour and results contitute good performance.For example, their aim could be to make profits,to increase the share price,to cure patients in a hospital,or to clear household rubbish.The concept of performance'is very relevant to both SBL and APM.SBL looks at how organisations can make decisions that improve their strategic performance and APM is focused on how organisations evaluate their performance. The primary required tasks are often found in the organisation's mission statement as it is there that the organisation's purpose should be defined.These are called'primary requred tasks'because although the primary task of a profit-seeking business is to make profits,this rests on other subsidiarytasks such as good design,low cost per unit, quality,fexibility,successful marketing and so on.Many of these are non-financial achievements. Some aspects of performance are 'nice to have'but others will be critical success factors.For example,the standard of an airine's meals and entertainment systems wil rnk after punctuality,reliability and safety,all of which are likely to be critical to the airline's success. 批注[1]: SBL和 APM 都要求考生掌握KPI 和 CSF, 但是很多 考生掌握不好，本文解释如下概念。 批注[2]:绩效： 概 念：一个任务或运行有多成功。 组织在行为和结果的哪些方面构成良好绩效方面存在很大
