Written by a member of the Strategic Business Leader examining team 批注[21]:负责任的领导不能被认为是战略商业领袖角色 的一个独立领域；它与战略规划、文化、伦理、可持续性以 及公共价值密切相关。企业不应再认为公共价值是公共部 门的专属领域；所有企业都有责任为社会的未来造福，企业 领导人的角色就是承担这些责任。 批注[22]:通过成为负责任的领导者，我们都可以在社会中 发挥积极的作用。因此，企业将蓬勃发展，利益相关者将 以多种方式受益。会计师通常在他们工作的企业中担任领 导角色，因此应该举例说明负责任的领导者的特征，并鼓 励其他人这样做。 批注[23]:会计师的一个关键作用是确保公司不只是专注 于财务目标，而是提出和实施其他关键指标，并提供负责 任决策的积极影响的证据。综合报告可以帮助实现这一点， 重要的是要认识到并非所有负责任领导的例子都单独具有 可衡量的影响，但累积起来，它有助于更好的企业文化和 绩 效 。 Performance indicators This article explains and illustrates key performance indicators and critical success factors. Introduction Both Strategic Business Leader(SBL)and Advanced Performance Management(APM)require candidates to be able to establish key performance indicators and critical success factors. A surprising number of candidates do not feel comfortable with these terms,and this article is aimed at explaining and illustrating these conceptsIn particularit will explain what is meant by: ·Objectives ● Critical success factors ● Performance indicators ·Key performance indicators Performance This can be defined as: 'A task or operation seen in terms of how successfully it is performed' Organisations differ greatly in which aspects of their behaviour and results constitute good performance.For example, their aim could be to make profits,to increase the share price,to cure patients in a hospital,or to clear household rubbish.The concept of'performance'is very relevant to both SBL and APM.SBL looks at how organisations can make decisions that improve their strategic performance and APM is focused on how organisations evaluate their performance. The primary required tasks are often found in the organisation's mission statement as it is there that the organisation's purpose should be defined.These are called'primary required tasks'because although the primary task of a profit-seeking business is to make profits,this rests on other subsidiary tasks such as good design,low cost per unit, quality,flexibility,successful marketing and so on.Many of these are non-financial achievements. Some aspects of performance are 'nice to have'but others will be critical success factors.For example,the standard of an airline's meals and entertainment systems will rank after punctuality,reliability and safety,all of which are likely to be critical to the airline's success. Objectives Objectives are simply targets that an organisation sets out to achieve.They are elements of the mission that have been quantified and are the basis for deciding appropriate performance measures and indicators.There islittle point 批注[1]: SBL 和 APM 都要求考生掌握 KPI和 CSF, 但是很多 考生掌握不好，本文解释如下概念。 批注[2]:绩效： 概念： 一个任务或运行有多成功。 组织在行为和结果的哪些方面构成良好绩效方面存在很大 差 异 。例如，他们的目标可能是盈利，提高股价，治疗医 院的病人，或清理家庭垃圾。“绩效”的概念与SBL和 APM 都非常相关。 SBL 侧重于组织如何做出提高战略绩效的决 策，而APM 则侧重于组织如何评估其绩效。 在使命陈述中可以找到组织的主要任务，它定义了组织的 目的。这些被称为“主要必要任务”,因为尽管 一个商业 企业的主要任务是盈利，但这取决于其他附属任务，如良 好的设计、低单位成本、质量、灵活性、成功的营销等等。 其中很多都是非经济成就。 某些方面的表现“很好”,但有些方面将是CSF。例如，航 空公司的餐饮和娱乐系统的标准将排在准点率、可靠性和 安全性之后，所有这些都可能对航空公司的成功至关重要。 批注[3]:目标： 目标是一个组织设定要实现的目标。它们是已量化的 任 务 的组成部分，如果您不知道结果是否令人满意，也不能决 定是否需要改变绩效，当前的评估就没什么意义。 组织将创建一个目标层次结构，其中包括影响整个组织的 公司目标和影响组织内单个业务单位的单位目标。 目标也会被分为主要目标和次要目标，例如， 一个组织可 能会为自己设定一个利润增长的主要目标，但随后需要制 定战略以确保实现这一主要目标。这就需要次要目标，例 如提高产品质量或更有效地利用资源。 measuring something if you do not know whether the result is satisfactory and cannot decide if performance needs to change.Organisations will create a hierarchy of objectives which will include corporate objectives which affect the organisation as a whole and unit objectives which will ffect individual business units within the organisation.Even here objectives will be categorised as primary and secondary,for example an organisation might set itself a primary objective of growth in profits but will then need to develop strategies to ensure this primary objective is achieved.This is where secondary objectives are needed,for example to improve product quality or to make more efficient use of resources. Objectives often follow the SMART rule.They should be: Specific:there is little point in setting an objective for a company to improve its inventory.What does that mean?It could mean that stock-outs should be less frequent,or average stock holdings should be lower,or the inventory will be held in better conditions to reduce wastage. Measurable:if you can't measure something you will e at a loss as to how to control it.Some aspects of performance might be difficult to measure,but efforts must be made.Customer satisfaction is important to most businesses and indications could be obtained by arranging customer surveys,repeat business and so on. Achievable/agreed/accepted:objectives are achieved by people and those people must accept and agree that the objectives are achievable and important. Relevant:relevant to the organisation and the person to whom the objectives are givenIt is important that people understand how achieving an objective will help organisational success.If this connection isn't clear,employees will begin to feel that the objective is simply a cynical exercise of management power.The person to whom the objective is given must also feel that they can affect its achievement. Time-imited:allobjectives have to be achieved within a specified time period otherwise procrastination will rule. Critical success factors A critical success factor(CSF)can be defined as: An area where an organisation must perform well f it is to succeed.' Alternatively, Johnson,Scholes &Whittington defined CSFs as: Those product features that are particularly valued by a group of customers,and,therefore,where the organisation must excel to outperform the competition.' This definition is more complex than the first,but itis more useful because it makes the organisation look towards its customers(or users)and recognises that their opinion of excellence is more important and reliable than internally generated opinions.If an organisation doesn't deliver what its customers,clients,patients,citizens or students value,it is failing. 批注[4]:目标通常遵循SMART规则。它们应该是： S 具体的：单说要改善库存没有任何意义，要具体说清是库 存缺货的频率应该降低，或者平均库存持有量应该降低， 或者库存将保持在更好的条件下以减少浪费。 M 可衡量的：不能衡量的事情没法有效控制。难以衡量的地 方也必须做出努力。客户满意度对大多数企业来说都很重 要，可以通过安排客户调查、重复业务等来获得指标。 A可实现的/同意的/可接受的：目标是由人实现的，这些人 必须接受并同意目标是可实现的和重要的。 R相关的：与组织和被赋予目标的人相关。人们要理解实现 目标如何有助于组织的成功。如果这种联系不明确，员工 就会开始觉得这个目标只是滥用职权。被给予目标的人也 必须感到他们能够影响目标的实现。 T 时间限制：所有的目标都必须在规定的时间内完成，否则 就会拖延。 批注[5]: CSF 定义：一个组织要想成功，就必须在这个领域 表现出色。 另一种定义为："那些产品特性特别受到一群客户的重视， 因此，企业的价值所在必须出类拔萃才能超越竞争对手。” 这个定义比第一个更复杂，但它更有用，因为它使组织正 视其客户(或用户),认识到他们对卓越的看法比内部产生的 意见更重要、更可靠。如果一个组织不能提供它的客户、 病人、市民或学生所看重的东西，那它就是失败的。 Performance indicators and key performance indicators Performance indicators(or performance measures)are methods used to assess performance.For example: In profit-seeking organisations: Profit Earnings per share Retum on capital employed In not-for-profit organisations: Exam grades(a school) Waiting times for hospital admission (a health service) Condition of roads(a local government highways department) Particularly in profit-seeking organisations,the prime financial performance indicators allow performance to be measured but they say little about how that performance has been achieved.So,high profits will depend on a combination of good sales volumes,adequate prices and sufficiently low costs.If high profits can only be achieved by a satisfactory combination of volume,price and cost,then those factors should be measured also and will need to be compared to standards and budgets. Similar effects are found in not-for-profit organisations.For example,in a school,a CSF might be that a pupil leaves with good standards of literacy.But that mght depend on pupil-teacher ratios,pupils'attendance and the experience of the teachers.Ií these factors contribute to good performance,they need to be measured and monitored. Just as CSFs are more importan than other aspects of performance,not all performance indicators are created equal. The performance indicators that measure the most important aspects of performance are the key performance indicators(KPls). To a large extent,KPls measure how well CSFs are achieved;other performance indicators measure how wellother aspects of performance are achieved There are a number of potential pitfalls in the design o performance indicators and measurement systems: There are a number of potential pitfalls in the design o performance indicators and measurement systems: Not enough performance measures are set Often,directo rs and employees will be judged on the results of performance measures.It has been said that 'Whatever gets measured gets done'and employees wil tend to concentrate on achieving the required performance where it is measured.The corollary is that Whatever doesn't get measured doesn't get done'and the danger is that employees willgnore areas of behaviour and performance which are not assessed. Too many performance indicators This occurs especiall where performance measures are not ranked byimportance and none have been identified as KPls.Performance indicators have to be measured,calculated and reported to management,and discrepancies must be explained or excuses invented.Too many measures can divert time from more important tasks and there is a danger that employees concentrate on the easier but more trivial measures than on the more difficult but vital targets. 批注[6]:在商业组织中，主要的财务绩效指标可以衡量业 绩，但它们很少说明业绩是如何实现的。因此，高利润将 取决于良好的销量、适当的价格和足够低的成本。如果高 利润只能通过令人满意的数量、价格和成本的结合来实现， 那么这些因素也应该加以衡量，并将需要与标准和预算进 行比较。 在非营利组织中也有类似的影响。例如，在一所学校里， CSF 可能是指学生的读写能力达到了良好的标准。但这可能 取决于学生与教师的比例、学生的出勤率和教师的经验。 如果这些因素有助于良好的绩效，就需要对它们进行测量 和监控。 并不是所有的绩效指标都是一样的，KPI 是关键绩效指标。 在很大程度上，KPls 衡量CSFs的实现情况，其他绩效指标 衡量的是其他方面的绩效实现情况。 批注[7]:以下是一些设置绩效指标的主要缺陷： 1.没有设定足够的绩效指标 通常，董事和员工将根据绩效衡量的结果进行考核。有人 说，“任何被衡量的事情都会被完成”,员工会倾向于在 被衡量的地方专注于实现所需的绩效。其必然结果是“没 有被衡量的事情就不会被完成”,危险在于员工会忽视那 些没有被评估的行为和表现领域。 2.太多的绩效指标 这种情况尤其发生在绩效指标没有按重要性进行排名，也 没有被确定为KPls 的情况下。绩效指标必须进行测量、计 算并报告给管理层，不一致的地方必须加以解释。太多的 绩效评估会分散更重要任务的时间，而且员工可能会把精 力集中在更容易但更琐碎的措施上，而不是更困难但更重 要的目标上。 The wrong performance measures An example of this would be applying strict cost measures in an organisation where luxury products and services are sold(a differentiation strategy).This is likely to detract from the organisation's strategic success. Too tight/too loose performance measures Performance indicators that are too diffcult to attain can lead to a loss of employee motivation and promote dysfunctional behaviours such as gaming and the misrepresentation of data.Performance measures that are too loose can pull down performance. Benchmarking can help to avoid his.Internal benchmarking generally sets measures based on previous period's measures or set measures with respect to other branches or divisionsHowever these internal benchmarks can lead to complacency as many organisations have to compete with others and benchmarks should be aligned to competitors'performance. 'Hit and run'performance indicators This means that a performance indicator is set and then it is assumed that things will look after themselves. Performance indicators need a management framework they are to be at all effective. Performance measures-a practical framework Expanding on the last point,above,to establish a performance measurement system,something like the following is needed for each measure:

1. A meaningful title of the measure What is its purpose and how does that purpose relate to strategic success? What other performance measures might be affected by this one,how are they affected and how are conflicts to be resolved? Who will be held responsible for it?
5. What is the source data,who is responsible for its supply,how is it measured and how is the measure calculated?
6. What investigations and explanations are required and who is responsible?
7. What target is set and how has that target been determined?
8. How often should the target be updated?
9. How often is the measure reported on?

10. Reporting and action? indicators in the SBL and APM syllabus Performance indicators are relevant to the following models and theories: Mission statements: these define the important aspects of performance that sum up the purpose of the organisation. Stakeholder
explanation_zh: recognises that different stakeholders have different views on what constitutes good performance.Sometimes what stakeholders want is different to what the mission statement suggests as the purpose of the organisation.This can be a particular problem when the stakeholders are key-players. Generic strategies:the main generic strategies to achieve competitive advantage are cost leadership and ifferentiation.If a company's success depends on being a cost leader(a CSF)then it must carefully monitor all its costs to achieve the leadership position.The company will therefore make use of performance indicators relating to cost and efficiency.If a company has chosen differentiation as its path to success,then it must ensure that it is offering enhanced products and services and must establish measures of these. Value chain:a value chain sets out an organisation's activities and enquires as to how the organisation can make profits:where is value added?For example,value might be added by promising fantastic quality.If so,that would be a CSF and a key performance indicator would the rate occurrence of bad units. Boston consulting group grid:this model uses relative market share and market growth to suggest what should be done with products or subsidiaries.In SBLif a company identifies a product as a problem child'BCG says that the appropriate action for the company is either to divest itself of that product or to invest to grow the product towards a 'star’position on the grid. This requires money to be spent on promotion,product enhancement,especially attractive pricing and perhaps investment in new,efficient equipment. PESTEL and Porter's five forces: both the macro-environment and competitive environment change continuously. Organisations have to keep these under review and react to the changes so that performance is sustained or improved.For example,if laws were introduced which stated that suppliers should be paid within a maximum of 60 days,then a performance measure will be needed to encourage and monitor the attainment of this target. Product life cycle:different performance measures are required at different stages of the life cycle.In the early days of a product's life,it is important to reach a successful growth trajectory and to stay ahead of would-be copycats.At the maturity stage,where there is great competition and the market is no longer growing,performance will depend on low costs per unit and maintaining market share to enjoy economies of scale. Company structure: different structures inevitably affect both performance and its management.For example,as businesses become larger many choose a divisionalised structure to allow specialisation in different parts of the 批注[10]: PI与以下理论相关： 使命宣言：绩效的重要方面，总结了组织的目的。 利益相关者分析：到不同的利益相关者对什么是良好绩效 有不同的看法。有时，利益相关者想要的与使命宣言所暗 示的组织目的是不同的。当涉众是关键角色时，这可能是 一个特别的问题。 一般战略：主要有成本领先和差异化战略，如果一家公司的 成功取决于成为成本领先(CSF), 那么它必须仔细监控其所 有成本以实现领导地位。因此，公司将使用与成本和效率 有关的绩效指标。如果一家公司选择差异化作为其成功之 路，那么它必须确保它提供增强的产品和服务，并且必须 建立这些措施。 价 值 链 ：价值链列出了组织的活动，并询问 了组织如何赚取利润：增值在哪里?例如，可以通过承诺出色 的质量来增加价值。如果是这样，这将是一个CSF 和 一 个 关键绩效指标，即不良品的发生率。 BCG 网 格：该模型使用相对市场份额和市场增长来建议如何 处理产品或子公司。在SBL中，如果一家公司将某产品认 定为“问题儿童”,应当要么剥离该产品，要么投资使该 产品在网格中成为“明星”。这就需要把钱花在促销、产 品改进上，尤其是有吸引力的定价，也许还需要投资于新 的高效设备。 PESTEL 和 Porter 五力：宏观环境和竞争环境都 是不断变化的。组织必须不断检讨这些指标，并对变化作 出反应，以维持或改善业绩。例如，如果法律规定供应商 应在最多60天内付款，那么就需要一项绩效措施来鼓励和 监测这一指标的实现情况。 产品生命周期：不同阶段需要不同的绩效评估。在产品生命 的早期，重要的是要达到一个成功的增长轨迹，并保持领 先于潜在的模仿者。在竞争激烈，市场不再增长的成熟阶 段，业绩将依靠低单位成本和保持市场份额来享受规模经 济 。 公司架构： 公司变大可能会采取事业部结构，使得不同的 部门可以专注于自己的业务单元。可以用ROI或 RI 等 评 估 事业部的绩效。 business:manufacturing/selling,European market/Asian market/North American market,product type A/product type B.Divisional performance measures,such as return on investment and residual income,then become relevant. Information technology (IT):new technologies will influence performance and could help to more effectively Information technology (IT):new technologies will influence performance and could help to more effectively measure performance.However,remember that sophisticated new technology does not guarantee better performance as costs can easily outweigh benefits.If IT is vital to a business,then downtime and query response time become relevanf as might a measure of system usability. Human resource management:what type of people should be recruited,and how are they to be motivated, appraised and rewarded to maximise the chance of good organisational performance?Performance measures are needed,for example,to monitor the effectiveness of training,job performance,job satisfaction,recruitment and retention.In addition,considerable effort has to be given to considering how employees'remuneration should be linked to performance . Fitzgerald and Moon building blocks The APM syllabus mentions three specific approaches or models: Balanced scorecard Performance pyramid Fitzgerald's and Moon's building blocks The balanced scorecard approach is probably the best known,but all seek to ensure that the net is thrown wide when designing performance measures for organisations so that factors such as quality,innovation,flexibility,stakeholder performance,and delivery and cycle time are listed as being important aspects of performance.Whenever an aspect of performance is important then a performance measure should be designed and used. The Fitzgerald and Moon model is worth a specific mention here as it is the only model which explicitly links performance measures to the individuals responsible for the performance. The model first sets out the dimensions(split into results and determinants)where key performance indicators should be established.You will see there is a mix of financial and non-financial,and both quantitative and qualitative: Results Financial performance Competitive performance Determinants Quality Flexibility Resource utilisation Innovation The model then suggests standards for KPls: Ownership:refers to the idea that KPls will be taken more seriously if staff have a say in setting targets. Staff will be more committed and will better understand why that KPI is needed. 批注[11]: 信息技术(IT):新技术将影响绩效，并有助于更有 效地衡量绩效。然而，复杂的新技术并不能保证更好的性 能，因为成本很容易超过收益。如果IT 对业务至关重要， 那么停机时间和查询响应时间就与系统使用的评估相关。 人力资源管理：应该招聘什么类型的人，如何激励、评估和 奖励他们，以最大限度地提高良好组织绩效的机会?例如， 需要采取业绩措施来监测培训、工作业绩、工作满意度、 征聘和保留的效力。此外必须努力考虑如何将雇员的薪酬 与业绩挂钩。 批注[12]: APM 大纲提到了三种具体的方法或模型： · 平衡计分卡 · 业绩金字塔 · 砖块模型 平衡计分卡方法可能是最著名的，但所有方法都力求确保 在为组织设计绩效衡量标准时，将网撒得更广，以便将质 量、创新、灵活性、利益相关者绩效、交付和周期时间等 因素列为绩效的重要方面。哪方面行为重要，哪里就应该 评估。 砖块模型是唯一一个将绩效指标与负责绩效的个人明确联 系起来的模型。该模型首先列出了应该建立 KPI的维度(分 为结果和决定因素)。这是财务和非金融的混合，定量和定 性都有。 批注[13]: KPI的标准： 所有：确保员工认同 KPI的 标 准 可实现：不可实现的标准会降低士气 公平：每个人的挑战难度是一样的，不可控的事情不应该 被 罚 Achievability:if KPls are frequently and obviously not achievable then motivation is harmed.Why would staff put in extra effort to try to achieve a target(and bonus)if they believe failure is inevitable. Fairness:everyone should be set similarly challenging objectives and it is essential that allowance should be made for uncontrollable events.Managers should not be penalised for events hat are completely outside everyone's control (for example,a natural disaster)or which is someone else's fault. The model then suggests how employee rewards should be set up to encourage employees to achieve the KPI The model then suggests how employee rewards should be set up to encourage employees to achieve the KPI targets Clarity:exactly how does performance translate into a reward? Motivation:the reward must be both desirable and must be perceived as achievableif it is to be motivating. Controllable:achievement of the KPI giving rise to the reward should be something the manager can influence and control. 批注[14]:实现 KPI的员工如何奖励： 明确：奖励规则清晰 激励：奖励够吸引人 可控：管理者可以影响或控制KPI
